================
File: ui/src/components/ui.tsx
================
  1: import { clsx } from 'clsx';
  2: import type { ReactNode } from 'react';
  3: interface SidebarItemProps {
  4:   icon: ReactNode;
  5:   label: string;
  6:   active?: boolean;
  7:   onClick?: () => void;
  8: }
  9: export function SidebarItem({ icon, label, active, onClick }: SidebarItemProps) {
 10:   return (
 11:     <button
 12:       onClick={onClick}
 13:       className={clsx(
 14:         'w-full flex items-center gap-3 px-3 py-2.5 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium transition-all duration-200',
 15:         active
 16:           ? 'bg-orange-500/10 text-orange-400 shadow-sm shadow-orange-900/10 border border-orange-500/10'
 17:           : 'text-slate-400 hover:bg-slate-800 hover:text-slate-200'
 18:       )}
 19:     >
 20:       {icon}
 21:       {label}
 22:     </button>
 23:   );
 24: }
 25: interface ButtonProps {
 26:   children: ReactNode;
 27:   variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost' | 'danger';
 28:   size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg';
 29:   disabled?: boolean;
 30:   onClick?: () => void;
 31:   className?: string;
 32: }
 33: export function Button({ children, variant = 'primary', size = 'md', disabled, onClick, className }: ButtonProps) {
 34:   return (
 35:     <button
 36:       onClick={onClick}
 37:       disabled={disabled}
 38:       className={clsx(
 39:         'flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg font-medium transition-all duration-200',
 40:         {
 41:           'bg-orange-600 hover:bg-orange-700 text-white': variant === 'primary',
 42:           'bg-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-600 text-slate-200': variant === 'secondary',
 43:           'text-slate-400 hover:text-white hover:bg-slate-800': variant === 'ghost',
 44:           'bg-red-600 hover:bg-red-700 text-white': variant === 'danger',
 45:           'px-3 py-1.5 text-sm': size === 'sm',
 46:           'px-4 py-2 text-sm': size === 'md',
 47:           'px-6 py-3 text-base': size === 'lg',
 48:         },
 49:         disabled && 'opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed',
 50:         className
 51:       )}
 52:     >
 53:       {children}
 54:     </button>
 55:   );
 56: }
 57: interface CardProps {
 58:   children: ReactNode;
 59:   className?: string;
 60:   hover?: boolean;
 61: }
 62: export function Card({ children, className, hover }: CardProps) {
 63:   return (
 64:     <div
 65:       className={clsx(
 66:         'bg-slate-900 rounded-xl border border-slate-800 overflow-hidden',
 67:         hover && 'hover:border-orange-500/30 transition-colors duration-200',
 68:         className
 69:       )}
 70:     >
 71:       {children}
 72:     </div>
 73:   );
 74: }
 75: interface StatusBadgeProps {
 76:   status: 'running' | 'stopped' | 'error' | 'paused' | 'pending_attestation' | 'attestation_failed';
 77: }
 78: export function StatusBadge({ status }: StatusBadgeProps) {
 79:   const colors = {
 80:     running: 'bg-green-500 shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(34,197,94,0.4)]',
 81:     stopped: 'bg-slate-500',
 82:     error: 'bg-red-500',
 83:     paused: 'bg-yellow-500',
 84:     pending_attestation: 'bg-yellow-400 animate-pulse',
 85:     attestation_failed: 'bg-red-500 shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(239,68,68,0.4)]',
 86:   };
 87:   return (
 88:     <div className={clsx('w-3 h-3 rounded-sm', colors[status])} />
 89:   );
 90: }
 91: interface EmptyStateProps {
 92:   title: string;
 93:   description?: string;
 94:   icon?: ReactNode;
 95: }
 96: export function EmptyState({ title, description, icon }: EmptyStateProps) {
 97:   return (
 98:     <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-12 text-slate-500 gap-2">
 99:       {icon && <div className="p-4 bg-slate-900 rounded-full border border-slate-800">{icon}</div>}
100:       <h3 className="text-lg font-medium text-slate-400">{title}</h3>
101:       {description && <p className="text-sm">{description}</p>}
102:     </div>
103:   );
104: }
105: interface LoadingProps {
106:   text?: string;
107: }
108: export function Loading({ text = 'Loading...' }: LoadingProps) {
109:   return (
110:     <div className="flex items-center justify-center py-12">
111:       <div className="text-slate-500 animate-pulse">{text}</div>
112:     </div>
113:   );
114: }

================
File: ui/src/hooks/useApi.ts
================
 1: import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
 2: const API_BASE = 'http://localhost:3000';
 3: function getApiToken(): string | null {
 4:   if (typeof window === 'undefined') return null;
 5:   const meta = document.querySelector('meta[name="vyoma-api-token"]');
 6:   return meta?.getAttribute('content') ?? null;
 7: }
 8: export function useApi<T>(endpoint: string, options?: { autoFetch?: boolean }) {
 9:   const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
10:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
11:   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
12:   const doFetch = async () => {
13:     if (!API_BASE) return;
14:     setLoading(true);
15:     setError(null);
16:     try {
17:       const headers: HeadersInit = {};
18:       const token = getApiToken();
19:       if (token) {
20:         headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${token}`;
21:       }
22:       const res = await globalThis.fetch(`${API_BASE}${endpoint}`, { headers });
23:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
24:       const json = await res.json();
25:       setData(json);
26:     } catch (e) {
27:       setError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Unknown error');
28:     } finally {
29:       setLoading(false);
30:     }
31:   };
32:   useEffect(() => {
33:     if (options?.autoFetch !== false) doFetch();
34:   }, [endpoint]);
35:   return { data, loading, error, refetch: doFetch };
36: }
37: export function useVmList() {
38:   return useApi<{ vms: Vm[] }>('/ps');
39: }
40: export function useImages() {
41:   return useApi<string[]>('/images');
42: }
43: export function useVolumes() {
44:   return useApi<Volume[]>('/volumes');
45: }
46: export function useNetworks() {
47:   return useApi<{ networks: Network[] }>('/networks');
48: }
49: export function useSwarmNodes() {
50:   return useApi<SwarmNode[]>('/swarm/nodes');
51: }
52: export function useSnapshots(vmId: string) {
53:   return useApi<{ snapshots: Snapshot[] }>(vmId ? `/snapshots/${vmId}` : '');
54: }
55: export interface Vm {
56:   id: string;
57:   labels: Record<string, string>;
58:   status?: string;
59:   ip_address?: string;
60: }
61: export interface Volume {
62:   name: string;
63:   path: string;
64: }
65: export interface Network {
66:   name: string;
67:   subnet: string;
68: }
69: export interface SwarmNode {
70:   hostname: string;
71:   role: string;
72:   ip: string;
73:   resources?: {
74:     cpu_usage: number;
75:     memory_usage_mb: number;
76:   };
77: }
78: export interface Snapshot {
79:   id: string;
80:   label?: string;
81:   created_at: number;
82:   size_bytes: number;
83: }

================
File: ui/src/views/HubBrowserView.tsx
================
 1: import { useState } from 'react';
 2: import { Search, Plus } from 'lucide-react';
 3: import { Button } from '../components/ui';
 4: interface HubImage {
 5:   name: string;
 6:   description: string;
 7:   stars: number;
 8:   pulls: string;
 9: }
10: const API_BASE = import.meta.env.DEV ? 'http://localhost:3000' : '';
11: export function HubBrowserView() {
12:   const [query, setQuery] = useState('');
13:   const [images, setImages] = useState<HubImage[]>([]);
14:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
15:   const searchHub = async () => {
16:     if (!query.trim()) return;
17:     setLoading(true);
18:     try {
19:       const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/hub/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}`);
20:       const data = await res.json();
21:       setImages(data.images || []);
22:     } catch {
23:       setImages([]);
24:     }
25:     setLoading(false);
26:   };
27:   const pullImage = async (name: string) => {
28:     await fetch(`${API_BASE}/pull`, {
29:       method: 'POST',
30:       body: JSON.stringify({ image: name }),
31:     });
32:     alert(`Pulling ${name}...`);
33:   };
34:   return (
35:     <div className="p-8 max-w-6xl mx-auto">
36:       <header className="mb-8">
37:         <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-1">Hub Browser</h2>
38:         <p className="text-sm text-slate-400">Search images from Vyoma Hub or Docker Hub.</p>
39:       </header>
40:       <div className="flex gap-3 mb-6">
41:         <div className="flex-1 relative">
42:           <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-slate-500" size={18} />
43:           <input
44:             value={query}
45:             onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
46:             onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && searchHub()}
47:             placeholder="Search images..."
48:             className="w-full bg-slate-900 border border-slate-700 rounded-lg pl-10 pr-4 py-2.5 text-white placeholder-slate-500 focus:border-orange-500 focus:outline-none"
49:           />
50:         </div>
51:         <Button onClick={searchHub}>Search</Button>
52:       </div>
53:       {loading ? (
54:         <div className="text-center py-12 text-slate-500 animate-pulse">Searching Hub...</div>
55:       ) : images.length === 0 ? (
56:         <div className="text-center py-12 text-slate-500">Search for images to browse the Hub.</div>
57:       ) : (
58:         <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4">
59:           {images.map((img, i) => (
60:             <div
61:               key={i}
62:               className="bg-slate-900 border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-4 hover:border-orange-500/30 transition"
63:             >
64:               <div className="flex items-start justify-between">
65:                 <div className="flex-1">
66:                   <h3 className="font-semibold text-white">{img.name}</h3>
67:                   <p className="text-sm text-slate-500 mt-1 line-clamp-2">{img.description || 'No description'}</p>
68:                 </div>
69:                 <button
70:                   onClick={() => pullImage(img.name)}
71:                   className="p-2 bg-slate-800 hover:bg-orange-600 rounded-lg text-slate-400 hover:text-white transition ml-3"
72:                 >
73:                   <Plus size={16} />
74:                 </button>
75:               </div>
76:               <div className="flex gap-4 mt-3 text-xs text-slate-500">
77:                 <span>★ {img.stars}</span>
78:                 <span>↓ {img.pulls}</span>
79:               </div>
80:             </div>
81:           ))}
82:         </div>
83:       )}
84:     </div>
85:   );
86: }

================
File: ui/src/views/TimeMachineView.tsx
================
 1: import { useState } from 'react';
 2: import { Clock, Play, Trash2, Plus } from 'lucide-react';
 3: import { useVmList, useSnapshots } from '../hooks/useApi';
 4: import { Button, EmptyState, Loading } from '../components/ui';
 5: const API_BASE = import.meta.env.DEV ? 'http://localhost:3000' : '';
 6: export function TimeMachineView() {
 7:   const { data: vmsData } = useVmList();
 8:   const [selectedVm, setSelectedVm] = useState('');
 9:   const { data: snapshotData, loading, refetch } = useSnapshots(selectedVm);
10:   const snapshots = snapshotData?.snapshots || [];
11:   const formatTime = (ts: number) => new Date(ts * 1000).toLocaleString();
12:   const formatSize = (b: number) => (b < 1024 * 1024 ? `${(b / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB` : `${(b / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)}MB`);
13:   const handleRestore = async (snapId: string) => {
14:     if (!confirm(`Restore to snapshot ${snapId.slice(0, 8)}?`)) return;
15:     await fetch(`${API_BASE}/snapshots/${selectedVm}/restore`, {
16:       method: 'POST',
17:       body: JSON.stringify({ snapshot_id: snapId }),
18:     });
19:     alert('VM restored!');
20:   };
21:   const handleCreateSnapshot = async () => {
22:     if (!selectedVm) return;
23:     const name = prompt('Snapshot name (optional):');
24:     await fetch(`${API_BASE}/snapshots/${selectedVm}`, {
25:       method: 'POST',
26:       body: JSON.stringify({ name: name || '' }),
27:     });
28:     refetch();
29:   };
30:   return (
31:     <div className="p-8 max-w-6xl mx-auto">
32:       <header className="mb-8">
33:         <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-1">TimeMachine</h2>
34:         <p className="text-sm text-slate-400">View and restore VM snapshots in a timeline.</p>
35:       </header>
36:       <div className="flex gap-4 mb-6">
37:         <select
38:           value={selectedVm}
39:           onChange={(e) => setSelectedVm(e.target.value)}
40:           className="bg-slate-900 border border-slate-700 rounded-lg px-4 py-2 text-white min-w-[200px]"
41:         >
42:           <option value="">Select a VM...</option>
43:           {vmsData?.vms.map((v) => (
44:             <option key={v.id} value={v.id}>
45:               {v.labels['vyoma.service'] || v.id.slice(0, 12)}
46:             </option>
47:           ))}
48:         </select>
49:         <Button onClick={handleCreateSnapshot} disabled={!selectedVm}>
50:           <Plus size={16} /> Create Snapshot
51:         </Button>
52:       </div>
53:       {!selectedVm ? (
54:         <EmptyState title="Select a VM" description="Choose a VM to view its snapshot timeline." icon={<Clock size={48} />} />
55:       ) : loading ? (
56:         <Loading text="Loading snapshots..." />
57:       ) : snapshots.length === 0 ? (
58:         <EmptyState title="No snapshots" description="Create a snapshot to start TimeMachine." icon={<Clock size={48} />} />
59:       ) : (
60:         <div className="relative">
61:           <div className="absolute left-8 top-0 bottom-0 w-0.5 bg-slate-800" />
62:           <div className="space-y-4">
63:             {snapshots.map((snap, i) => (
64:               <div key={snap.id} className="relative flex items-start gap-6 ml-4">
65:                 <div className="absolute left-4 w-8 h-8 -ml-4 rounded-full bg-slate-800 border-2 border-orange-500 flex items-center justify-center z-10">
66:                   <Clock size={14} className="text-orange-500" />
67:                 </div>
68:                 <div className="flex-1 ml-8 bg-slate-900 border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-4 hover:border-orange-500/30 transition">
69:                   <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
70:                     <div>
71:                       <div className="font-semibold text-white">snap:{snapshots.length - i - 1}</div>
72:                       <div className="text-xs text-slate-500 mt-1">
73:                         {formatTime(snap.created_at)} · {formatSize(snap.size_bytes)}
74:                       </div>
75:                     </div>
76:                     <div className="flex gap-2">
77:                       <button
78:                         onClick={() => handleRestore(snap.id)}
79:                         className="p-2 hover:bg-slate-800 rounded text-slate-400 hover:text-white"
80:                         title="Restore"
81:                       >
82:                         <Play size={16} />
83:                       </button>
84:                       <button className="p-2 hover:bg-red-500/20 rounded text-slate-400 hover:text-red-400" title="Delete">
85:                         <Trash2 size={16} />
86:                       </button>
87:                     </div>
88:                   </div>
89:                   {snap.label && <div className="mt-2 text-sm text-slate-400">{snap.label}</div>}
90:                 </div>
91:               </div>
92:             ))}
93:           </div>
94:         </div>
95:       )}
96:     </div>
97:   );
98: }

================
File: ui/src/views/TopologyView.tsx
================
  1: import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
  2: import * as d3 from 'd3';
  3: import { useVmList, useNetworks } from '../hooks/useApi';
  4: interface TopologyNode {
  5:   id: string;
  6:   type: 'vm' | 'network';
  7:   label: string;
  8:   x?: number;
  9:   y?: number;
 10: }
 11: interface TopologyLink {
 12:   source: string;
 13:   target: string;
 14: }
 15: export function TopologyView() {
 16:   const svgRef = useRef<SVGSVGElement>(null);
 17:   const { data: vmsData } = useVmList();
 18:   const { data: netsData } = useNetworks();
 19:   const [nodes, setNodes] = useState<TopologyNode[]>([]);
 20:   const [links, setLinks] = useState<TopologyLink[]>([]);
 21:   useEffect(() => {
 22:     if (!vmsData?.vms || !netsData?.networks) return;
 23:     const topoNodes: TopologyNode[] = [
 24:       ...vmsData.vms.map((v) => ({ id: v.id, type: 'vm' as const, label: v.labels['vyoma.service'] || v.id.slice(0, 8) })),
 25:       ...netsData.networks.map((n: { name: string }) => ({ id: n.name, type: 'network' as const, label: n.name })),
 26:     ];
 27:     const topoLinks: TopologyLink[] = vmsData.vms.map((v) => ({ source: v.id, target: v.labels['network'] || 'default' }));
 28:     setNodes(topoNodes);
 29:     setLinks(topoLinks);
 30:   }, [vmsData, netsData]);
 31:   useEffect(() => {
 32:     if (!svgRef.current || nodes.length === 0) return;
 33:     const svg = d3.select(svgRef.current);
 34:     svg.selectAll('*').remove();
 35:     const width = svgRef.current.clientWidth || 800;
 36:     const height = svgRef.current.clientHeight || 500;
 37:     const simulation = d3.forceSimulation(nodes as any)
 38:       .force('link', d3.forceLink(links).id((d: any) => d.id).distance(120))
 39:       .force('charge', d3.forceManyBody().strength(-400))
 40:       .force('center', d3.forceCenter(width / 2, height / 2))
 41:       .force('collision', d3.forceCollide().radius(40));
 42:     const link = svg.append('g')
 43:       .selectAll('line')
 44:       .data(links)
 45:       .enter()
 46:       .append('line')
 47:       .attr('stroke', '#334155')
 48:       .attr('stroke-width', 2);
 49:     const node = svg.append('g')
 50:       .selectAll('g')
 51:       .data(nodes)
 52:       .enter()
 53:       .append('g')
 54:       .call(d3.drag<any, any>()
 55:         .on('start', (event, d) => {
 56:           if (!event.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0.3).restart();
 57:           d.fx = d.x;
 58:           d.fy = d.y;
 59:         })
 60:         .on('drag', (event, d) => {
 61:           d.fx = event.x;
 62:           d.fy = event.y;
 63:         })
 64:         .on('end', (event, d) => {
 65:           if (!event.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0);
 66:           delete d.fx;
 67:           delete d.fy;
 68:         })
 69:       );
 70:     node.append('circle')
 71:       .attr('r', 24)
 72:       .attr('fill', (d) => d.type === 'vm' ? '#f97316' : '#3b82f6')
 73:       .attr('stroke', '#1e293b')
 74:       .attr('stroke-width', 2);
 75:     node.append('text')
 76:       .text((d) => d.label.slice(0, 8))
 77:       .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
 78:       .attr('dy', 40)
 79:       .attr('fill', '#64748b')
 80:       .attr('font-size', '11px');
 81:     simulation.on('tick', () => {
 82:       link
 83:         .attr('x1', (d: any) => d.source.x)
 84:         .attr('y1', (d: any) => d.source.y)
 85:         .attr('x2', (d: any) => d.target.x)
 86:         .attr('y2', (d: any) => d.target.y);
 87:       node.attr('transform', (d: any) => `translate(${d.x},${d.y})`);
 88:     });
 89:   }, [nodes, links]);
 90:   return (
 91:     <div className="p-8 max-w-6xl mx-auto h-[600px] flex flex-col">
 92:       <header className="mb-4">
 93:         <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-1">Network Topology</h2>
 94:         <p className="text-sm text-slate-400">Interactive graph of VMs and networks. Drag nodes to reposition.</p>
 95:       </header>
 96:       <div className="flex-1 bg-slate-900 rounded-xl border border-slate-800 overflow-hidden relative">
 97:         {nodes.length === 0 ? (
 98:           <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center text-slate-500">
 99:             No topology data. Run some VMs first.
100:           </div>
101:         ) : (
102:           <svg ref={svgRef} className="w-full h-full" />
103:         )}
104:       </div>
105:     </div>
106:   );
107: }

 2: <html lang="en">

 7:     <title>Vyoma - MicroVM Dashboard</title>

================
File: vk8s/pkg/cri/container.go
================
  1: package cri
  2: import (
  3: 	"context"
  4: 	"encoding/json"
  5: 	"fmt"
  6: 	"sync"
  7: 	"time"
  8: 	pb "k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1"
  9: 	"github.com/vyoma/vk8s/pkg/agent"
 10: )
 11: type ContainerInfo struct {
 12: 	ID        string
 13: 	PodID     string
 14: 	Name      string
 15: 	Image     string
 16: 	Created   int64
 17: 	State     pb.ContainerState
 18: 	Config    *pb.ContainerConfig
 19: 	Pid       uint32
 20: 	StartTime int64
 21: }
 22: func (s *VyomaCriServer) CreateContainer(ctx context.Context, req *pb.CreateContainerRequest) (*pb.CreateContainerResponse, error) {
 23: 	podID := req.GetPodSandboxId()
 24: 	config := req.GetConfig()
 25: 	metadata := config.GetMetadata()
 26: 	s.logger.Printf("CreateContainer: pod=%s name=%s image=%s", podID, metadata.GetName(), config.GetImage().GetImage())
 27: 	s.mu.RLock()
 28: 	pod, ok := s.pods[podID]
 29: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
 30: 	if !ok {
 31: 		return nil, errorf(codes.NotFound, "pod not found: %s", podID)
 32: 	}
 33: 	containerID := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", pod.VMID, metadata.GetName())
 34: 	agentCli := agent.NewTCPClient(s.getVMIP(ctx, pod.VMID))
 35: 	if err := agentCli.Connect(ctx); err != nil {
 36: 		s.logError(ctx, "CreateContainer.agentConnect", err)
 37: 	}
 38: 	containerDir := fmt.Sprintf("/var/lib/vyoma/containers/%s", containerID)
 39: 	env := make(map[string]string)
 40: 	for _, e := range config.GetEnvs() {
 41: 		env[e.GetKey()] = e.GetValue()
 42: 	}
 43: 	mkdirCmd := []string{"mkdir", "-p", containerDir}
 44: 	if _, _, _, err := agentCli.ExecCommand(ctx, mkdirCmd, env, "/"); err != nil {
 45: 		s.logError(ctx, "CreateContainer.mkdir", err)
 46: 	}
 47: 	s.mu.Lock()
 48: 	s.containers[containerID] = &ContainerInfo{
 49: 		ID:      containerID,
 50: 		PodID:   podID,
 51: 		Name:    metadata.GetName(),
 52: 		Image:   config.GetImage().GetImage(),
 53: 		Created: time.Now().Unix(),
 54: 		State:   pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_CREATED,
 55: 		Config:  config,
 56: 	}
 57: 	s.mu.Unlock()
 58: 	agentCli.Close()
 59: 	s.logger.Printf("Container created: %s", containerID)
 60: 	return &pb.CreateContainerResponse{ContainerId: containerID}, nil
 61: }
 62: func (s *VyomaCriServer) StartContainer(ctx context.Context, req *pb.StartContainerRequest) (*pb.StartContainerResponse, error) {
 63: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
 64: 	s.logger.Printf("StartContainer: %s", containerID)
 65: 	s.mu.RLock()
 66: 	container, ok := s.containers[containerID]
 67: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
 68: 	if !ok {
 69: 		return nil, errorf(codes.NotFound, "container not found: %s", containerID)
 70: 	}
 71: 	s.mu.RLock()
 72: 	pod, ok := s.pods[container.PodID]
 73: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
 74: 	if !ok {
 75: 		return nil, errorf(codes.NotFound, "pod not found for container: %s", containerID)
 76: 	}
 77: 	agentCli := agent.NewTCPClient(s.getVMIP(ctx, pod.VMID))
 78: 	if err := agentCli.Connect(ctx); err != nil {
 79: 		return nil, errorf(codes.Internal, "connect to VM agent: %v", err)
 80: 	}
 81: 	defer agentCli.Close()
 82: 	env := make(map[string]string)
 83: 	for _, e := range container.Config.GetEnvs() {
 84: 		env[e.GetKey()] = e.GetValue()
 85: 	}
 86: 	cmd := []string{"/bin/sh", "-c", "echo container started"}
 87: 	workdir := "/"
 88: 	if container.Config != nil {
 89: 		if len(container.Config.Command) > 0 {
 90: 			cmd = container.Config.Command
 91: 		}
 92: 		if container.Config.WorkingDir != "" {
 93: 			workdir = container.Config.WorkingDir
 94: 		}
 95: 	}
 96: 	stdout, stderr, exitCode, err := agentCli.ExecCommand(ctx, cmd, env, workdir)
 97: 	if err != nil {
 98: 		s.logError(ctx, "StartContainer.exec", err)
 99: 	}
100: 	s.logger.Printf("Container start: %s exit=%d stdout=%s stderr=%s", containerID, exitCode, string(stdout), string(stderr))
101: 	s.mu.Lock()
102: 	container.State = pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_RUNNING
103: 	container.StartTime = time.Now().Unix()
104: 	s.mu.Unlock()
105: 	return &pb.StartContainerResponse{}, nil
106: }
107: func (s *VyomaCriServer) StopContainer(ctx context.Context, req *pb.StopContainerRequest) (*pb.StopContainerResponse, error) {
108: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
109: 	timeout := req.GetTimeout()
110: 	if timeout == 0 {
111: 		timeout = 10
112: 	}
113: 	s.logger.Printf("StopContainer: %s timeout=%ds", containerID, timeout)
114: 	s.mu.RLock()
115: 	container, ok := s.containers[containerID]
116: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
117: 	if !ok {
118: 		return &pb.StopContainerResponse{}, nil
119: 	}
120: 	s.mu.RLock()
121: 	pod, ok := s.pods[container.PodID]
122: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
123: 	if ok {
124: 		agentCli := agent.NewTCPClient(s.getVMIP(ctx, pod.VMID))
125: 		if err := agentCli.Connect(ctx); err == nil {
126: 			defer agentCli.Close()
127: 			sigTerm := []string{"kill", "-TERM", "1"}
128: 			agentCli.ExecCommand(ctx, sigTerm, nil, "/")
129: 			time.Sleep(time.Duration(timeout) * time.Second)
130: 			sigKill := []string{"kill", "-KILL", "1"}
131: 			agentCli.ExecCommand(ctx, sigKill, nil, "/")
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	s.mu.Lock()
135: 	container.State = pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_EXITED
136: 	s.mu.Unlock()
137: 	return &pb.StopContainerResponse{}, nil
138: }
139: func (s *VyomaCriServer) RemoveContainer(ctx context.Context, req *pb.RemoveContainerRequest) (*pb.RemoveContainerResponse, error) {
140: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
141: 	s.logger.Printf("RemoveContainer: %s", containerID)
142: 	s.mu.Lock()
143: 	container, ok := s.containers[containerID]
144: 	if ok {
145: 		s.mu.RUnlock()
146: 		s.mu.RLock()
147: 		pod, podOk := s.pods[container.PodID]
148: 		s.mu.RUnlock()
149: 		if podOk {
150: 			agentCli := agent.NewTCPClient(s.getVMIP(ctx, pod.VMID))
151: 			if err := agentCli.Connect(ctx); err == nil {
152: 				defer agentCli.Close()
153: 				cleanupCmd := []string{"rm", "-rf", fmt.Sprintf("/var/lib/vyoma/containers/%s", containerID)}
154: 				agentCli.ExecCommand(ctx, cleanupCmd, nil, "/")
155: 			}
156: 		}
157: 		s.mu.Lock()
158: 	}
159: 	delete(s.containers, containerID)
160: 	s.mu.Unlock()
161: 	return &pb.RemoveContainerResponse{}, nil
162: }
163: func (s *VyomaCriServer) ContainerStatus(ctx context.Context, req *pb.ContainerStatusRequest) (*pb.ContainerStatusResponse, error) {
164: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
165: 	verbose := req.GetVerbose()
166: 	s.mu.RLock()
167: 	container, ok := s.containers[containerID]
168: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
169: 	if !ok {
170: 		return nil, errorf(codes.NotFound, "container not found: %s", containerID)
171: 	}
172: 	state := container.State
173: 	reason := ""
174: 	startedAt := container.StartTime
175: 	finishedAt := int64(0)
176: 	exitCode := int32(0)
177: 	signal := int32(0)
178: 	restartCount := uint32(0)
179: 	switch state {
180: 	case pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_CREATED:
181: 		reason = "Created"
182: 	case pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_RUNNING:
183: 		reason = "Running"
184: 	case pb.ContainerState_CONTAINER_EXITED:
185: 		reason = "Exited"
186: 		finishedAt = time.Now().Unix()
187: 		exitCode = 0
188: 	}
189: 	var info map[string]string
190: 	if verbose {
191: 		s.mu.RLock()
192: 		pod, ok := s.pods[container.PodID]
193: 		s.mu.RUnlock()
194: 		if ok {
195: 			agentCli := agent.NewTCPClient(s.getVMIP(ctx, pod.VMID))
196: 			if err := agentCli.Connect(ctx); err == nil {
197: 				defer agentCli.Close()
198: 				if metrics, err := agentCli.GetMetrics(ctx); err == nil {
199: 					info = map[string]string{
200: 						"mem_used_kb":    fmt.Sprintf("%d", metrics.MemUsedKb),
201: 						"mem_total_kb":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", metrics.MemTotalKb),
202: 						"process_count":  fmt.Sprintf("%d", metrics.ProcessCount),
203: 					}
204: 				}
205: 			}
206: 		}
207: 	}
208: 	return &pb.ContainerStatusResponse{
209: 		Status: &pb.ContainerStatus{
210: 			Id:                container.ID,
211: 			Metadata:          &pb.ContainerMetadata{Name: container.Name},
212: 			State:             state,
213: 			CreatedAt:         container.Created,
214: 			StartedAt:         startedAt,
215: 			FinishedAt:        finishedAt,
216: 			ExitCode:          exitCode,
217: 			Signal:            signal,
218: 			RestartCount:      restartCount,
219: 			Image:             &pb.ImageSpec{Image: container.Image},
220: 			ImageRef:          container.Image,
221: 			Reason:            reason,
222: 			LogPath:           fmt.Sprintf("/var/log/pods/%s/%s.log", container.PodID, container.ID),
223: 			Labels:            container.Config.GetLabels(),
224: 			Annotations:       container.Config.GetAnnotations(),
225: 		},
226: 		Info: info,
227: 	}, nil
228: }
229: func (s *VyomaCriServer) ListContainers(ctx context.Context, req *pb.ListContainersRequest) (*pb.ListContainersResponse, error) {
230: 	s.mu.RLock()
231: 	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
232: 	filter := req.GetFilter()
233: 	containers := make([]*pb.Container, 0, len(s.containers))
234: 	for _, c := range s.containers {
235: 		if filter != nil {
236: 			if filter.Id != "" && c.ID != filter.Id {
237: 				continue
238: 			}
239: 			if filter.PodSandboxId != "" && c.PodID != filter.PodSandboxId {
240: 				continue
241: 			}
242: 			if filter.State != nil && c.State != filter.State.State {
243: 				continue
244: 			}
245: 			if len(filter.LabelSelectors) > 0 {
246: 				match := true
247: 				for _, sel := range filter.LabelSelectors {
248: 					if c.Config == nil || c.Config.Labels == nil {
249: 						match = false
250: 						break
251: 					}
252: 					if val, ok := c.Config.Labels[sel]; !ok || val == "" {
253: 						match = false
254: 						break
255: 					}
256: 				}
257: 				if !match {
258: 					continue
259: 				}
260: 			}
261: 		}
262: 		containers = append(containers, &pb.Container{
263: 			Id:          c.ID,
264: 			PodSandboxId: c.PodID,
265: 			Metadata:    &pb.ContainerMetadata{Name: c.Name},
266: 			Image:       c.Image,
267: 			ImageRef:    c.Image,
268: 			State:       c.State,
269: 			CreatedAt:   c.Created,
270: 			Labels:      c.Config.GetLabels(),
271: 		})
272: 	}
273: 	return &pb.ListContainersResponse{Containers: containers}, nil
274: }
275: func (s *VyomaCriServer) UpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, req *pb.UpdateContainerResourcesRequest) (*pb.UpdateContainerResourcesResponse, error) {
276: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
277: 	s.logger.Printf("UpdateContainerResources: %s", containerID)
278: 	s.mu.RLock()
279: 	container, ok := s.containers[containerID]
280: 	s.mu.RUnlock()
281: 	if !ok {
282: 		return nil, errorf(codes.NotFound, "container not found: %s", containerID)
283: 	}
284: 	if req.GetLinux() != nil {
285: 		s.logger.Printf("  linux resources: cpu=%v memory=%v", req.Linux.CPU, req.Linux.Memory)
286: 	}
287: 	return &pb.UpdateContainerResourcesResponse{}, nil
288: }

290: 	containerID := req.GetContainerId()
291: 	s.logger.Printf("ReopenContainerLog: %s", containerID)
292: 	return &pb.ReopenContainerLogResponse{}, nil
293: }
294: func (s *VyomaCriServer) getVMIP(ctx context.Context, vmID string) string {
295: 	return s.getVMIPWithLookup(ctx, vmID)
296: }
297: func (s *VyomaCriServer) getVMIPWithLookup(ctx context.Context, vmID string) string {
298: 	data, err := s.httpRequest(ctx, "GET", "/vms/"+vmID, nil)
299: 	if err != nil {
300: 		return "10.0.0.2"
301: 	}
302: 	var vmInfo struct {
303: 		IP string `json:"ip"`
304: 	}
305: 	if json.Unmarshal(data, &vmInfo) == nil && vmInfo.IP != "" {
306: 		return vmInfo.IP
307: 	}
308: 	return "10.0.0.2"
309: }
310: type containerCreateRequest struct {
311: 	ContainerID string                 `json:"container_id"`
312: 	Image       string                 `json:"image"`
313: 	Cmd         []string               `json:"cmd"`
314: 	Env         []string               `json:"env"`
315: 	Workdir     string                 `json:"workdir"`
316: 	Mounts      []*containerMount      `json:"mounts"`
317: }
318: type containerMount struct {
319: 	HostPath      string `json:"host_path"`
320: 	ContainerPath string `json:"container_path"`
321: 	Readonly      bool   `json:"readonly"`
322: }

325: 	PID         uint32 `json:"pid"`
326: }

 32: 	pod := &PodSandbox{

================
File: crates/vyoma-core/src/network.rs
================
 1: use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
 2: use std::process::Command;
 3: use tracing::{info, warn};
 4: pub struct NetworkManager;
 5: impl NetworkManager {
 6:     pub fn setup_bridge(name: &str, ip_cidr: &str) -> Result<()> {
 7:         info!("Delegating to vyoma-net BridgeManager for bridge {}", name);
 8:         let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()?;
 9:         rt.block_on(async {
10:             let bm = vyoma_net::BridgeManager::new().await?;
11:             let idx = bm.create_bridge(name).await?;
12:             bm.set_up(name).await?;
13:             bm.set_ip(name, ip_cidr).await?;
14:             info!("Bridge {} created with index {}", name, idx);
15:             Ok(())
16:         })
17:     }
18:     pub fn setup_tap(tap_name: &str, bridge_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
19:         info!("Delegating to vyoma-net for TAP {}", tap_name);
20:         let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()?;
21:         rt.block_on(async {
22:             let tm = vyoma_net::TapManager::new().await?;
23:             tm.create_tap(tap_name).await?;
24:             tm.set_up(tap_name).await?;
25:             let bm = vyoma_net::BridgeManager::new().await?;
26:             bm.add_tap_to_bridge(tap_name, bridge_name).await?;
27:             info!("TAP {} attached to bridge {}", tap_name, bridge_name);
28:             Ok(())
29:         })
30:     }
31:     pub fn remove_interface(name: &str) -> Result<()> {
32:         info!("Removing network interface '{}'", name);
33:         let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()?;
34:         rt.block_on(async {
35:             let bm = vyoma_net::BridgeManager::new().await?;
36:             match bm.delete_bridge(name).await {
37:                 Ok(_) => {
38:                     info!("Bridge {} deleted", name);
39:                     Ok(())
40:                 }
41:                 Err(_) => {
42:                     let tm = vyoma_net::TapManager::new().await?;
43:                     tm.delete_tap(name).await?;
44:                     info!("Interface {} deleted", name);
45:                     Ok(())
46:                 }
47:             }
48:         })
49:     }
50:     pub fn setup_nat(bridge_cidr: &str) -> Result<()> {
51:         info!("Enabling NAT/Masquerade for source {}", bridge_cidr);
52:         // Enable IP forwarding by writing to procfs (native approach)
53:         if let Err(e) = std::fs::write("/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward", "1\n") {
54:             warn!("Failed to enable ip_forward via procfs: {}", e);
55:         }
56:         // Use native iptables crate instead of CLI
57:         let ip = match iptables::new(false) {
58:             Ok(ip) => ip,
59:             Err(e) => return Err(anyhow!("Failed to initialize iptables: {}", e)),
60:         };
61:         // Check if MASQUERADE rule already exists using the exists() method
62:         let rule = format!("-s {} ! -d {} -j MASQUERADE", bridge_cidr, bridge_cidr);
63:         let rule_exists = ip.exists("nat", "POSTROUTING", &rule).unwrap_or(false);
64:         if !rule_exists {
65:             // Add MASQUERADE rule for traffic from the bridge network going to other networks
66:             if let Err(e) = ip.append("nat", "POSTROUTING", &rule) {
67:                 return Err(anyhow!("Failed to setup NAT: {}", e));
68:             }
69:             info!("NAT rule added for {}", bridge_cidr);
70:         } else {
71:             info!("NAT rule for {} already exists.", bridge_cidr);
72:         }
73:         Ok(())
74:     }
75:     pub fn setup_tc_redirect(if_ingress: &str, if_egress: &str) -> Result<()> {
76:         info!("Setting up TC redirect: {} <-> {}", if_ingress, if_egress);
77:         let run_tc = |args: &[&str]| -> Result<()> {
78:             let out = Command::new("tc")
79:                 .args(args)
80:                 .output()?;
81:             if !out.status.success() {
82:                  let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr);
83:                  if !stderr.contains("File exists") {
84:                      return Err(anyhow!("TC failed ({:?}): {}", args, stderr));
85:                  }
86:              }
87:              Ok(())
88:          };
89:         run_tc(&["qdisc", "add", "dev", if_ingress, "ingress"])?;
90:         run_tc(&["filter", "add", "dev", if_ingress, "parent", "ffff:", "protocol", "all", "u32", "match", "u32", "0", "0", "action", "mirred", "egress", "redirect", "dev", if_egress])?;
91:         run_tc(&["qdisc", "add", "dev", if_egress, "ingress"])?;
92:         run_tc(&["filter", "add", "dev", if_egress, "parent", "ffff:", "protocol", "all", "u32", "match", "u32", "0", "0", "action", "mirred", "egress", "redirect", "dev", if_ingress])?;
93:         Ok(())
94:     }
95: }

================
File: crates/vyoma-core/src/storage.rs
================
  1: use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
  2: use std::path::Path;
  3: use std::process::Command;
  4: use tracing::{info, warn};
  5: pub struct StorageManager;
  6: impl StorageManager {
  7:     pub fn create_empty_file(path: &Path, size_mb: u64) -> Result<()> {
  8:         info!("Creating {}MB file at {:?}", size_mb, path);
  9:         let size_arg = format!("{}M", size_mb);
 10:         let status = Command::new("truncate")
 11:             .arg("-s")
 12:             .arg(size_arg)
 13:             .arg(path)
 14:             .status()
 15:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to execute truncate: {}", e))?;
 16:         if !status.success() {
 17:             return Err(anyhow!("truncate failed with status: {}", status));
 18:         }
 19:         Ok(())
 20:     }
 21:     pub fn format_ext4(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
 22:         info!("Formatting {:?} as ext4", path);
 23:         let status = Command::new("mkfs.ext4")
 24:             .arg("-F") 
 25:             .arg(path)
 26:             .status()
 27:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to execute mkfs.ext4: {}", e))?;
 28:         if !status.success() {
 29:             return Err(anyhow!("mkfs.ext4 failed with status: {}", status));
 30:         }
 31:         Ok(())
 32:     }
 33:     pub fn populate_image(image_path: &Path, source_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {
 34:         let mount_point = tempfile::tempdir()?;
 35:         let mount_path = mount_point.path();
 36:         info!("Mounting {:?} to {:?}", image_path, mount_path);
 37:         let status = Command::new("mount")
 38:             .arg("-o")
 39:             .arg("loop")
 40:             .arg(image_path)
 41:             .arg(mount_path)
 42:             .status()
 43:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to execute mount: {}", e))?;
 44:         if !status.success() {
 45:              return Err(anyhow!("Mount failed. Status: {}", status));
 46:         }
 47:         info!("Copying files from {:?} to {:?}", source_dir, mount_path);
 48:         let src_pattern = format!("{}/.", source_dir.to_string_lossy());
 49:         let status = Command::new("cp")
 50:             .arg("-a")
 51:             .arg(&src_pattern)
 52:             .arg(mount_path)
 53:             .status()?;
 54:         if !status.success() {
 55:              let _ = Command::new("umount").arg(mount_path).status();
 56:              return Err(anyhow!("Failed to copy files"));
 57:         }
 58:         info!("Unmounting...");
 59:         let status = Command::new("umount")
 60:             .arg(mount_path)
 61:             .status()?;
 62:         if !status.success() {
 63:             warn!("Failed to unmount {:?}. You may need to do it manually.", mount_path);
 64:         }
 65: Ok(())
 66:     }
 67:     pub fn setup_loop_device(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
 68:         info!("Delegating to vyoma-storage LoopManager for {:?}", path);
 69:         let lm = vyoma_storage::LoopManager::new()?;
 70:         let device = lm.attach(path)?;
 71:         let device_path = device.path().to_string_lossy().to_string();
 72:         info!("Attached {:?} to {}", path, device_path);
 73:         Ok(device_path)
 74:     }
 75:     pub fn detach_loop_device(device_path: &str) -> Result<()> {
 76:         info!("Delegating to vyoma-storage for loop device detach: {}", device_path);
 77:         let lm = vyoma_storage::LoopManager::new()?;
 78:         let device = vyoma_storage::LoopDevice::new(
 79:             std::path::PathBuf::from(device_path),
 80:             None,
 81:         );
 82:         lm.detach(&device)?;
 83:         info!("Detached loop device {}", device_path);
 84:         Ok(())
 85:     }
 86:     pub fn create_dm_snapshot(name: &str, base_dev: &str, cow_dev: &str, _size_sectors: u64) -> Result<String> {
 87:         use vyoma_storage::DmManager;
 88:         let dm_manager = DmManager::new()?;
 89:         let base_path = std::path::Path::new(base_dev);
 90:         let cow_path = std::path::Path::new(cow_dev);
 91:         let dm_device = dm_manager.create_snapshot(&name, base_path, cow_path)?;
 92:         Ok(dm_device.path().to_string_lossy().to_string())
 93:     }
 94:     pub fn remove_dm_device(name: &str) -> Result<()> {
 95:         use vyoma_storage::DmManager;
 96:         let dm_manager = DmManager::new()?;
 97:         dm_manager.remove_snapshot(name)?;
 98:         Ok(())
 99:     }
100:     pub fn commit_snapshot(src_device: &Path, dst_file: &Path) -> Result<()> {
101:         info!("Committing block device {:?} to file {:?}", src_device, dst_file);
102:         if !src_device.exists() {
103:             return Err(anyhow!("Source device does not exist: {:?}", src_device));
104:         }
105:         let mut src_f = std::fs::File::open(src_device).map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to open src: {}", e))?;
106:         let mut dst_f = std::fs::File::create(dst_file).map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to create dst: {}", e))?;
107:         std::io::copy(&mut src_f, &mut dst_f).map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to copy blocks: {}", e))?;
108:         Ok(())
109:     }
110: }

 89: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
 90: pub struct VmifImage {
 91:     pub manifest: VmifManifest,
 92:     pub rootfs_path: PathBuf,
 93:     pub kernel_path: Option<PathBuf>,
 94:     pub initrd_path: Option<PathBuf>,
 95: }
 96: #[derive(Error, Debug)]
 97: pub enum VmifError {
 98:     #[error("IO error: {0}")]
 99:     Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
100:     #[error("JSON error: {0}")]
101:     Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
102:     #[error("Invalid schema version: {0}")]
103:     InvalidSchemaVersion(u32),
104:     #[error("Missing required field: {0}")]
105:     MissingField(String),
106: }
107: impl VmifManifest {
108:     pub fn new(
109:         arch: String,
110:         kernel: Option<String>,
111:         initrd: Option<String>,
112:         rootfs: String,
113:         config: OciImageConfig,
114:         size_bytes: u64,
115:     ) -> Self {
116:         Self {
117:             schema_version: 1,
118:             created: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
119:             arch,
120:             kernel,
121:             initrd,
122:             rootfs,
123:             config,
124:             labels: HashMap::new(),
125:             size_bytes,
126:             firmware: FirmwareInfo::default(),
127:             measured_boot: MeasuredBootInfo::default(),
128:             confidential_computing: ConfidentialComputingInfo::default(),
129:         }
130:     }
131:     pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), VmifError> {
132:         if self.schema_version != 1 {
133:             return Err(VmifError::InvalidSchemaVersion(self.schema_version));
134:         }
135:         if self.rootfs.is_empty() {
136:             return Err(VmifError::MissingField("rootfs".to_string()));
137:         }
138:         if self.arch.is_empty() {
139:             return Err(VmifError::MissingField("arch".to_string()));
140:         }
141:         Ok(())
142:     }
143:     pub fn with_labels(mut self, labels: HashMap<String, String>) -> Self {
144:         self.labels = labels;
145:         self
146:     }
147:     pub fn full_command(&self) -> Vec<String> {
148:         let mut cmd = vec![];
149:         if let Some(ep) = &self.config.entrypoint {
150:             cmd.extend_from_slice(ep);
151:         }
152:         if let Some(c) = &self.config.cmd {
153:             cmd.extend_from_slice(c);
154:         }
155:         if cmd.is_empty() {
156:             cmd.push("/bin/sh".to_string());
157:         }
158:         cmd
159:     }
160: }
161: impl VmifImage {
162:     pub fn new(manifest: VmifManifest, rootfs_path: PathBuf) -> Self {
163:         Self {
164:             manifest,
165:             rootfs_path,
166:             kernel_path: None,
167:             initrd_path: None,
168:         }
169:     }
170:     pub fn with_kernel(mut self, kernel_path: PathBuf) -> Self {
171:         self.kernel_path = Some(kernel_path);
172:         self
173:     }
174:     pub fn with_initrd(mut self, initrd_path: PathBuf) -> Self {
175:         self.initrd_path = Some(initrd_path);
176:         self
177:     }
178:     pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), VmifError> {
179:         self.manifest.validate()?;
180:         if !self.rootfs_path.exists() {
181:             return Err(VmifError::MissingField(format!(
182:                 "rootfs not found at {:?}",
183:                 self.rootfs_path
184:             )));
185:         }
186:         if let Some(ref kernel) = self.kernel_path {
187:             if !kernel.exists() {
188:                 return Err(VmifError::MissingField(format!(
189:                     "kernel not found at {:?}",
190:                     kernel
191:                 )));
192:             }
193:         }
194:         if let Some(ref initrd) = self.initrd_path {
195:             if !initrd.exists() {
196:                 return Err(VmifError::MissingField(format!(
197:                     "initrd not found at {:?}",
198:                     initrd
199:                 )));
200:             }
201:         }
202:         Ok(())
203:     }
204: }

File: crates/vyoma-storage/src/cow.rs
================
  1: use std::fs::File;
  2: #[cfg(unix)]
  3: use std::os::unix::fs::FileTypeExt;
  4: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
  5: use tracing::{info, warn};
  6: use loopdev::LoopControl;
  7: use crate::error::{StorageError, Result};
  8: pub struct LoopDevice {
  9:     pub path: PathBuf,
 10:     // Store the underlying loopdev device so we can detach it natively
 11:     device: Option<loopdev::LoopDevice>,
 12: }
 13: impl LoopDevice {
 14:     pub fn new(path: PathBuf, device: Option<loopdev::LoopDevice>) -> Self {
 15:         Self { path, device }
 16:     }
 17:     pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {
 18:         &self.path
 19:     }
 20: }
 21: pub struct LoopManager {
 22:     control: LoopControl,
 23: }
 24: impl LoopManager {
 25:     pub fn new() -> Result<Self> {
 26:         info!("Initializing Loop manager");
 27:         let control = LoopControl::open().map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(e))?;
 28:         Ok(Self { control })
 29:     }
 30:     pub fn attach(&self, file: &Path) -> Result<LoopDevice> {
 31:         info!("Attaching loop device to {:?}", file);
 32:         if !file.exists() {
 33:             return Err(StorageError::NotFound(format!("File not found: {:?}", file)));
 34:         }
 35:         let ld = self.control.next_free().map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(e))?;
 36:         // Ensure read/write
 37:         ld.with().read_only(false).attach(file).map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(e))?;
 38:         let loop_path = ld.path().unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(""));
 39:         Ok(LoopDevice::new(loop_path, Some(ld)))
 40:     }
 41:     pub fn detach(&self, device: &LoopDevice) -> Result<()> {
 42:         info!("Detaching loop device {:?}", device.path);
 43:         if let Some(ld) = &device.device {
 44:             ld.detach().map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(e))?;
 45:         } else {
 46:             let path_str = match device.path.to_str() {
 47:                 Some(s) => s,
 48:                 None => {
 49:                     warn!("Loop device path is not valid UTF-8; skipping detach");
 50:                     return Ok(());
 51:                 }
 52:             };
 53:             #[cfg(unix)]
 54:             {
 55:                 let path = std::path::Path::new(path_str);
 56:                 let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path) {
 57:                     Ok(m) => m,
 58:                     Err(_) => {
 59:                         info!("Loop device {} already removed; nothing to detach", path_str);
 60:                         return Ok(());
 61:                     }
 62:                 };
 63:                 if !metadata.file_type().is_block_device() {
 64:                     warn!("Path {} is not a block device; skipping detach", path_str);
 65:                     return Ok(());
 66:                 }
 67:                 match loopdev::LoopDevice::open(path_str) {
 68:                     Ok(ld) => {
 69:                         if let Err(e) = ld.detach() {
 70:                             warn!("Failed to detach loop device {} via fallback: {}; assuming it is already freed", path_str, e);
 71:                         }
 72:                     }
 73:                     Err(e) => {
 74:                         warn!("Failed to open loop device {} via fallback: {}; assuming already detached", path_str, e);
 75:                     }
 76:                 }
 77:             }
 78:             #[cfg(not(unix))]
 79:             {
 80:                 warn!("Loop device detachment not supported on non-Unix platforms");
 81:             }
 82:         }
 83:         Ok(())
 84:     }
 85:     pub fn create_cow_file(path: &Path, size_mb: u64) -> Result<()> {
 86:         info!("Creating COW file: {:?} ({} MB)", path, size_mb);
 87:         if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
 88:             std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
 89:         }
 90:         let file = File::create(path)?;
 91:         file.set_len(size_mb * 1024 * 1024)?;
 92:         info!("COW file created successfully");
 93:         Ok(())
 94:     }
 95:     pub fn get_size(path: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
 96:         let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path)?;
 97:         Ok(metadata.len())
 98:     }
 99:     pub fn is_attached(&self, device: &LoopDevice) -> Result<bool> {
100:         Ok(device.path.exists()) // Proper check might involve interrogating LoopControl natively
101:     }
102:     pub fn list_devices(&self) -> Result<Vec<LoopDevice>> {
103:         info!("Listing loop devices");
104:         let mut devices = Vec::new();
105:         // Since list_devices isn't trivially exposed or needed safely right now, we keep it minimum
106:         Ok(devices)
107:     }
108: }

114:     fn test_create_cow_file() {

116:         let cow_path = temp_dir.path().join("test.cow");
117:         LoopManager::create_cow_file(&cow_path, 100).unwrap();

134:         let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
135:         let regular_file = temp_dir.path().join("not_a_device");
136:         std::fs::write(&regular_file, b"test").unwrap();
137:         let device = LoopDevice::new(regular_file, None);
138:         let result = manager.detach(&device);
139:         assert!(result.is_ok());
140:     }
141: }

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/state/recovery.rs
================
  1: use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
  2: use std::collections::HashMap;
  3: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
  4: use std::sync::Arc;
  5: use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};
  6: use tracing::{info, warn, error};
  7: use super::wal::Wal;
  8: use super::AppState;
  9: use super::VmState;
 10: use vyoma_core::vmm::VmmManager;
 11: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
 12: pub enum VmRecoveryStatus {
 13:     Running,
 14:     Stopped,
 15:     Crashed,
 16:     Unknown,
 17: }
 18: #[derive(Debug)]
 19: pub struct RecoveredVm {
 20:     pub vm_id: String,
 21:     pub state: VmState,
 22:     pub status: VmRecoveryStatus,
 23: }
 24: pub struct Recovery;
 25: impl Recovery {
 26:     pub async fn recover_on_startup(home: &Path, wal: &Wal, state: &AppState) -> Result<Vec<RecoveredVm>> {
 27:         let mut recovered = Vec::new();
 28:         let vms_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms");
 29:         if !vms_dir.exists() {
 30:             info!("No vms directory found, skipping recovery");
 31:             return Ok(recovered);
 32:         }
 33:         let active_vms: HashMap<String, u64> = wal.get_active_vm_ids()
 34:             .into_iter()
 35:             .collect();
 36:         if active_vms.is_empty() {
 37:             info!("No active VMs in WAL, skipping recovery");
 38:             return Ok(recovered);
 39:         }
 40:         info!("WAL indicates {} active VMs to check", active_vms.len());
 41:         for (vm_id, _) in active_vms {
 42:             let vm_dir = vms_dir.join(&vm_id);
 43:             if !vm_dir.exists() {
 44:                 warn!("VM {} active in WAL but directory missing, skipping", vm_id);
 45:                 continue;
 46:             }
 47: let state_file = vm_dir.join("state.json");
 48:             let vm_state = if !state_file.exists() {
 49:                 warn!("No state.json for VM {}, attempting to reconstruct from directory", vm_id);
 50:                 match Self::reconstruct_vm_state(&vm_id, &vm_dir).await {
 51:                     Ok(state) => {
 52:                         info!("Successfully reconstructed state for VM {}", vm_id);
 53:                         state
 54:                     }
 55:                     Err(e) => {
 56:                         error!("Failed to reconstruct state for VM {}: {}", vm_id, e);
 57:                         let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock");
 58:                         if ch_socket.exists() {
 59:                             warn!("VM {} CH socket still present, skipping cleanup to preserve for manual inspection", vm_id);
 60:                             continue;
 61:                         }
 62:                         error!("Catastrophic: VM {} has no state.json and CH socket is gone, manual intervention required", vm_id);
 63:                         continue;
 64:                     }
 65:                 }
 66:             } else {
 67:                 let state_content = match std::fs::read_to_string(&state_file) {
 68:                     Ok(c) => c,
 69:                     Err(e) => {
 70:                         error!("Failed to read state for {}: {}", vm_id, e);
 71:                         continue;
 72:                     }
 73:                 };
 74:                 match serde_json::from_str::<super::VmState>(&state_content) {
 75:                     Ok(s) => s,
 76:                     Err(e) => {
 77:                         error!("Failed to parse state for {}: {}", vm_id, e);
 78:                         continue;
 79:                     }
 80:                 }
 81:             };
 82:             let status = Self::check_vm_status(&vm_id, &vm_dir).await;
 83:             match status {
 84:                 VmRecoveryStatus::Running => {
 85:                     info!("VM {} is ALIVE. Adopting...", vm_id);
 86:                     recovered.push(RecoveredVm {
 87:                         vm_id: vm_id.clone(),
 88:                         state: vm_state,
 89:                         status: VmRecoveryStatus::Running,
 90:                     });
 91:                 }
 92:                 VmRecoveryStatus::Crashed => {
 93:                     warn!("VM {} is DEAD. Cleaning up orphaned resources...", vm_id);
 94:                     if let Err(e) = Self::cleanup_orphaned_resources(&vm_dir).await {
 95:                         error!("Failed to cleanup {}: {}", vm_id, e);
 96:                     }
 97:                 }
 98:                 _ => {
 99:                     info!("VM {} status: {:?}", vm_id, status);
100:                 }
101:             }
102:         }
103:         info!("Recovery complete: {} VMs adopted", recovered.len());
104:         Ok(recovered)
105:     }
106:     async fn check_vm_status(vm_id: &str, vm_dir: &Path) -> VmRecoveryStatus {
107:         let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock");
108:         if !ch_socket.exists() {
109:             return VmRecoveryStatus::Crashed;
110:         }
111:         let socket_path = ch_socket.to_string_lossy().to_string();
112:         let check_future = async {
113:             let vmm = VmmManager::new(&socket_path);
114:             vmm.check_alive().await
115:         };
116:         match timeout(Duration::from_secs(3), check_future).await {
117:             Ok(true) => VmRecoveryStatus::Running,
118:             Ok(false) => VmRecoveryStatus::Crashed,
119:             Err(_) => {
120:                 warn!("VM {} check timed out", vm_id);
121:                 VmRecoveryStatus::Unknown
122:             }
123:         }
124:     }
125:     pub async fn cleanup_orphaned_resources(vm_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {
126:         use vyoma_core::network::NetworkManager;
127:         use vyoma_core::storage::StorageManager;
128:         use vyoma_core::cgroups::CgroupManager;
129:         info!("Cleaning up orphaned resources in {:?}", vm_dir);
130:         let state_file = vm_dir.join("state.json");
131:         if !state_file.exists() {
132:             return Ok(());
133:         }
134:         let state_content = std::fs::read_to_string(&state_file)?;
135:         let state: VmState = serde_json::from_str(&state_content)?;
136:         if !state.tap_name.is_empty() {
137:             if let Err(e) = NetworkManager::remove_interface(&state.tap_name) {
138:                 warn!("Failed to remove TAP {}: {}", state.tap_name, e);
139:             }
140:         }
141:         if !state.dm_name.is_empty() {
142:             if let Err(e) = StorageManager::remove_dm_device(&state.dm_name) {
143:                 warn!("Failed to remove DM {}: {}", state.dm_name, e);
144:             }
145:         }
146:         for dev in &state.loop_devices {
147:             if let Err(e) = StorageManager::detach_loop_device(dev) {
148:                 warn!("Failed to detach loop {}: {}", dev, e);
149:             }
150:         }
151:         if let Some(ref cgroup_path) = state.cgroup_path {
152:             let cm = CgroupManager::new();
153:             if let Err(e) = cm.remove_vm_cgroup(&state.id) {
154:                 warn!("Failed to remove cgroup: {}", e);
155:             }
156:         }
157:         info!("Orphaned resource cleanup complete for {}", state.id);
158:         Ok(())
159:     }
160:     async fn reconstruct_vm_state(vm_id: &str, vm_dir: &Path) -> Result<super::VmState> {
161:         let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock");
162:         if !ch_socket.exists() {
163:             return Err(anyhow!("CH socket not found, cannot reconstruct state"));
164:         }
165:         let status = Self::check_vm_status(vm_id, vm_dir).await;
166:         if !matches!(status, VmRecoveryStatus::Running) {
167:             return Err(anyhow!("VM is not running, cannot reconstruct state"));
168:         }
169:         Ok(super::VmState {
170:             id: vm_id.to_string(),
171:             tap_name: String::new(),
172:             dm_name: String::new(),
173:             loop_devices: Vec::new(),
174:             cow_file_path: String::new(),
175:             ip_address: "10.0.0.2".to_string(),
176:             cgroup_path: None,
177:             netns_path: None,
178:             ports: Vec::new(),
179:             volumes: Vec::new(),
180:             hostname: None,
181:             labels: std::collections::HashMap::new(),
182:             base_image_path: String::new(),
183:             vcpu: 0,
184:             mem_size_mib: 0,
185:             networks: Vec::new(),
186:             status: super::VmStatus::Running,
187:         })
188:     }
189: }

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/vm_service/network.rs
================
  1: //! Network setup for VM instances
  2: //!
  3: //! # Network Namespace Handling
  4: //!
  5: //! Network namespace creation now uses vyoma-net's NetNsManager module.
  6: //! This provides a cleaner API while still using ip netns under the hood
  7: //! for persistent namespace management.
  8: //!
  9: //! The implementation:
 10: //! - Does NOT use sudo - relies on CAP_NET_ADMIN capability instead
 11: //! - Uses vyoma-net's netns module for consistent API
 12: use anyhow::{Context, Result};
 13: use tracing::{info, warn};
 14: use vyoma_net::{create_netns, delete_netns};
 15: use super::types::{VmNetworkConfig, NetworkInfo};
 16: use crate::state::AppState;
 17: pub async fn setup_network(
 18:     state: &AppState,
 19:     vm_id: &str,
 20:     networks: &[String],
 21: ) -> Result<VmNetworkConfig> {
 22:     setup_privileged_network(state, vm_id, networks).await
 23: }
 24: async fn setup_privileged_network(
 25:     state: &AppState,
 26:     vm_id: &str,
 27:     networks: &[String],
 28: ) -> Result<VmNetworkConfig> {
 29:     let netns_name = format!("vm-{}", vm_id);
 30:     let netns_path = format!("/var/run/netns/{}", netns_name);
 31:     std::fs::create_dir_all("/var/run/netns")
 32:         .context("Failed to create /var/run/netns")?;
 33:     // Use vyoma-net's netns module instead of direct ip command
 34:     if let Err(e) = create_netns(&netns_name) {
 35:         warn!("netns creation warning: {}", e);
 36:     }
 37:     let networks_to_attach: Vec<String> = if networks.is_empty() {
 38:         vec!["default".to_string()]
 39:     } else {
 40:         networks.to_vec()
 41:     };
 42:     let attachments = state
 43:         .cni_manager
 44:         .add_multiple(&networks_to_attach, vm_id, &netns_path)
 45:         .context("CNI ADD failed")?;
 46:     let mut network_infos = Vec::new();
 47:     for (idx, attachment) in attachments.iter().enumerate() {
 48:         info!(
 49:             "CNI: Attached {} to network '{}' with IP {}",
 50:             attachment.interface_name, attachment.network_name, attachment.result.ip_address
 51:         );
 52:         let tap_name = if idx == 0 {
 53:             format!("tap{}", &vm_id[0..8])
 54:         } else {
 55:             format!("tap{}-{}", &vm_id[0..8], idx)
 56:         };
 57:         network_infos.push(NetworkInfo {
 58:             ip: attachment.result.ip_address.clone(),
 59:             tap_name,
 60:             gateway: attachment.result.gateway.clone(),
 61:             interface_name: attachment.interface_name.clone(),
 62:             network_name: attachment.network_name.clone(),
 63:         });
 64:     }
 65:     if network_infos.is_empty() {
 66:         anyhow::bail!("No networks could be attached to VM");
 67:     }
 68:     let ip_address = network_infos.first().map(|n| n.ip.clone())
 69:         .unwrap_or_else(|| "10.0.2.15".to_string());
 70:     let primary_tap = network_infos.first().map(|n| n.tap_name.clone())
 71:         .unwrap_or_else(|| "tap0".to_string());
 72:     let gateway = network_infos.first()
 73:         .and_then(|n| n.gateway.clone())
 74:         .unwrap_or_else(|| "172.16.0.1".to_string());
 75:     Ok(VmNetworkConfig {
 76:         ip_address,
 77:         primary_tap,
 78:         gateway,
 79:         network_infos,
 80:         netns_path: Some(netns_path),
 81:     })
 82: }
 83: pub async fn cleanup_network(
 84:     state: &AppState,
 85:     vm_id: &str,
 86:     networks: &[String],
 87:     netns_path: &Option<String>,
 88: ) -> Result<()> {
 89:     if let Some(ns) = netns_path {
 90:         let netns_name = format!("vm-{}", vm_id);
 91:         if !networks.is_empty() {
 92:             let _ = state.cni_manager.del_multiple(networks, vm_id, ns);
 93:         } else {
 94:             let _ = state.cni_manager.del(None, vm_id, ns, "eth0");
 95:         }
 96:         // Use vyoma-net's netns module for cleanup
 97:         let _ = delete_netns(&netns_name);
 98:     }
 99:     Ok(())
100: }

 1: #!/bin/bash
 2: set -e
 3: source tests/e2e/common.sh
 4: echo "=== Test 01: VM Lifecycle ==="
 5: check_root
 6: setup_env
 7: # Start Daemon
 8: echo "Starting Daemon..."
 9: sudo -E $VYOMAD_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3001 > $TEST_HOME/daemon.log 2>&1 &
10: DAEMON_PID=$!
11: sleep 3
12: # Helper
13: VYOMA="$VYOMA_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3001"
14: # 1. Pull
15: echo "Pulling image..."
16: $VYOMA pull alpine:latest || { echo "Pull failed (network issue?)"; exit 1; }
17: assert_success "Image Pull"
18: # 2. Run
19: echo "Running VM..."
20: # We need to capture ID. The CLI might print a message.
21: # For Test Script reliability, having CLI output JSON is better, but currently it prints text.
22: # We will just run it and check PS.
23: # Use a long-running command to keep VM alive for pause/resume tests
24: $VYOMA run alpine:latest --vcpu 1 --memory 128 --hostname test-vm
25: assert_success "Run Request"
26: # Quick pause/resume (within 2 seconds - Alpine's /bin/sh exits quickly)
27: sleep 1
28: # 5. Pause/Resume (Must be done quickly before VM exits)
29: echo "Pausing VM..."
30: VM_ID=$($VYOMA ps | grep "test-vm" | awk '{print $1}')
31: echo "VM ID: $VM_ID"
32: if [ -n "$VM_ID" ]; then
33:     $VYOMA pause $VM_ID
34:     assert_success "Pause VM"
35:     echo "Resuming VM..."
36:     $VYOMA resume $VM_ID
37:     assert_success "Resume VM"
38: fi
39: sleep 4
40: # 3. PS
41: echo "Listing VMs..."
42: $VYOMA ps
43: if $VYOMA ps | grep -q "test-vm"; then
44:     echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: VM found in PS${NC}"
45: else
46:     echo -e "${RED}Fail: VM not found${NC}"
47:     exit 1
48: fi
49: # 4. Logs (Check output)
50: # $VYOMA logs <id> ... need ID.
51: # Extract ID from PS
52: VM_ID=$($VYOMA ps | grep "test-vm" | awk '{print $1}')
53: echo "VM ID: $VM_ID"
54: echo "Checking Logs (Timeout 5s)..."
55: timeout 5s $VYOMA logs $VM_ID || true
56: assert_success "Logs Retrieval"
57: # 6. Restart (Disabled: Issue #101 - Restart tries to pull local path)
58: # echo "Restarting VM..."
59: # $VYOMA restart $VM_ID
60: # assert_success "Restart VM"
61: # sleep 5
62: # Verify Restart (New PID or VM ID might change? Logic says Restart replaces VM)
63: # VYOMA restart command replaces VM. ID might stay same?
64: # Check PS again.
65: # if $VYOMA ps | grep -q "test-vm"; then
66: #      echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: VM Restarted${NC}"
67: # else
68: #      echo -e "${RED}Fail: VM missing after restart${NC}"
69: #      exit 1
70: # fi
71: # 7. Stop
72: echo "Stopping VM..."
73: $VYOMA stop $VM_ID
74: # Note: If ID changed during restart, we might need to re-fetch ID.
75: # Restart logic in CLI: "Stopping VM... Starting replacement VM".
76: # It prints new VM ID?
77: # We should use Hostname to stop to be safe.
78: $VYOMA stop test-vm || $VYOMA stop $VM_ID || true
79: assert_success "Stop Request"
80: sleep 2
81: if $VYOMA ps | grep -q "$VM_ID"; then
82:     echo -e "${RED}Fail: VM still running${NC}"
83:     # exit 1 (Soft fail, might take time to stop)
84: else 
85:     echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: VM stopped${NC}"
86: fi
87: # Cleanup
88: cleanup_env $DAEMON_PID
89: echo "=== Test 01 Passed ==="

File: tests/e2e/05_swarm.sh
================
 1: #!/bin/bash
 2: set -e
 3: source tests/e2e/common.sh
 4: echo "=== Test 05: Swarm Multi-Node ==="
 5: check_root
 6: # Setup Node 1
 7: echo "Setting up Node 1..."
 8: export TEST_HOME=$(mktemp -d) # HOME 1
 9: HOME1=$TEST_HOME
10: sudo -E HOME=$HOME1 $VYOMAD_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/node1.sock --http-port 3005 > $HOME1/daemon.log 2>&1 &
11: PID1=$!
12: # Setup Node 2
13: echo "Setting up Node 2..."
14: HOME2=$(mktemp -d)
15: sudo -E HOME=$HOME2 VYOMA_DAEMON_PORT=3006 $VYOMAD_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/node2.sock --http-port 3006 > $HOME2/daemon.log 2>&1 &
16: PID2=$!
17: sleep 3
18: VYOMA1="$VYOMA_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/node1.sock --http-port 3005"
19: VYOMA2="$VYOMA_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/node2.sock --http-port 3006"
20: # 1. Init Swarm
21: echo "Initializing Swarm on Node 1..."
22: $VYOMA1 swarm init
23: assert_success "Swarm Init"
24: # 2. Join Swarm
25: echo "Joining Node 2 to Node 1..."
26: # Syntax: vyoma swarm join <IP>
27: IGNITE_DAEMON_PORT=3005 $VYOMA2 swarm join 127.0.0.1
28: assert_success "Swarm Join"
29: # 3. List Nodes
30: echo "Listing Nodes..."
31: NODES=$($VYOMA1 swarm ls)
32: echo "$NODES"
33: if echo "$NODES" | grep -q "machine_id"; then
34:      echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: Nodes listed${NC}"
35: else
36:      echo -e "${RED}Fail: No nodes found${NC}"
37: fi
38: # Cleanup
39: echo "Cleaning up..."
40: kill $PID1 $PID2 || true
41: wait $PID1 $PID2 || true
42: rm -rf $HOME1 $HOME2
43: echo "=== Test 05 Passed ==="

 2: import { useVmList } from '../hooks/useApi';
 3: import { Card, StatusBadge, EmptyState, Loading } from '../components/ui';
 4: export function MicroVMsView() {
 5:   const { data, loading, refetch } = useVmList();
 6:   return (
 7:     <div className="p-8 max-w-6xl mx-auto">
 8:       <header className="flex items-center justify-between mb-8">
 9:         <div>
10:           <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-1">MicroVMs</h2>
11:           <p className="text-sm text-slate-400">Manage your running micro-virtual machines.</p>
12:         </div>
13:         <button
14:           onClick={refetch}
15:           className="p-2 bg-slate-800 border border-slate-700 rounded hover:bg-slate-700 transition text-slate-400 hover:text-white"
16:         >
17:           <RefreshCw size={18} />
18:         </button>
19:       </header>
20:       <Card>
21:         <div className="grid grid-cols-12 gap-4 p-4 border-b border-slate-800 text-xs font-semibold text-slate-500 uppercase tracking-wider">

33:           ) : (

41:                                       vm.status?.startsWith('AttestationFailed') ? 'attestation_failed' :
42:                                       vm.status?.startsWith('PendingAttestation') ? 'pending_attestation' :
43:                                       vm.status?.startsWith('Error') ? 'error' : 'stopped'} />
44:                 </div>
45:                 <div className="col-span-4">
46:                   <div className="font-semibold text-white text-sm">
47:                     {vm.labels['vyoma.service'] || 'MicroVM'}

50:                     {vm.id.substring(0, 12)}
51:                   </div>
52:                 </div>
53:                 <div className="col-span-3 text-sm text-slate-400">
54:                   {vm.labels['image'] || 'alpine'}
55:                 </div>
56:                 <div className="col-span-2 text-sm text-slate-400 font-mono">
57:                   {vm.ip_address}
58:                 </div>
59:                 <div className="col-span-2 flex justify-end gap-2 opacity-60 group-hover:opacity-100 transition">

62:                   </button>

64:                     <Square size={16} />
65:                   </button>
66:                 </div>
67:               </div>
68:             ))

70:         </div>
