================
File: crates/vyoma-storage/src/dm.rs
================
  1: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
  2: use tracing::info;
  3: // Add devicemapper imports
  4: use devicemapper::{DM, DmName, DmOptions, DmUuid, DevId};
  5: use crate::error::{StorageError, Result};
  6: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  7: pub struct DmDevice {
  8:     pub name: String,
  9:     pub path: PathBuf,
 10: }
 11: impl DmDevice {
 12:     pub fn new(name: String, path: PathBuf) -> Self {
 13:         Self { name, path }
 14:     }
 15:     pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {
 16:         &self.path
 17:     }
 18:     pub fn name(&self) -> &str {
 19:         &self.name
 20:     }
 21: }
 22: pub struct DmManager {
 23:     dm: DM,
 24: }
 25: impl DmManager {
 26:     pub fn new() -> Result<Self> {
 27:         info!("Initializing Device Mapper manager");
 28:         let dm = DM::new().map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
 29:         Ok(Self { dm })
 30:     }
 31:     fn device_size_sectors(path: &Path) -> Result<u64> {
 32:         use std::os::unix::fs::MetadataExt;
 33:         let meta = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(StorageError::Io)?;
 34:         let bytes = meta.size();
 35:         Ok(bytes / 512)
 36:     }
 37: pub fn create_snapshot(
 38:     &self,
 39:     name: &str,
 40:     base_dev: &Path,
 41:     cow_dev: &Path,
 42: ) -> Result<DmDevice> {
 43:         info!("Creating snapshot {}: base={:?}, cow={:?}", name, base_dev, cow_dev);
 44:         if !base_dev.exists() {
 45:             return Err(StorageError::NotFound(format!("Base device not found: {:?}", base_dev)));
 46:         }
 47:         if !cow_dev.exists() {
 48:             return Err(StorageError::NotFound(format!("COW device not found: {:?}", cow_dev)));
 49:         }
 50:         let origin_name_str = format!("{}-origin", name);
 51:         let sectors = Self::device_size_sectors(base_dev)?;
 52:         // 1. Create origin linear device via devicemapper API
 53:         let origin_name = DmName::new(&origin_name_str)
 54:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Invalid DM name {}: {}", origin_name_str, e)))?;
 55:         let origin_uuid_str = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
 56:         let origin_uuid = DmUuid::new(&origin_uuid_str)
 57:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Invalid UUID for origin: {}", e)))?;
 58:         let origin_table = vec![(
 59:             0u64,
 60:             sectors,
 61:             "linear".to_string(),
 62:             format!("{} 0", base_dev.display()),
 63:         )];
 64:         self.dm
 65:             .device_create(&origin_name, Some(&origin_uuid), DmOptions::default())
 66:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Failed to create origin device via dm API: {}", e)))?;
 67:         self.dm
 68:             .table_load(&DevId::Name(&origin_name), &origin_table, DmOptions::default())
 69:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Failed to load origin table via dm API: {}", e)))?;
 70:         // 2. Create snapshot device via devicemapper API
 71:         let snap_name = DmName::new(name)
 72:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Invalid DM name {}: {}", name, e)))?;
 73:         let snap_uuid_str = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
 74:         let snap_uuid = DmUuid::new(&snap_uuid_str)
 75:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Invalid UUID for snapshot: {}", e)))?;
 76:         let origin_dev_path = format!("/dev/mapper/{}", origin_name_str);
 77:         let snap_table = vec![(
 78:             0u64,
 79:             sectors,
 80:             "snapshot".to_string(),
 81:             format!("{} {} P 8", origin_dev_path, cow_dev.display()),
 82:         )];
 83:         self.dm
 84:             .device_create(&snap_name, Some(&snap_uuid), DmOptions::default())
 85:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Failed to create snapshot device via dm API: {}", e)))?;
 86:         self.dm
 87:             .table_load(&DevId::Name(&snap_name), &snap_table, DmOptions::default())
 88:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Failed to load snapshot table via dm API: {}", e)))?;
 89:         self.activate_device(&origin_name)?;
 90:         self.activate_device(&snap_name)?;
 91:         let path = PathBuf::from(format!("/dev/mapper/{}", name));
 92:         Ok(DmDevice::new(name.to_string(), path))
 93:     }
 94:     pub fn remove_snapshot(&self, name: &str) -> Result<()> {
 95:         info!("Removing snapshot {}", name);
 96:         let origin_name_str = format!("{}-origin", name);
 97:         // Remove snap first
 98:         let dm_name = DmName::new(name).map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
 99:         self.dm.device_remove(&DevId::Name(dm_name), DmOptions::default())
100:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
101:         // Remove origin
102:         if let Ok(origin_dm_name) = DmName::new(&origin_name_str) {
103:             let _ = self.dm.device_remove(&DevId::Name(origin_dm_name), DmOptions::default());
104:         }
105:         Ok(())
106:     }
107:     pub fn list_devices(&self) -> Result<Vec<DmDevice>> {
108:         info!("Listing device mapper devices");
109:         let devs = self.dm.list_devices().map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
110:         let mut dm_devices = Vec::new();
111:         // Since iterator output in older devicemapper is 2-tuple instead of 3
112:         for item in devs {
113:             let dev_name = item.0;
114:             let path = PathBuf::from(format!("/dev/mapper/{}", dev_name.to_string()));
115:             dm_devices.push(DmDevice::new(dev_name.to_string(), path));
116:         }
117:         Ok(dm_devices)
118:     }
119:     pub fn device_exists(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {
120:         let dm_name = DmName::new(name).map_err(|e| StorageError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
121:         match self.dm.device_info(&DevId::Name(dm_name)) {
122:             Ok(_) => Ok(true),
123:             Err(_) => Ok(false),
124:         }
125:     }
126:     fn activate_device(&self, name: &DmName) -> Result<()> {
127:         let _ = self
128:             .dm
129:             .device_suspend(&DevId::Name(name), DmOptions::default())
130:             .map_err(|e| StorageError::Other(format!("Failed to activate device {}: {}", name, e)))?;
131:         Ok(())
132:     }
133: }

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/swarm/network_integration.rs
================
  1: use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
  2: use std::process::Command;
  3: use std::net::{Ipv4Addr, SocketAddr};
  4: use tracing::{info, warn, error};
  5: use vyoma_net::{WireGuardNode, WireGuardConfig, PeerConfig, add_route_to_peer_endpoint, add_route_to_subnet, remove_route_to_subnet};
  6: use crate::swarm::SwarmSideEffect;
  7: pub struct NetworkIntegration {
  8:     wireguard_node: Arc<Mutex<Option<WireGuardNode>>>,
  9:     data_dir: std::path::PathBuf,
 10: }
 11: impl NetworkIntegration {
 12:     pub fn new(data_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {
 13:         Self {
 14:             wireguard_node: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
 15:             data_dir,
 16:         }
 17:     }
 18:     fn ensure_wireguard(&self, subnet_id: u8) -> bool {
 19:         let mut guard = match self.wireguard_node.lock() {
 20:             Ok(g) => g,
 21:             Err(e) => {
 22:                 warn!("Failed to lock wireguard: {}", e);
 23:                 return false;
 24:             }
 25:         };
 26:         if let Some(ref wg) = *guard {
 27:             if wg.is_running() {
 28:                 return true;
 29:             }
 30:         }
 31:         let key_path = self.data_dir.join("wireguard").join("private.key");
 32:         if let Some(parent) = key_path.parent() {
 33:             if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {
 34:                 warn!("Failed to create wireguard dir: {}", e);
 35:                 return false;
 36:             }
 37:         }
 38:         let node_ip = Ipv4Addr::new(10, 42, subnet_id, 1);
 39:         let mut config = WireGuardConfig::default();
 40:         config.node_ip = Some(node_ip);
 41:         match WireGuardNode::from_key(key_path, config) {
 42:             Ok(mut node) => {
 43:                 if node.start().is_ok() {
 44:                     info!("WireGuard started on subnet 10.42.{}.0/24", subnet_id);
 45:                     *guard = Some(node);
 46:                     return true;
 47:                 }
 48:             }
 49:             Err(e) => {
 50:                 warn!("Failed to init WireGuard: {}", e);
 51:             }
 52:         }
 53:         false
 54:     }
 55:     pub fn setup_local_node(&self, node_id: u64, subnet_id: u8, peers: &[crate::swarm::NodeInfo]) {
 56:         info!("Setting up local node {} with subnet 10.42.{}.0/24", node_id, subnet_id);
 57:         self.ensure_wireguard(subnet_id);
 58:         self.configure_bridge(subnet_id);
 59:         self.ensure_vxlan_device();
 60:         for peer in peers {
 61:             if let (Some(wg_key), Some(wg_port)) = (&peer.wireguard_key, peer.wireguard_port) {
 62:                 if let Some(addr) = peer.addr.split(':').next() {
 63:                     self.add_wireguard_peer(
 64:                         wg_key,
 65:                         &format!("{}:{}", addr, wg_port),
 66:                         &format!("10.42.{}.0/24", peer.subnet_id.unwrap_or(0)),
 67:                     );
 68:                 }
 69:             }
 70:             if let Some(addr) = peer.addr.split(':').next() {
 71:                 self.add_vxlan_route(addr, peer.subnet_id.unwrap_or(0));
 72:             }
 73:         }
 74:         info!("Local node setup complete");
 75:     }
 76:     pub fn add_node(&self, node_id: u64, addr: &str, wireguard_key: Option<&str>, wireguard_port: Option<u16>, subnet_id: u8) {
 77:         info!("Adding node {} at {} to network (subnet: 10.42.{}.0/24)", node_id, addr, subnet_id);
 78:         if let (Some(key), Some(port)) = (wireguard_key, wireguard_port) {
 79:             self.add_wireguard_peer(key, &format!("{}:{}", addr, port), &format!("10.42.{}.0/24", subnet_id));
 80:         }
 81:         self.add_vxlan_route(addr, subnet_id);
 82:     }
 83:     pub fn remove_node(&self, node_id: u64, subnet_id: u8) {
 84:         info!("Removing node {} from network (subnet: 10.42.{}.0/24)", node_id, subnet_id);
 85:         self.remove_subnet_route(subnet_id);
 86:     }
 87:     pub fn update_node(&self, _node_id: u64, old_subnet_id: u8, new_addr: Option<&str>, new_wg_key: Option<&str>, new_wg_port: Option<u16>, _new_subnet_id: u8) {
 88:         if new_addr.is_some() || new_wg_key.is_some() {
 89:             if let Some(addr) = new_addr {
 90:                 if let (Some(key), Some(port)) = (new_wg_key, new_wg_port) {
 91:                     self.add_wireguard_peer(key, &format!("{}:{}", addr, port), &format!("10.42.{}.0/24", old_subnet_id));
 92:                 }
 93:             }
 94:         }
 95:     }
 96:     fn add_wireguard_peer(&self, public_key: &str, endpoint: &str, allowed_ips: &str) {
 97:         let mut guard = match self.wireguard_node.lock() {
 98:             Ok(g) => g,
 99:             Err(e) => {
100:                 warn!("Failed to lock wireguard: {}", e);
101:                 return;
102:             }
103:         };
104:         if let Some(ref mut wg) = *guard {
105:             if wg.is_running() {
106:                 let endpoint_addr: SocketAddr = match endpoint.parse() {
107:                     Ok(addr) => addr,
108:                     Err(e) => {
109:                         warn!("Invalid endpoint {}: {}", endpoint, e);
110:                         return;
111:                     }
112:                 };
113:                 let peer_config = PeerConfig::new(public_key.to_string(), endpoint_addr)
114:                     .with_allowed_ips(vec![allowed_ips.to_string()]);
115:                 match wg.add_peer(peer_config) {
116:                     Ok(_) => info!("Added WireGuard peer: {} at {}", public_key, endpoint),
117:                     Err(e) => warn!("Failed to add WG peer: {}", e),
118:                 }
119:             }
120:         } else {
121:             warn!("WireGuard not available, skipping peer addition");
122:         }
123:     }
124:     fn add_vxlan_route(&self, peer_ip: &str, subnet_id: u8) {
125:         let subnet = format!("10.42.{}.0/24", subnet_id);
126:         let wg_iface = "vyoma-wg0";
127:         {
128:             let guard = match self.wireguard_node.lock() {
129:                 Ok(g) => g,
130:                 Err(e) => {
131:                     warn!("Failed to lock wireguard: {}", e);
132:                     return;
133:                 }
134:             };
135:             if let Some(ref wg) = *guard {
136:                 if wg.is_running() {
137:                     if let Some(handle) = wg.get_rt_handle() {
138:                         let _ = add_route_to_peer_endpoint(handle, peer_ip, wg_iface);
139:                         let _ = add_route_to_subnet(handle, &subnet, peer_ip, wg_iface);
140:                     }
141:                     info!("Added route to {} via WireGuard", subnet);
142:                     return;
143:                 }
144:             }
145:         }
146:         let if_name = "vyoma-vxlan";
147:         let vxlan_id = "42";
148:         info!("Adding route to {} via VXLAN -> {}", subnet, peer_ip);
149:         let res = Command::new("ip")
150:             .args(&[
151:                 "route", "add", &subnet,
152:                 "encap", "vxlan", "id", vxlan_id, "dst", peer_ip,
153:                 "dev", if_name
154:             ])
155:             .output();
156:         match res {
157:             Ok(out) => {
158:                 if !out.status.success() {
159:                     let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr);
160:                     if !stderr.contains("File exists") && !stderr.contains("exists") {
161:                         warn!("Route add failed: {}", stderr);
162:                     }
163:                 } else {
164:                     info!("Added VXLAN route to {}", subnet);
165:                 }
166:             }
167:             Err(e) => error!("Failed to add route: {}", e),
168:         }
169:     }
170:     fn remove_subnet_route(&self, subnet_id: u8) {
171:         let subnet = format!("10.42.{}.0/24", subnet_id);
172:         let handle = {
173:             let guard = match self.wireguard_node.lock() {
174:                 Ok(g) => g,
175:                 Err(_) => return,
176:             };
177:             if let Some(ref wg) = *guard {
178:                 wg.get_rt_handle().map(|h| h.clone())
179:             } else {
180:                 None
181:             }
182:         };
183:         if let Some(handle) = handle {
184:             if let Err(e) = remove_route_to_subnet(&handle, &subnet) {
185:                 warn!("Failed to remove subnet route {}: {:?}", subnet, e);
186:             } else {
187:                 info!("Removed route for subnet {}", subnet);
188:             }
189:         }
190:     }
191:     fn configure_bridge(&self, subnet_id: u8) {
192:         let subnet = format!("10.42.{}.0/24", subnet_id);
193:         let home = match dirs::home_dir() {
194:             Some(h) => h,
195:             None => {
196:                 warn!("No home dir found");
197:                 return;
198:             }
199:         };
200:         let cni_config_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("cni").join("net.d");
201:         let bridge_conf = cni_config_dir.join("10-vyoma-bridge.conf");
202:         if !cni_config_dir.exists() {
203:             if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&cni_config_dir) {
204:                 warn!("Failed to create CNI config dir: {}", e);
205:                 return;
206:             }
207:         }
208:         let conf = serde_json::json!({
209:             "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
210:             "name": "vyoma-net",
211:             "type": "bridge",
212:             "bridge": "ign0",
213:             "isGateway": true,
214:             "ipMasq": true,
215:             "ipam": {
216:                 "type": "host-local",
217:                 "subnet": subnet,
218:                 "routes": [ { "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" } ]
219:             }
220:         });
221:         if let Ok(f) = std::fs::File::create(&bridge_conf) {
222:             if let Err(e) = serde_json::to_writer_pretty(f, &conf) {
223:                 warn!("Failed to write CNI config: {}", e);
224:             } else {
225:                 info!("Configured bridge for subnet {}", subnet);
226:             }
227:         }
228:     }
229:     fn ensure_vxlan_device(&self) {
230:         let if_name = "vyoma-vxlan";
231:         let output = Command::new("ip").args(&["link", "show", if_name]).output();
232:         if let Ok(o) = output {
233:             if o.status.success() { return; }
234:         }
235:         info!("Creating VXLAN device {}", if_name);
236:         let _ = Command::new("ip")
237:             .args(&["link", "add", if_name, "type", "vxlan", "id", "42", "dstport", "4789", "external"])
238:             .output();
239:         let _ = Command::new("ip")
240:             .args(&["link", "set", if_name, "up"])
241:             .output();
242:     }
243:     pub fn shutdown(&self) {
244:         info!("Shutting down NetworkIntegration...");
245:         if let Ok(mut guard) = self.wireguard_node.lock() {
246:             if let Some(mut wg) = guard.take() {
247:                 let _ = wg.stop();
248:             }
249:         }
250:         info!("NetworkIntegration shutdown complete");
251:     }
252:     pub fn get_local_wireguard_ip(&self) -> Option<Ipv4Addr> {
253:         self.wireguard_node
254:             .lock()
255:             .ok()
256:             .and_then(|guard| guard.as_ref().and_then(|wg| wg.config.node_ip))
257:     }
258: }
259: impl Clone for NetworkIntegration {
260:     fn clone(&self) -> Self {
261:         Self {
262:             wireguard_node: self.wireguard_node.clone(),
263:             data_dir: self.data_dir.clone(),
264:         }
265:     }
266: }
267: pub fn create_network_callback(
268:     network_integration: NetworkIntegration,
269: ) -> Box<dyn Fn(&SwarmSideEffect) + Send + Sync> {
270:     Box::new(move |effect| {
271:         match effect {
272:             SwarmSideEffect::LocalNodeConfigured { node_id, subnet_id, peers } => {
273:                 network_integration.setup_local_node(*node_id, *subnet_id, peers);
274:             }
275:             SwarmSideEffect::NodeAdded { node_id, addr, wireguard_key, wireguard_port, subnet_id } => {
276:                 network_integration.add_node(
277:                     *node_id,
278:                     addr,
279:                     wireguard_key.as_deref(),
280:                     *wireguard_port,
281:                     *subnet_id,
282:                 );
283:             }
284:             SwarmSideEffect::NodeRemoved { node_id, subnet_id } => {
285:                 network_integration.remove_node(*node_id, *subnet_id);
286:             }
287:             SwarmSideEffect::NodeUpdated { node_id, old_subnet_id, new_addr, new_wireguard_key, new_wireguard_port } => {
288:                 network_integration.update_node(
289:                     *node_id,
290:                     *old_subnet_id,
291:                     new_addr.as_deref(),
292:                     new_wireguard_key.as_deref(),
293:                     *new_wireguard_port,
294:                     *old_subnet_id,
295:                 );
296:             }
297:         }
298:     })
299: }

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/grpc.rs
================
  1: use std::sync::Arc;
  2: use tonic::{Request, Response, Status};
  3: use tracing::{info, error};
  4: use vyoma_proto::v1::vm_service_server::VmService;
  5: use vyoma_proto::v1::{
  6:     CreateVmRequest, CreateVmResponse, ExecOutput, ExecRequest, ListVmsRequest, ListVmsResponse,
  7:     LogLine, LogRequest, MigrateRequest, MigrationProgress, PortMapping as ProtoPortMapping,
  8:     RestoreRequest, SnapshotInfo, SnapshotRequest, VmIdRequest, VmInfo as ProtoVmInfo,
  9:     VmStatusResponse, VolumeMapping as ProtoVolumeMapping,
 10: };
 11: use crate::state::AppState;
 12: use crate::vm_service;
 13: use crate::vm_service::types::VmRunRequest;
 14: use vyoma_core::api::{PortMapping, VolumeMount};
 15: pub struct GrpcVmService {
 16:     state: Arc<AppState>,
 17: }
 18: impl GrpcVmService {
 19:     pub fn new(state: Arc<AppState>) -> Self {
 20:         Self { state }
 21:     }
 22: }
 23: #[tonic::async_trait]
 24: impl VmService for GrpcVmService {
 25:     async fn create_vm(
 26:         &self,
 27:         request: Request<CreateVmRequest>,
 28:     ) -> Result<Response<CreateVmResponse>, Status> {
 29:         let req = request.into_inner();
 30:         info!("gRPC: Request to create VM: {} (image: {})", req.name, req.image);
 31:         // Convert protobuf types to internal types with validation
 32:         let ports: Vec<PortMapping> = req.ports.into_iter().map(|p| {
 33:             let host_port = u16::try_from(p.host)
 34:                 .map_err(|_| Status::invalid_argument(format!("host port {} out of range", p.host)))?;
 35:             let vm_port = u16::try_from(p.vm)
 36:                 .map_err(|_| Status::invalid_argument(format!("vm port {} out of range", p.vm)))?;
 37:             Ok(PortMapping { host_port, vm_port })
 38:         }).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, Status>>()?;
 39:         let volumes: Vec<VolumeMount> = req.volumes.into_iter().map(|v| VolumeMount {
 40:             host_path: v.host_path,
 41:             vm_path: v.vm_path,
 42:             read_only: false, // default to read-write for CRI
 43:         }).collect();
 44:         // Build labels - include name as a label
 45:         let mut labels = std::collections::HashMap::new();
 46:         if !req.name.is_empty() {
 47:             labels.insert("name".to_string(), req.name.clone());
 48:             labels.insert("vyoma.service".to_string(), req.name.clone());
 49:         }
 50:         let vm_request = VmRunRequest {
 51:             image: req.image.clone(),
 52:             vcpu: req.vcpus as u32,
 53:             mem_size_mib: req.memory_mb as u32,
 54:             ports,
 55:             volumes,
 56:             hostname: if req.name.is_empty() { None } else { Some(req.name.clone()) },
 57:             networks: req.networks.clone(),
 58:             labels,
 59:             base_image_path: String::new(), // will be resolved during creation
 60:         };
 61:         let state = Arc::clone(&self.state);
 62:         match vm_service::run_vm(state, vm_request).await {
 63:             Ok(result) => {
 64:                 info!("gRPC: VM created successfully: {}", result.vm_id);
 65:                 Ok(Response::new(CreateVmResponse {
 66:                     vm_id: result.vm_id,
 67:                 }))
 68:             }
 69:             Err(e) => {
 70:                 error!("gRPC: Failed to create VM: {}", e);
 71:                 Err(Status::internal(format!("Failed to create VM: {}", e)))
 72:             }
 73:         }
 74:     }
 75:     async fn start_vm(
 76:         &self,
 77:         request: Request<VmIdRequest>,
 78:     ) -> Result<Response<VmStatusResponse>, Status> {
 79:         let req = request.into_inner();
 80:         let vm_id = req.vm_id;
 81:         info!("gRPC: Request to start VM: {}", vm_id);
 82:         // Check if VM exists - since run_vm already boots, just verify it exists
 83:         let exists = {
 84:             let vms = self.state.vms.lock().await;
 85:             vms.contains_key(&vm_id)
 86:         };
 87:         if exists {
 88:             Ok(Response::new(VmStatusResponse {
 89:                 vm_id,
 90:                 status: "Running".to_string(),
 91:             }))
 92:         } else {
 93:             Err(Status::not_found("VM not found"))
 94:         }
 95:     }
 96:     async fn stop_vm(
 97:         &self,
 98:         request: Request<VmIdRequest>,
 99:     ) -> Result<Response<VmStatusResponse>, Status> {
100:         let req = request.into_inner();
101:         let id = req.vm_id;
102:         info!("gRPC: Request to stop VM: {}", id);
103:         let vm_arc = {
104:             let mut vms = self.state.vms.lock().await;
105:             vms.remove(&id)
106:         };
107:         if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
108:             let mut vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
109:             vm.cleanup(&self.state.cni_manager).await;
110:             if let Err(e) = self.state.wal.append(&crate::state::wal::WalEntry::vm_stop(id.clone())) {
111:                 error!("Failed to write WAL entry: {}", e);
112:             }
113:             let _ = self.state.events_tx.send(serde_json::json!({
114:                 "type": "vm_stop",
115:                 "id": id
116:             }).to_string());
117:             Ok(Response::new(VmStatusResponse {
118:                 vm_id: id,
119:                 status: "stopped".to_string()
120:             }))
121:         } else {
122:             Err(Status::not_found("VM not found"))
123:         }
124:     }
125:     async fn delete_vm(
126:         &self,
127:         request: Request<VmIdRequest>,
128:     ) -> Result<Response<()>, Status> {
129:         let req = request.into_inner();
130:         let vm_id = req.vm_id;
131:         info!("gRPC: Request to delete VM: {}", vm_id);
132:         // Use the vm_service::state::stop_vm function
133:         match crate::vm_service::state::stop_vm(&self.state, &vm_id).await {
134:             Ok(_) => {
135:                 info!("gRPC: VM deleted successfully: {}", vm_id);
136:                 Ok(Response::new(()))
137:             }
138:             Err(e) => {
139:                 error!("gRPC: Failed to delete VM: {}", e);
140:                 Err(Status::internal(format!("Failed to delete VM: {}", e)))
141:             }
142:         }
143:     }
144:     async fn list_vms(
145:         &self,
146:         _request: Request<ListVmsRequest>,
147:     ) -> Result<Response<ListVmsResponse>, Status> {
148:         let instances: Vec<std::sync::Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<crate::state::VmInstance>>> = {
149:             let vms_map = self.state.vms.lock().await;
150:             vms_map.values().cloned().collect()
151:         };
152:         let mut vms = Vec::new();
153:         for arc_inst in instances {
154:             let inst = arc_inst.lock().await;
155:             vms.push(ProtoVmInfo {
156:                 id: inst.id.clone(),
157:                 image: inst.base_image_path.clone(),
158:                 status: "running".to_string(),
159:                 ip: inst.ip_address.clone(),
160:                 vcpus: inst.vcpu,
161:                 memory_mb: inst.mem_size_mib as u64,
162:                 ports: vec![],
163:                 created_at: 0,
164:             });
165:         }
166:         Ok(Response::new(ListVmsResponse { vms }))
167:     }
168:     async fn get_vm(
169:         &self,
170:         request: Request<VmIdRequest>,
171:     ) -> Result<Response<ProtoVmInfo>, Status> {
172:         let req = request.into_inner();
173:         let vm_arc = {
174:             let vms = self.state.vms.lock().await;
175:             vms.get(&req.vm_id).cloned()
176:         };
177:         if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
178:             let inst = vm_mutex.lock().await;
179:             let info = ProtoVmInfo {
180:                 id: inst.id.clone(),
181:                 image: inst.base_image_path.clone(),
182:                 status: "running".to_string(),
183:                 ip: inst.ip_address.clone(),
184:                 vcpus: inst.vcpu,
185:                 memory_mb: inst.mem_size_mib as u64,
186:                 ports: vec![],
187:                 created_at: 0,
188:             };
189:             Ok(Response::new(info))
190:         } else {
191:             Err(Status::not_found("VM not found"))
192:         }
193:     }
194:     type ExecCommandStream = tonic::codegen::BoxStream<ExecOutput>;
195:     async fn exec_command(
196:         &self,
197:         request: Request<ExecRequest>,
198:     ) -> Result<Response<Self::ExecCommandStream>, Status> {
199:         Err(Status::unimplemented("Not implemented"))
200:     }
201:     type StreamLogsStream = tonic::codegen::BoxStream<LogLine>;
202:     async fn stream_logs(
203:         &self,
204:         request: Request<LogRequest>,
205:     ) -> Result<Response<Self::StreamLogsStream>, Status> {
206:         Err(Status::unimplemented("Not implemented"))
207:     }
208:     async fn create_snapshot(
209:         &self,
210:         request: Request<SnapshotRequest>,
211:     ) -> Result<Response<SnapshotInfo>, Status> {
212:         Err(Status::unimplemented("Not implemented"))
213:     }
214:     async fn restore_snapshot(
215:         &self,
216:         request: Request<RestoreRequest>,
217:     ) -> Result<Response<ProtoVmInfo>, Status> {
218:         Err(Status::unimplemented("Not implemented"))
219:     }
220:     type MigrateVmStream = tonic::codegen::BoxStream<MigrationProgress>;
221:     async fn migrate_vm(
222:         &self,
223:         request: Request<MigrateRequest>,
224:     ) -> Result<Response<Self::MigrateVmStream>, Status> {
225:         Err(Status::unimplemented("Not implemented"))
226:     }
227: }

================
File: tests/e2e/04_compose.sh
================
 1: #!/bin/bash
 2: set -e
 3: source tests/e2e/common.sh
 4: echo "=== Test 04: Compose (vyoma up) ==="
 5: check_root
 6: setup_env
 7: # Start Daemon
 8: echo "Starting Daemon (3004)..."
 9: sudo -E $VYOMAD_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3004 > $TEST_HOME/daemon.log 2>&1 &
10: DAEMON_PID=$!
11: sleep 3
12: VYOMA="$VYOMA_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3004"
13: # 1. Setup Compose File
14: mkdir -p $TEST_HOME/compose_test
15: cd $TEST_HOME/compose_test
16: cat <<EOF > vyoma-compose.yml
17: version: '1'
18: services:
19:   web:
20:     image: alpine:latest
21:     cpus: 1
22:     memory: 128
23:   db:
24:     image: alpine:latest
25:     cpus: 1
26:     memory: 128
27: EOF
28: # 2. Up
29: echo "Running vyoma up..."
30: $VYOMA up -d
31: assert_success "Compose Up"
32: sleep 5
33: # 3. Verify
34: echo "Verifying Services..."
35: PS=$($VYOMA ps)
36: echo "$PS"
37: if echo "$PS" | grep -q "web" && echo "$PS" | grep -q "db"; then
38:     echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: Services Running${NC}"
39: else
40:     echo -e "${RED}Fail: Services missing${NC}"
41:     exit 1
42: fi
43: # 3.5 Scale
44: echo "Scaling web=2..."
45: # Command: vyoma scale web=2
46: $VYOMA scale web=2
47: assert_success "Scale Request"
48: sleep 5
49: PS_SCALE=$($VYOMA ps)
50: # grep -c "web" should be 2?
51: # The service name might be "compose_test_web_1", "compose_test_web_2".
52: # Labels will prevent collision?
53: # Implementation of "scale" CLI was: "vyoma scale <service>=<count>".
54: # It calls "vyoma run" repeatedly?
55: # Let's verify scaling took effect.
56: if [ $(echo "$PS_SCALE" | grep -c "web") -ge 2 ]; then
57:      echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: Scaled to 2 instances${NC}"
58: else
59:      echo -e "${RED}Fail: Scale failed${NC}"
60: fi
61: # 4. Down
62: echo "Running vyoma down..."
63: $VYOMA down
64: assert_success "Compose Down"
65: sleep 2
66: if $VYOMA ps | grep -q "web"; then
67:     echo -e "${RED}Fail: Services still running${NC}"
68:     # exit 1
69: else
70:     echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: Services Removed${NC}"
71: fi
72: cleanup_env $DAEMON_PID
73: echo "=== Test 04 Passed ==="

   1: use anyhow::Result;
   2: use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
   3: use colored::Colorize;
   4: use flate2::read::GzDecoder;
   5: use flate2::write::GzEncoder;
   6: use flate2::Compression;
   7: use futures::stream::StreamExt;
   8: use reqwest::{Client, StatusCode};
   9: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
  10: use std::collections::HashMap;
  11: use std::fs::File;
  12: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
  13: use tar::Archive; // Removed Builder since we will use tar::Builder inline
  14: use tracing::{error, info, warn};
  15: use vyoma_compose::VyomaCompose;
  16: use vyoma_core::api::PortMapping;
  17: use vyoma_core::api::VolumeMount;
  18: use vyoma_image::signing::TrustPolicy;
  19: use vyoma_image::SignedManifest;
  20: /// Resolve socket path with fallback to user-writable location
  21: fn resolve_socket_path(default_path: &str) -> String {
  22:     // Try default path first
  23:     if Path::new(default_path).exists() {
  24:         return default_path.to_string();
  25:     }
  26:     // Fallback to user-specific runtime directory
  27:     if let Ok(xdg) = std::env::var("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR") {
  28:         let user_socket = format!("{}/vyoma.sock", xdg);
  29:         if Path::new(&user_socket).exists() {
  30:             return user_socket;
  31:         }
  32:     }
  33:     // Fallback to /tmp (for development)
  34:     let tmp_socket = format!("{}/vyoma.sock", std::env::temp_dir().display());
  35:     if Path::new(&tmp_socket).exists() {
  36:         return tmp_socket;
  37:     }
  38:     // Return default path anyway - let it fail with clear error
  39:     default_path.to_string()
  40: }
  41: #[derive(Parser)]
  42: #[command(name = "vyoma")]
  43: #[command(about = "Vyoma: Docker for Micro-VMs", long_about = None)]
  44: struct Cli {
  45:     /// Socket path to daemon (Unix Socket)
  46:     #[arg(short, long, global = true, default_value = "/run/vyoma/vyoma.sock")]
  47:     socket_path: String,
  48:     /// HTTP port of daemon (for HTTP requests through Unix Socket)
  49:     #[arg(short = 'p', long, global = true, default_value_t = 8080)]
  50:     http_port: u16,
  51:     #[command(subcommand)]
  52:     command: Commands,
  53: }
  54: #[derive(Subcommand)]
  55: enum Commands {
  56:     /// Pull an image from Docker Hub
  57:     Pull {
  58:         /// Image to pull (e.g. ubuntu:latest)
  59:         image: String,
  60:     },
  61:     /// Run a new VM
  62:     Run {
  63:         /// Image to run (e.g. ubuntu:latest)
  64:         image: String,
  65:         /// vCPUs (default: 1)
  66:         #[arg(long, default_value = "1")]
  67:         vcpu: u32,
  68:         /// Memory in MiB (default: 512)
  69:         #[arg(long, default_value = "512")]
  70:         memory: u32,
  71:         /// Port mappings (e.g. -p 8080:80)
  72:         #[arg(short, long)]
  73:         ports: Vec<String>,
  74:         /// Volume mounts (e.g. -v /home/user/app:/app)
  75:         #[arg(short, long)]
  76:         volumes: Vec<String>,
  77:         /// Hostname for the VM
  78:         #[arg(long)]
  79:         hostname: Option<String>,
  80:         /// Labels (key=value)
  81:         #[arg(short = 'l', long)]
  82:         labels: Vec<String>,
  83:     },
  84:     /// Stop a VM
  85:     Stop {
  86:         /// VM ID
  87:         id: String,
  88:     },
  89:     /// Pause a VM
  90:     Pause {
  91:         /// VM ID
  92:         id: String,
  93:     },
  94:     /// Resume a VM
  95:     Resume {
  96:         /// VM ID
  97:         id: String,
  98:     },
  99:     /// Commit an active VM into a new image
 100:     Commit {
 101:         /// VM ID or Name
 102:         id: String,
 103:         /// New image name (e.g., custom-img:latest)
 104:         new_image_name: String,
 105:     },
 106:      /// List active VMs
 107:      Ps,
 108:       /// Show snapshot history of a VM
 109:       History {
 110:           /// VM ID
 111:           id: String,
 112:       },
 113:       /// Create a snapshot of a VM
 114:       Snapshot {
 115:           /// VM ID
 116:           id: String,
 117:       },
 118:      /// Time travel a VM to a specific snapshot
 119:      TimeTravel {
 120:          /// VM ID
 121:          id: String,
 122:          /// Snapshot ID to travel to
 123:          #[arg(long)]
 124:          to: String,
 125:      },
 126:     /// Save a VM snapshot to a file (Export/Teleportation)
 127:     Save {
 128:         /// VM ID to save
 129:         id: String,
 130:         /// Output file path (e.g. my-vm.tar.gz)
 131:         output: String,
 132:     },
 133:     /// Load a VM from a file (Import/Teleportation)
 134:     Load {
 135:         /// Input file path (e.g. my-vm.tar.gz)
 136:         input: String,
 137:     },
 138:     /// Live Teleport a VM to another Swarm node
 139:     Teleport {
 140:         /// VM ID to teleport
 141:         id: String,
 142:         /// Target Node IP (must expose gRPC on 7071)
 143:         target: String,
 144:         /// Bandwidth limit in Mbps (optional)
 145:         #[arg(long)]
 146:         bandwidth: Option<u32>,
 147:         /// Show live progress bar
 148:         #[arg(long, default_value = "true")]
 149:         progress: bool,
 150:     },
 151:     /// Stream logs from a VM
 152:     Logs {
 153:         /// VM ID
