 155:         /// Follow log output
 156:         #[arg(short = 'f', long)]
 157:         follow: bool,
 158:     },
 159:     /// Build a new image from an Vyomafile
 160:     Build {
 161:         /// Path to build context (directory containing Vyomafile)
 162:         #[arg(default_value = ".")]
 163:         path: String,
 164:         /// Perform measured build: launch ephemeral VM to pre-compute PCR values
 165:         #[arg(long)]
 166:         measured: bool,
 167:     },
 168:     /// Check system dependencies and environment health
 169:     Doctor,
 170:     /// Manage networks
 171:     Network {
 172:         #[command(subcommand)]
 173:         command: NetworkCommands,
 174:     },
 175:     /// Create and start resources from a compose file
 176:     Up {
 177:         /// Path to compose file (default: vyoma-compose.yml)
 178:         #[arg(short, long, default_value = "vyoma-compose.yml")]
 179:         file: String,
 180:         /// Detached mode: Run containers in the background
 181:         #[arg(short, long)]
 182:         detach: bool,
 183:     },
 184:     /// Stop and remove resources
 185:     Down {
 186:         /// Path to compose file (default: vyoma-compose.yml)
 187:         #[arg(short, long, default_value = "vyoma-compose.yml")]
 188:         file: String,
 189:     },
 190:     /// Execute a command in a VM (via SSH)
 191:     Exec {
 192:         /// VM ID or Name
 193:         id: String,
 194:         /// Command to run
 195:         #[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]
 196:         cmd: Vec<String>,
 197:     },
 198:     /// Restart a VM (Replaces it)
 199:     Restart {
 200:         /// VM ID or Name
 201:         id: String,
 202:     },
 203:     /// Scale services (e.g. web=3)
 204:     Scale {
 205:         /// Scaling arguments (service=count)
 206:         replicas: Vec<String>,
 207:         /// Path to compose file (default: vyoma-compose.yml)
 208:         #[arg(short, long, default_value = "vyoma-compose.yml")]
 209:         file: String,
 210:     },
 211:     /// Manage Swarm/Cluster
 212:     Swarm {
 213:         #[command(subcommand)]
 214:         command: SwarmCommands,
 215:     },
 216:     /// Attest a VM using TPM quote verification
 217:     Attest {
 218:         /// VM ID to attest
 219:         id: String,
 220:         /// Path to signed VMIF manifest (auto-discovered if omitted)
 221:         #[arg(long)]
 222:         manifest: Option<String>,
 223:         /// Trust policy keys directory for manifest signature verification
 224:         #[arg(long)]
 225:         trust_keys: Option<String>,
 226:         /// Skip manifest signature verification (use with caution)
 227:         #[arg(long)]
 228:         no_verify_signature: bool,
 229:         /// Output format: human (default) or json
 230:         #[arg(long, default_value = "human")]
 231:         format: String,
 232:     },
 233: }
 234: #[derive(Subcommand)]
 235: enum SwarmCommands {
 236:     /// Initialize a new swarm
 237:     Init,
 238:     /// Join an existing swarm
 239:     Join {
 240:         /// IP address of a node in the swarm
 241:         ip: String,
 242:     },
 243:     /// List nodes in the swarm
 244:     Ls,
 245: }
 246: #[derive(Subcommand)]
 247: enum NetworkCommands {
 248:     /// List available networks
 249:     Ls,
 250:     /// Create a new bridge network
 251:     Create {
 252:         /// Network name
 253:         name: String,
 254:         /// Subnet CIDR (e.g. 10.244.0.0/16)
 255:         #[arg(long)]
 256:         subnet: String,
 257:         /// Network Driver (bridge, overlay)
 258:         #[arg(long, default_value = "bridge")]
 259:         driver: String,
 260:     },
 261:     /// Remove a network
 262:     Rm {
 263:         /// Network name
 264:         name: String,
 265:     },
 266: }
 267: #[derive(Serialize)]
 268: struct RunRequest {
 269:     image: String,
 270:     vcpu: u32,
 271:     mem_size_mib: u32,
 272:     ports: Vec<PortMapping>,
 273:     volumes: Vec<VolumeMount>,
 274:     hostname: Option<String>,
 275:     labels: HashMap<String, String>,
 276: }
 277: #[derive(Serialize)]
 278: struct RestoreRequest {
 279:     snapshot_path: String,
 280:     mem_path: String,
 281:     cow_path: String,
 282:     original_vm_id: String,
 283: }
 284: #[derive(Serialize)]
 285: struct TimeTravelRequest {
 286:     vm_id: String,
 287:     snapshot_id: String,
 288: }
 289: #[derive(Deserialize, Debug)]
 290: struct RunResponse {
 291:     vm_id: String,
 292:     status: String,
 293: }
 294: #[tokio::main]
 295: async fn main() -> Result<()> {
 296:     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();
 297:     let cli = Cli::parse();
 298:     // Resolve socket path with fallback to user-writable location
 299:     let socket_path = resolve_socket_path(&cli.socket_path);
 300:     info!("Using socket path: {}", socket_path);
 301:     // Connect via Unix Socket
 302:     let client = Client::builder()
 303:         .unix_socket(socket_path.as_str())
 304:         .build()?;
 305:     // Daemon URL for HTTP requests (goes through Unix Socket proxy)
 306:     let daemon_url = format!("http://localhost:{}", cli.http_port);
 307:     match cli.command {
 308:         Commands::Pull { image } => {
 309:             info!("Requesting to pull image: {}", image);
 310:             let resp = client
 311:                 .post(format!("{}/pull", daemon_url))
 312:                 .json(&serde_json::json!({ "image": image }))
 313:                 .send()
 314:                 .await;
 315:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 316:         }
 317:         Commands::Run {
 318:             image,
 319:             vcpu,
 320:             memory,
 321:             ports,
 322:             volumes,
 323:             hostname,
 324:             labels,
 325:         } => {
 326:             info!("Requesting to run image: {}", image);
 327:             let mut port_mappings = Vec::new();
 328:             for p in ports {
 329:                 let parts: Vec<&str> = p.split(':').collect();
 330:                 if parts.len() != 2 {
 331:                     error!("Invalid port format: {}. Use host:vm (e.g., 8080:80)", p);
 332:                     return Ok(());
 333:                 }
 334:                 let host_port = parts[0]
 335:                     .parse::<u16>()
 336:                     .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid host port: {}", parts[0]))?;
 337:                 let vm_port = parts[1]
 338:                     .parse::<u16>()
 339:                     .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid vm port: {}", parts[1]))?;
 340:                 port_mappings.push(PortMapping { host_port, vm_port });
 341:             }
 342:             let mut volume_mounts = Vec::new();
 343:             for v in volumes {
 344:                 let parts: Vec<&str> = v.split(':').collect();
 345:                 if parts.len() != 2 {
 346:                     error!(
 347:                         "Invalid volume format: {}. Use host:vm (e.g., /foo:/bar)",
 348:                         v
 349:                     );
 350:                     return Ok(());
 351:                 }
 352:                 volume_mounts.push(VolumeMount {
 353:                     host_path: parts[0].to_string(),
 354:                     vm_path: parts[1].to_string(),
 355:                     read_only: false, // Default RW for now
 356:                 });
 357:             }
 358:             let mut label_map = HashMap::new();
 359:             for l in labels {
 360:                 if let Some((k, v)) = l.split_once('=') {
 361:                     label_map.insert(k.to_string(), v.to_string());
 362:                 } else {
 363:                     label_map.insert(l.clone(), "".to_string());
 364:                 }
 365:             }
 366:             let payload = RunRequest {
 367:                 image,
 368:                 vcpu,
 369:                 mem_size_mib: memory,
 370:                 ports: port_mappings,
 371:                 volumes: volume_mounts,
 372:                 hostname,
 373:                 labels: label_map,
 374:             };
 375:             let resp = client
 376:                 .post(format!("{}/run", daemon_url))
 377:                 .json(&payload)
 378:                 .send()
 379:                 .await;
 380:             handle_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 381:         }
 382:         Commands::Commit { id, new_image_name } => {
 383:             info!("Requesting to commit VM {} to image {}", id, new_image_name);
 384:             let payload = serde_json::json!({
 385:                 "new_image_name": new_image_name
 386:             });
 387:             let resp = client
 388:                 .post(format!("{}/commit/{}", daemon_url, id))
 389:                 .json(&payload)
 390:                 .send()
 391:                 .await;
 392:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 393:         }
 394:         Commands::Stop { id } => {
 395:             info!("Requesting to stop VM: {}", id);
 396:             let resp = client
 397:                 .post(format!("{}/stop/{}", daemon_url, id))
 398:                 .send()
 399:                 .await;
 400:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 401:         }
 402:         Commands::Pause { id } => {
 403:             info!("Requesting to pause VM: {}", id);
 404:             let resp = client
 405:                 .post(format!("{}/pause/{}", daemon_url, id))
 406:                 .send()
 407:                 .await;
 408:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 409:         }
 410:         Commands::Resume { id } => {
 411:             info!("Requesting to resume VM: {}", id);
 412:             let resp = client
 413:                 .post(format!("{}/resume/{}", daemon_url, id))
 414:                 .send()
 415:                 .await;
 416:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 417:         }
 418:         Commands::Exec { id, cmd } => {
 419:             // 1. Resolve IP
 420:             let mut target_ip = String::new();
 421:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 422:             if let Ok(r) = resp {
 423:                 if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
 424:                     for vm in list.vms {
 425:                         if vm.id == id
 426:                             || vm.hostname.as_deref() == Some(id.as_str())
 427:                             || vm.labels.get("vyoma.service").map(|s| s.as_str())
 428:                                 == Some(id.as_str())
 429:                         {
 430:                             target_ip = vm
 431:                                 .ip_address
 432:                                 .split('/')
 433:                                 .next()
 434:                                 .unwrap_or(&vm.ip_address)
 435:                                 .to_string();
 436:                             break;
 437:                         }
 438:                     }
 439:                 }
 440:             }
 441:             if target_ip.is_empty() {
 442:                 error!("VM '{}' not found or has no IP.", id);
 443:                 return Ok(());
 444:             }
 445:             info!("Executing command via SSH on {}...", target_ip);
 446:             let _ = std::process::Command::new("ssh")
 447:                 .arg("-o")
 448:                 .arg("StrictHostKeyChecking=no")
 449:                 .arg("-o")
 450:                 .arg("UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null")
 451:                 .arg(format!("root@{}", target_ip))
 452:                 .args(cmd)
 453:                 .status(); // Interactive if cmd is empty?
 454:         }
 455:         Commands::Restart { id } => {
 456:             // 1. Resolve ID (if Name provided)
 457:             let mut target_id = id.clone();
 458:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 459:             if let Ok(r) = resp {
 460:                 if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
 461:                     for vm in list.vms {
 462:                         if vm.id == id
 463:                             || vm.hostname.as_deref() == Some(id.as_str())
 464:                             || vm.labels.get("vyoma.service").map(|s| s.as_str())
 465:                                 == Some(id.as_str())
 466:                         {
 467:                             target_id = vm.id;
 468:                             break;
 469:                         }
 470:                     }
 471:                 }
 472:             }
 473:             // 2. Inspect (to get config)
 474:             info!("Restarting VM {} (Fetching config...)", target_id);
 475:             let resp = client
 476:                 .get(format!("{}/vms/{}", daemon_url, target_id))
 477:                 .send()
 478:                 .await;
 479:             match resp {
 480:                 Ok(r) => {
 481:                     if !r.status().is_success() {
 482:                         error!("Failed to inspect VM {}: {}", target_id, r.status());
 483:                         return Ok(());
 484:                     }
 485:                     let vm_state: VmState = r.json().await?;
 486:                     // 3. Stop
 487:                     info!("Stopping VM {}...", target_id);
 488:                     let _ = client
 489:                         .post(format!("{}/stop/{}", daemon_url, target_id))
 490:                         .send()
 491:                         .await;
 492:                     // 4. Run (New)
 493:                     info!("Starting replacement VM...");
 494:                     let payload = RunRequest {
 495:                         image: vm_state.base_image_path, // Pass resolved path
 496:                         vcpu: vm_state.vcpu,
 497:                         mem_size_mib: vm_state.mem_size_mib,
 498:                         ports: vm_state.ports,
 499:                         volumes: vm_state.volumes,
 500:                         hostname: vm_state.hostname,
 501:                         labels: vm_state.labels,
 502:                     };
 503:                     let resp = client
 504:                         .post(format!("{}/run", daemon_url))
 505:                         .json(&payload)
 506:                         .send()
 507:                         .await;
 508:                     handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 509:                 }
 510:                 Err(e) => error!("Failed to connect to daemon: {}", e),
 511:             }
 512:         }
 513:         Commands::Ps => {
 514:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 515:             match resp {
 516:                 Ok(response) => {
 517:                     if response.status().is_success() {
 518:                         let body: ListResponse = response.json().await?;
 519:                         println!(
 520:                             "{:<36} {:<15} {:<15} {:<15} {:<30}",
 521:                             "VM ID", "IP ADDRESS", "HOSTNAME", "ATTESTATION", "LABELS"
 522:                         );
 523:                         for vm in body.vms {
 524:                             let labels_str = vm
 525:                                 .labels
 526:                                 .iter()
 527:                                 .map(|(k, v)| {
 528:                                     if v.is_empty() {
 529:                                         k.clone()
 530:                                     } else {
 531:                                         format!("{}={}", k, v)
 532:                                     }
 533:                                 })
 534:                                  .collect::<Vec<_>>()
 535:                                  .join(", ");
 536:                             let hostname_str = vm.hostname.unwrap_or_else(|| "-".to_string());
 537:                             let attest_str = vm.attestation_status.unwrap_or_else(|| "-".to_string());
 538:                             println!(
 539:                                 "{:<36} {:<15} {:<15} {:<15} {:<30}",
 540:                                 vm.id, vm.ip_address, hostname_str, attest_str, labels_str
 541:                             );
 542:                         }
 543:                     } else {
 544:                         error!("Daemon returned error: {}", response.status());
 545:                     }
 546:                 }
 547:                 Err(e) => {
 548:                     error!("Failed to connect to daemon at {}: {}", daemon_url, e);
 549:                 }
 550:             }
 551:         }
 552:         Commands::Attest { id, .. } => {
 553:             info!("Requesting attestation for VM: {}", id);
 554:             let resp = client
 555:                 .post(format!("{}/attest/{}", daemon_url, id))
 556:                 .send()
 557:                 .await;
 558:             match resp {
 559:                 Ok(response) => {
 560:                     if response.status().is_success() {
 561:                         let attest_resp: serde_json::Value = response.json().await?;
 562:                         let verified = attest_resp.get("verified")
 563:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())
 564:                             .unwrap_or(false);
 565:                         let vm_id = attest_resp.get("vm_id")
 566:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
 567:                             .unwrap_or(&id);
 568:                         if verified {
 569:                             println!("VM {} attestation: {}", vm_id, "VERIFIED".green());
 570:                         } else {
 571:                             println!("VM {} attestation: {}", vm_id, "FAILED".red());
 572:                         }
 573:                         if let Some(pcr_results) = attest_resp.get("pcr_results").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
 574:                             println!("\nPCR Results:");
 575:                             println!("{:<10} {:<64} {:<64} {:<12}",
 576:                                 "PCR Index", "Expected", "Actual", "Verified");
 577:                             for pcr in pcr_results {
 578:                                 let pcr_index = pcr.get("pcr_index").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);
 579:                                 let expected = pcr.get("expected").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
 580:                                 let actual = pcr.get("actual").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
 581:                                 let verified = pcr.get("verified").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
 582:                                 let status = if verified { "✓".green() } else { "✗".red() };
 583:                                 println!(
 584:                                     "{:<10} {:<64} {:<64} {:<12}",
 585:                                     pcr_index, expected, actual, status
 586:                                 );
 587:                             }
 588:                         }
 589:                         if let Some(error) = attest_resp.get("error").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
 590:                             println!("\nError: {}", error.red());
 591:                         }
 592:                     } else {
 593:                         let error_msg = response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| "Unknown error".to_string());
 594:                         error!("Attestation failed: {}", error_msg);
 595:                     }
 596:                 }
 597:                 Err(e) => {
 598:                     error!("Failed to connect to daemon at {}: {}", daemon_url, e);
 599:                 }
 600:             }
 601:         }
 602:         Commands::Snapshot { id } => {
 603:             info!("Requesting to snapshot VM: {}", id);
 604:             let resp = client
 605:                 .post(format!("{}/snapshot/{}", daemon_url, id))
 606:                 .send()
 607:                 .await;
 608:             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 609:         }
 610:         Commands::History { id } => {
 611:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/history/{}", daemon_url, id)).send().await;
 612:             match resp {
 613:                 Ok(response) => {
 614:                     if response.status().is_success() {
 615:                         let json: serde_json::Value = response.json().await?;
 616:                         println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json)?);
 617:                     } else {
 618:                         error!("Daemon returned error: {}", response.status());
 619:                     }
 620:                 }
 621:                 Err(e) => error!("Failed to connect: {}", e),
 622:             }
 623:         }
 624:         Commands::TimeTravel { id, to } => {
 625:             info!("Requesting to time-travel VM {} to snapshot: {}", id, to);
 626:             let payload = TimeTravelRequest {
 627:                 vm_id: id,
 628:                 snapshot_id: to,
 629:             };
 630:             let resp = client
 631:                 .post(format!("{}/time-travel", daemon_url))
 632:                 .json(&payload)
 633:                 .send()
 634:                 .await;
 635:             handle_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 636:         }
 637:         Commands::Save { id, output } => {
 638:             info!("Saving VM {} to {}", id, output);
 639:             export_vm(&id, &output)?;
 640:         }
 641:         Commands::Load { input } => {
 642:             info!("Loading VM from {}", input);
 643:             import_vm(&input, &daemon_url).await?;
 644:         }
 645:         Commands::Teleport { id, target, bandwidth, progress } => {
 646:             info!("Initiating Live Teleportation for VM {} to target {}", id, target);
 647:             let target_daemon = format!("http://{}:3000", target);
 648:             let status_check = client.get(format!("{}/teleport/status/test", target_daemon)).send().await;
 649:             let use_live_migration = status_check.map(|r| r.status().as_u16() != 404).unwrap_or(false);
 650:             if !use_live_migration {
 651:                 warn!("Target node {} doesn't support live migration, falling back to snapshot/stream mode", target);
 652:                 // Start receiver on the target node
 653:                 let receiver_payload = serde_json::json!({
 654:                     "vm_id": id
 655:                 });
 656:                 let recv_resp = client
 657:                     .post(format!("{}/receive-teleport", target_daemon))
 658:                     .json(&receiver_payload)
 659:                     .send()
 660:                     .await;
 661:                 if let Err(e) = recv_resp {
 662:                     error!("Failed to start receiver on target {}: {}", target, e);
 663:                     return Ok(());
 664:                 }
 665:                 let recv_resp = recv_resp.unwrap();
 666:                 if !recv_resp.status().is_success() {
 667:                     error!("Target refused to start receiver: {}", recv_resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 668:                     return Ok(());
 669:                 }
 670:                 info!("Receiver started on target: {}", recv_resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 671:                 // Send migration from source
 672:                 let payload = serde_json::json!({
 673:                     "vm_id": id,
 674:                     "target_node_ip": target
 675:                 });
 676:                 let resp = client
 677:                     .post(format!("{}/teleport", daemon_url))
 678:                     .json(&payload)
 679:                     .send()
 680:                     .await?;
 681:                 if !resp.status().is_success() {
 682:                     error!("Teleport (fallback) failed: {}", resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 683:                     return Ok(());
 684:                 }
 685:                 println!("{}", resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 686:                 return Ok(());
 687:             }
 688:             let payload = serde_json::json!({
 689:                 "vm_id": id,
 690:                 "target_node_ip": target,
 691:                 "bandwidth_mbps": bandwidth
 692:             });
 693:             let resp = client
 694:                 .post(format!("{}/teleport", daemon_url))
 695:                 .json(&payload)
 696:                 .send()
 697:                 .await?;
 698:             if !resp.status().is_success() {
 699:                 error!("Teleport failed: {}", resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 700:                 return Ok(());
 701:             }
 702:             let text = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
 703:             println!("{}", text);
 704:             if progress {
 705:                 if let Ok(data) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&text) {
 706:                     if let Some(session_id) = data.get("session_id").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
 707:                         show_migration_progress(&client, &daemon_url, session_id).await;
 708:                     }
 709:                 }
 710:             }
 711:         }
 712:         Commands::Logs { id, follow: _ } => {
 713:             // 1. Resolve ID (if Name provided)
 714:             let mut target_id = id.clone();
 715:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 716:             if let Ok(r) = resp {
 717:                 if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
 718:                     for vm in list.vms {
 719:                         if vm.id == id
 720:                             || vm.hostname.as_deref() == Some(id.as_str())
 721:                             || vm.labels.get("vyoma.service").map(|s| s.as_str())
 722:                                 == Some(id.as_str())
 723:                         {
 724:                             target_id = vm.id;
 725:                             break;
 726:                         }
 727:                     }
 728:                 }
 729:             }
 730:             let resp = client
 731:                 .get(format!("{}/logs/{}", daemon_url, target_id))
 732:                 .send()
 733:                 .await?;
 734:             if !resp.status().is_success() {
 735:                 error!("Failed to get logs: {}", resp.status());
 736:                 return Ok(());
 737:             }
 738:             let mut stream = resp.bytes_stream();
 739:             let mut buffer = String::new();
 740:             while let Some(item) = stream.next().await {
 741:                 let bytes = item?;
 742:                 let chunk = String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes);
 743:                 buffer.push_str(&chunk);
 744:                 while let Some(idx) = buffer.find('\n') {
 745:                     let line = buffer[..idx].to_string();
 746:                     buffer = buffer[idx + 1..].to_string();
 747:                     if line.starts_with("data: ") {
 748:                         println!("{}", &line[6..]);
 749:                     }
 750:                 }
 751:             }
 752:         }
 753:         Commands::Build { path, measured } => {
 754:             info!("Building image from context: {} (measured={})", path, measured);
 755:             build_image_with_client(&path, &client, &daemon_url, measured).await?;
 756:         }
 757:         Commands::Doctor => {
 758:             run_doctor().await?;
 759:         }
 760:         Commands::Network { command } => match command {
 761:             NetworkCommands::Ls => {
 762:                 let resp = client.get(format!("{}/networks", daemon_url)).send().await;
 763:                 match resp {
 764:                     Ok(response) => {
 765:                         if response.status().is_success() {
 766:                             let networks: Vec<String> = response.json().await?;
 767:                             println!("NETWORKS:");
 768:                             for net in networks {
 769:                                 println!("{}", net);
 770:                             }
 771:                         } else {
 772:                             error!("Daemon returned error: {}", response.status());
 773:                         }
 774:                     }
 775:                     Err(e) => error!("Failed to list networks: {}", e),
 776:                 }
 777:             }
 778:             NetworkCommands::Create {
 779:                 name,
 780:                 subnet,
 781:                 driver,
 782:             } => {
 783:                 let payload = serde_json::json!({
 784:                     "name": name,
 785:                     "subnet": subnet,
 786:                     "driver": driver
 787:                 });
 788:                 let resp = client
 789:                     .post(format!("{}/networks", daemon_url))
 790:                     .json(&payload)
 791:                     .send()
 792:                     .await;
 793:                 handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 794:             }
 795:             NetworkCommands::Rm { name } => {
 796:                 let resp = client
 797:                     .delete(format!("{}/networks/{}", daemon_url, name))
 798:                     .send()
 799:                     .await;
 800:                 handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 801:             }
 802:         },
 803:         Commands::Swarm { command } => match command {
 804:             SwarmCommands::Init => {
 805:                 info!("Initializing swarm...");
 806:                 let resp = client
 807:                     .post(format!("{}/swarm/init", daemon_url))
 808:                     .send()
 809:                     .await;
 810:                 handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 811:             }
 812:             SwarmCommands::Join { ip } => {
 813:                 info!("Joining swarm at {}...", ip);
 814:                 let payload = serde_json::json!({ "seed_ip": ip });
 815:                 let resp = client
 816:                     .post(format!("{}/swarm/join", daemon_url))
 817:                     .json(&payload)
 818:                     .send()
 819:                     .await;
 820:                 handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 821:             }
 822:             SwarmCommands::Ls => {
 823:                 let resp = client
 824:                     .get(format!("{}/swarm/nodes", daemon_url))
 825:                     .send()
 826:                     .await;
 827:                 // Just print raw JSON for MVP
 828:                 handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
 829:             }
 830:         },
 831:         Commands::Up { file, detach } => {
 832:             info!("Processing compose file: {}", file);
 833:             match VyomaCompose::from_file(&file) {
 834:                 Ok(compose) => {
 835:                     println!("Vyoma Compose v{}", compose.version);
 836:                     let stack_name = std::env::current_dir()
 837:                         .ok()
 838:                         .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string()))
 839:                         .unwrap_or_else(|| "default".to_string());
 840:                     let service_order = match compose.start_order() {
 841:                         Ok(o) => o,
 842:                         Err(e) => {
 843:                             error!("Dependency resolution failed: {}", e);
 844:                             return Ok(());
 845:                         }
 846:                     };
 847:                     println!("Services found: {}", service_order.len());
 848:                     // Provision Networks
 849:                     let mut created_networks = HashMap::new();
 850:                     for (net_name, net_config) in &compose.networks {
 851:                         let full_net_name = format!("{}_{}", stack_name, net_name);
 852:                         let req = serde_json::json!({
 853:                             "name": full_net_name,
 854:                             "subnet": net_config.ipam.config.first().and_then(|c| c.subnet.clone()).unwrap_or_else(|| "".to_string()),
 855:                             "driver": net_config.driver.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "bridge".to_string())
 856:                         });
 857:                         let resp = client.post(format!("{}/networks", daemon_url))
 858:                             .json(&req)
 859:                             .send()
 860:                             .await;
 861:                         if let Ok(r) = resp {
 862:                             if r.status().is_success() {
 863:                                 info!("Network {} provisioned", full_net_name);
 864:                             } else {
 865:                                 warn!("Failed to provision network {}: {}", full_net_name, r.text().await.unwrap_or_default());
 866:                             }
 867:                         }
 868:                         created_networks.insert(net_name.clone(), full_net_name);
 869:                     }
 870:                     // Pre-check running services via Daemon
 871:                     let mut service_ids = HashMap::new();
 872:                     let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 873:                     if let Ok(r) = resp {
 874:                         if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
 875:                             for vm in list.vms {
 876:                                 if let Some(s) = vm.labels.get("vyoma.stack") {
 877:                                     if s == &stack_name {
 878:                                         let service_name = vm
 879:                                             .labels
 880:                                             .get("vyoma.service")
 881:                                             .cloned()
 882:                                             .unwrap_or(vm.id.clone());
 883:                                         service_ids.insert(service_name, vm.id);
 884:                                     }
 885:                                 }
 886:                             }
 887:                         }
 888:                     }
 889:                     for (name, service) in service_order {
 890:                         if service_ids.contains_key(&name) {
 891:                             info!("Service '{}' is already running.", name);
 892:                             continue;
 893:                         }
 894:                         info!("Starting service: {}", name);
 895:                         let image_target = if let Some(ref build) = service.build {
 896:                             let context = match build {
 897:                                 vyoma_compose::BuildSource::Path(p) => p.clone(),
 898:                                 vyoma_compose::BuildSource::Config(c) => c.context.clone(),
 899:                             };
 900:                             info!("Building service '{}' from {}", name, context);
 901:                             build_image_with_client(&context, &client, &daemon_url, false).await?
 902:                         } else if let Some(ref img) = service.image {
 903:                             img.clone()
 904:                         } else {
 905:                             error!("Service '{}' has no image or build context", name);
 906:                             continue;
 907:                         };
 908:                         // Prepare Ports
 909:                         let mut port_mappings = Vec::new();
 910:                         if let Some(ports) = service.ports {
 911:                             for p in ports {
 912:                                 let parts: Vec<&str> = p.split(':').collect();
 913:                                 if parts.len() == 2 {
 914:                                     let h = parts[0].parse().unwrap_or(0);
 915:                                     let v = parts[1].parse().unwrap_or(0);
 916:                                     port_mappings.push(PortMapping {
 917:                                         host_port: h,
 918:                                         vm_port: v,
 919:                                     });
 920:                                 }
 921:                             }
 922:                         }
 923:                         // Prepare Volumes
 924:                         let mut volume_mounts = Vec::new();
 925:                         if let Some(vols) = service.volumes {
 926:                             for v in vols {
 927:                                 let parts: Vec<&str> = v.split(':').collect();
 928:                                 if parts.len() == 2 {
 929:                                     volume_mounts.push(VolumeMount {
 930:                                         host_path: parts[0].to_string(),
 931:                                         vm_path: parts[1].to_string(),
 932:                                         read_only: false,
 933:                                     });
 934:                                 }
 935:                             }
 936:                         }
 937:                         let payload = RunRequest {
 938:                             image: image_target,
 939:                             vcpu: service.cpus.unwrap_or(1),
 940:                             mem_size_mib: service.memory.unwrap_or(512),
 941:                             ports: port_mappings,
 942:                             volumes: volume_mounts,
 943:                             hostname: Some(name.clone()),
 944:                             labels: {
 945:                                 let mut l = HashMap::new();
 946:                                 l.insert("vyoma.stack".to_string(), stack_name.clone());
 947:                                 l.insert("vyoma.service".to_string(), name.clone());
 948:                                 l
 949:                             },
 950:                         };
 951:                         let resp = client
 952:                             .post(format!("{}/run", daemon_url))
 953:                             .json(&payload)
 954:                             .send()
 955:                             .await;
 956:                         match resp {
 957:                             Ok(r) => {
 958:                                 if r.status().is_success() {
 959:                                     let body: RunResponse = r.json().await?;
 960:                                     info!("Service '{}' started as VM {}", name, body.vm_id);
 961:                                     service_ids.insert(name.clone(), body.vm_id);
 962:                                 } else {
 963:                                     error!("Failed to start service '{}': {}", name, r.status());
 964:                                 }
 965:                             }
 966:                             Err(e) => error!("Failed to request start for '{}': {}", name, e),
 967:                         }
 968:                     }
 969:                     if detach {
 970:                         println!("(Detached mode selected)");
 971:                     }
 972:                 }
 973:                 Err(e) => error!("Failed to parse compose file '{}': {}", file, e),
 974:             }
 975:         }
 976:         Commands::Down { file } => {
 977:             // 1. Identify Stack Name
 978:             let stack_name = std::env::current_dir()
 979:                 .ok()
 980:                 .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string()))
 981:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| "default".to_string());
 982:             info!("Stopping stack: {}", stack_name);
 983:             // 2. Fetch all VMs to find stack members
 984:             let mut vms_to_stop = HashMap::new(); // name -> id
 985:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
 986:             if let Ok(r) = resp {
 987:                 if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
 988:                     for vm in list.vms {
 989:                         if let Some(s) = vm.labels.get("vyoma.stack") {
 990:                             if s == &stack_name {
 991:                                 // Found a VM belonging to this stack
 992:                                 let service_name = vm
 993:                                     .labels
 994:                                     .get("vyoma.service")
 995:                                     .cloned()
 996:                                     .unwrap_or(vm.id.clone());
 997:                                 vms_to_stop.insert(service_name, vm.id);
 998:                             }
 999:                         }
1000:                     }
1001:                 }
1002:             }
1003:             if vms_to_stop.is_empty() {
1004:                 println!("No running services found for stack '{}'.", stack_name);
1005:                 let _ = std::fs::remove_file("vyoma-compose.state");
1006:                 return Ok(());
1007:             }
1008:             // 3. Determine Order
1009:             let mut stop_order = Vec::new();
1010:             if let Ok(compose) = VyomaCompose::from_file(&file) {
1011:                 if let Ok(order) = compose.start_order() {
1012:                     stop_order = order.into_iter().rev().map(|(n, _)| n).collect();
1013:                 }
1014:             }
1015:             // Add any remaining services from running list not in stop_order
1016:             for name in vms_to_stop.keys() {
1017:                 if !stop_order.contains(name) {
1018:                     stop_order.push(name.clone());
1019:                 }
1020:             }
1021:             for name in stop_order {
1022:                 if let Some(id) = vms_to_stop.get(&name) {
1023:                     info!("Stopping service '{}' (VM {})", name, id);
1024:                     let resp = client
1025:                         .post(format!("{}/stop/{}", daemon_url, id))
1026:                         .send()
1027:                         .await;
1028:                     match resp {
1029:                         Ok(r) => {
1030:                             if !r.status().is_success() {
1031:                                 error!("Failed to stop VM {}: {}", id, r.status());
1032:                             }
1033:                         }
1034:                         Err(e) => error!("Failed to stop VM {}: {}", id, e),
1035:                     }
1036:                 }
1037:             }
1038:             let _ = std::fs::remove_file("vyoma-compose.state");
1039:             println!("Stack stopped and removed.");
1040:         }
1041:         Commands::Scale { replicas, file } => {
1042:             // 1. Load Compose File
1043:             let compose = match VyomaCompose::from_file(&file) {
1044:                 Ok(c) => c,
1045:                 Err(e) => {
1046:                     error!(
1047:                         "Validation Error: Cannot scale without valid {}: {}",
1048:                         file, e
1049:                     );
1050:                     return Ok(());
1051:                 }
1052:             };
1053:             // 2. Identify Stack Name
1054:             let stack_name = std::env::current_dir()
1055:                 .ok()
1056:                 .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string()))
1057:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| "default".to_string());
1058:             // 3. Process scale args
1059:             let mut scale_map = HashMap::new();
1060:             for r in replicas {
1061:                 if let Some((svc, count_str)) = r.split_once('=') {
1062:                     if let Ok(count) = count_str.parse::<usize>() {
1063:                         if compose.services.contains_key(svc) {
1064:                             scale_map.insert(svc.to_string(), count);
1065:                         } else {
1066:                             error!("Service '{}' not defined in compose file.", svc);
1067:                         }
1068:                     } else {
1069:                         error!("Invalid count format for '{}'. Expected integer.", svc);
1070:                     }
1071:                 } else {
1072:                     error!("Invalid format: {}. Expected service=count", r);
1073:                 }
1074:             }
1075:             if scale_map.is_empty() {
1076:                 println!("No valid scaling instructions provided.");
1077:                 return Ok(());
1078:             }
1079:             // 4. Get Current State
1080:             let mut current_state: HashMap<String, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();
1081:             let resp = client.get(format!("{}/ps", daemon_url)).send().await;
1082:             if let Ok(r) = resp {
1083:                 if let Ok(list) = r.json::<ListResponse>().await {
1084:                     for vm in list.vms {
1085:                         if let Some(s) = vm.labels.get("vyoma.stack") {
1086:                             if s == &stack_name {
1087:                                 if let Some(svc) = vm.labels.get("vyoma.service") {
1088:                                     current_state.entry(svc.clone()).or_default().push(vm.id);
1089:                                 }
1090:                             }
1091:                         }
1092:                     }
1093:                 }
1094:             }
1095:             // 5. Reconcile
1096:             for (svc_name, target_count) in scale_map {
1097:                 let running_list = current_state.get(&svc_name).cloned().unwrap_or_default();
1098:                 let running_count = running_list.len();
1099:                 info!(
1100:                     "Scaling {} from {} to {}",
1101:                     svc_name, running_count, target_count
1102:                 );
1103:                 if target_count > running_count {
1104:                     let needed = target_count - running_count;
1105:                     let service = compose.services.get(&svc_name).unwrap();
1106:                     for i in 0..needed {
1107:                         info!("Starting replica {}/{}", i + 1, needed);
1108:                         start_service_helper(&client, &daemon_url, &stack_name, &svc_name, service)
1109:                             .await?;
1110:                     }
1111:                 } else if running_count > target_count {
1112:                     let remove_count = running_count - target_count;
1113:                     // Stop the LAST N instances
1114:                     for i in 0..remove_count {
1115:                         if let Some(id) = running_list.get(running_count - 1 - i) {
1116:                             info!("Stopping replica {} (VM {})", running_count - i, id);
1117:                             let resp = client
1118:                                 .post(format!("{}/stop/{}", daemon_url, id))
1119:                                 .send()
1120:                                 .await;
1121:                             handle_simple_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
1122:                         }
1123:                     }
1124:                 } else {
1125:                     println!(
1126:                         "Service {} is already at target scale ({}).",
1127:                         svc_name, target_count
1128:                     );
1129:                 }
1130:             }
1131:         }
1132:         Commands::Attest { id, manifest, trust_keys, no_verify_signature, format } => {
1133:             let json_output = format == "json";
1134:             match run_attest(&client, &daemon_url, &id, manifest.as_deref(), trust_keys.as_deref(), no_verify_signature, json_output).await {
1135:                 Ok(verified) => {
1136:                     if !verified {
1137:                         std::process::exit(1);
1138:                     }
1139:                 }
1140:                 Err(e) => {
1141:                     error!("{}", e);
1142:                     std::process::exit(1);
1143:                 }
1144:             }
1145:         }
1146:     }
1147:     Ok(())
1148: }
1149: async fn run_doctor() -> Result<()> {
1150:     println!(
1151:         "{}",
1152:         "Vyoma Doctor - System Health Check".bold().underline()
1153:     );
1154:     println!();
1155:     let mut all_passed = true;
1156:     // Helper to print status
1157:     let check = |name: &str, result: Result<bool>, required: bool| -> bool {
1158:         match result {
1159:             Ok(true) => {
1160:                 println!("{} {}", "[OK]".green().bold(), name);
1161:                 true
1162:             }
1163:             Ok(false) => {
1164:                 if required {
1165:                     println!("{} {}", "[FAIL]".red().bold(), name);
1166:                     false
1167:                 } else {
1168:                     println!("{} {}", "[WARN]".yellow().bold(), name);
1169:                     true // Warn doesn't fail overall
1170:                 }
1171:             }
1172:             Err(e) => {
1173:                 if required {
1174:                     println!("{} {} ({})", "[FAIL]".red().bold(), name, e);
1175:                     false
1176:                 } else {
1177:                     println!("{} {} ({})", "[WARN]".yellow().bold(), name, e);
1178:                     true
1179:                 }
1180:             }
1181:         }
1182:     };
1183:     // 1. KVM Access
1184:     if !check("KVM Device Access (/dev/kvm)", check_kvm(), true) {
1185:         all_passed = false;
1186:     }
1187:     // 2. Cgroups
1188:     if !check("Cgroups v2 (/sys/fs/cgroup)", check_cgroups(), true) {
1189:         all_passed = false;
1190:     }
1191:     // 3. Binaries
1192:     if !check("Cloud Hypervisor Binary", check_binary("cloud-hypervisor"), true) {
1193:         all_passed = false;
1194:     }
1195:     if !check("Virtiofsd Binary", check_binary("virtiofsd"), true) {
1196:         all_passed = false;
1197:     }
1198:     // 4. Networking
1199:     if !check("Vyoma Bridge (vyoma0)", check_bridge("vyoma0"), false) {} // Warn only
1200:     // 5. Rootless Tools
1201:     if !check("debugfs (e2fsprogs)", check_binary("debugfs"), false) {} // Needed for rootless build
1202:     println!();
1203:     if all_passed {
1204:         println!("{}", "Your system is ready for Vyoma!".green().bold());
1205:     } else {
1206:         println!(
1207:             "{}",
1208:             "Some checks failed. Please review the errors above."
1209:                 .red()
1210:                 .bold()
1211:         );
1212:     }
1213:     Ok(())
1214: }
1215: fn check_kvm() -> Result<bool> {
1216:     use std::fs::OpenOptions;
1217:     let path = Path::new("/dev/kvm");
1218:     if !path.exists() {
1219:         return Ok(false);
1220:     }
1221:     // Try to open R/W
1222:     OpenOptions::new().read(true).write(true).open(path)?;
1223:     Ok(true)
1224: }
1225: fn check_cgroups() -> Result<bool> {
1226:     // Just check if mount point exists and is cgroup2
1227:     // Simple check: /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.controllers should exist
1228:     let path = Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.controllers");
1229:     Ok(path.exists())
1230: }
1231: fn check_binary(name: &str) -> Result<bool> {
1232:     use std::path::Path;
1233:     // Check standard packaging paths (ADR 021)
1234:     if Path::new(&format!("/opt/vyoma/bin/{}", name)).exists() {
1235:         return Ok(true);
1236:     }
1237:     if Path::new(&format!("/usr/libexec/vyoma/{}", name)).exists() {
1238:         return Ok(true);
1239:     }
1240:     // Check bundled path from package
1241:     if Path::new(&format!("/usr/lib/vyoma/{}", name)).exists() {
1242:         return Ok(true);
1243:     }
1244:     // Check local development path
1245:     if Path::new(&format!("bin/{}", name)).exists() {
1246:         return Ok(true);
1247:     }
1248:     // Check system PATH
1249:     let status = std::process::Command::new("which")
1250:         .arg(name)
1251:         .output()?
1252:         .status;
1253:     Ok(status.success())
1254: }
1255: fn command_exists(name: &str) -> bool {
1256:     std::process::Command::new("which")
1257:         .arg(name)
1258:         .output()
1259:         .map(|o| o.status.success())
1260:         .unwrap_or(false)
1261: }
1262: fn check_bridge(bridge_name: &str) -> Result<bool> {
1263:     let output = std::process::Command::new("ip")
1264:         .arg("link")
1265:         .arg("show")
1266:         .arg(bridge_name)
1267:         .output()?;
1268:     Ok(output.status.success())
1269: }
1270: async fn build_image_with_client(context_path: &str, client: &Client, daemon_url: &str, measured: bool) -> Result<String> {
1271:     let context_path = Path::new(context_path);
1272:     if !context_path.exists() {
1273:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1274:             "Context path does not exist: {:?}",
1275:             context_path
1276:         ));
1277:     }
1278:     // Create tarball to temp file
1279:     let temp_dir = tempfile::tempdir()?;
1280:     let tar_path = temp_dir.path().join("context.tar.gz");
1281:     // Create tar.gz using subprocess for reliability
1282:     let status = std::process::Command::new("tar")
1283:         .arg("-czf")
1284:         .arg(&tar_path)
1285:         .arg("-C")
1286:         .arg(context_path)
1287:         .arg(".")
1288:         .status()?;
1289:     if !status.success() {
1290:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to create tarball"));
1291:     }
1292:     // Send to daemon
1293:     let file = tokio::fs::File::open(&tar_path).await?;
1294:     let stream = tokio_util::codec::FramedRead::new(file, tokio_util::codec::BytesCodec::new());
1295:     let body = reqwest::Body::wrap_stream(stream);
1296:     let measured_query = if measured { "?measured=true" } else { "" };
1297:     let resp = client
1298:         .post(format!("{}/build{}", daemon_url, measured_query))
1299:         .body(body)
1300:         .send()
1301:         .await?;
1302:     if !resp.status().is_success() {
1303:         let status = resp.status();
1304:         let text = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
1305:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Build failed ({}): {}", status, text));
1306:     }
1307:     let image_id = resp.text().await?;
1308:     info!("Build complete. Image ID: {}", image_id);
1309:     Ok(image_id)
1310: }
1311: async fn build_image(context_path: &str, daemon_url: &str) -> Result<String> {
1312:     let client = Client::new();
1313:     build_image_with_client(context_path, &client, daemon_url, false).await
1314: }
1315: async fn run_attest(
1316:     client: &Client,
1317:     daemon_url: &str,
1318:     vm_id: &str,
1319:     manifest_path: Option<&str>,
1320:     trust_keys_path: Option<&str>,
1321:     no_verify_signature: bool,
1322:     json_output: bool,
1323: ) -> Result<bool> {
1324:     info!("Attesting VM: {}", vm_id);
1325:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No home directory"))?;
1326:     let (signed_manifest, manifest_source) = if let Some(ref mpath) = manifest_path {
1327:         let path = PathBuf::from(mpath);
1328:         let signed = SignedManifest::load_from_file(&path)
1329:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to load manifest from {}: {}", mpath, e))?;
1330:         (signed, format!("explicit: {}", mpath))
1331:     } else {
1332:         let inspect_url = format!("{}/vm/{}", daemon_url, vm_id);
1333:         let resp = client.get(&inspect_url).send().await
1334:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to inspect VM {}: {}", vm_id, e))?;
1335:         if resp.status() != StatusCode::OK {
1336:             return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("VM {} not found (daemon returned {})", vm_id, resp.status()));
1337:         }
1338:         #[derive(Deserialize)]
1339:         struct VmInspect { base_image_path: String }
1340:         let vm_info: VmInspect = resp.json().await
1341:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse VM info: {}", e))?;
1342:         let image_name = std::path::Path::new(&vm_info.base_image_path)
1343:             .file_name()
1344:             .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string())
1345:             .unwrap_or_else(|| vm_info.base_image_path.clone());
1346:         let candidates = [
1347:             home.join(".vyoma").join("images").join(&image_name),
1348:             home.join(".vyoma").join("images").join(&image_name),
1349:         ];
1350:         let mut loaded = None;
1351:         let mut source = String::new();
1352:         for dir in &candidates {
1353:             let sig_path = dir.join("vyoma.toml.sig");
1354:             if sig_path.exists() {
1355:                 match SignedManifest::load_from_file(&sig_path) {
1356:                     Ok(sm) => {
1357:                         loaded = Some(sm);
1358:                         source = format!("auto-discovered signed: {}", sig_path.display());
1359:                         break;
1360:                     }
1361:                     Err(e) => warn!("Failed to load {}: {}", sig_path.display(), e),
1362:                 }
1363:             }
1364:             let plain_path = dir.join("vyoma.toml");
1365:             if plain_path.exists() {
1366:                 match vyoma_image::VmifConverter::load_signed_manifest(&plain_path) {
1367:                     Ok(sm) => {
1368:                         loaded = Some(sm);
1369:                         source = format!("auto-discovered: {}", plain_path.display());
1370:                         break;
1371:                     }
1372:                     Err(e) => warn!("Failed to load {}: {}", plain_path.display(), e),
1373:                 }
1374:             }
1375:         }
1376:         match loaded {
1377:             Some(sm) => (sm, source),
1378:             None => return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1379:                 "No manifest found for image '{}'. Searched: {:?}. Build image first.",
1380:                 image_name, candidates
1381:             )),
1382:         }
1383:     };
1384:     if !no_verify_signature {
1385:         let mut policy = TrustPolicy::new(true);
1386:         let keys_dir = if let Some(ref kp) = trust_keys_path {
1387:             PathBuf::from(kp)
1388:         } else {
1389:             home.join(".vyoma").join("keys").join("trusted")
1390:         };
1391:         policy.load_trusted_keys_from_dir(keys_dir.clone())
1392:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to load trusted keys from {:?}: {}", keys_dir, e))?;
1393:         match policy.verify(&signed_manifest) {
1394:             Ok(()) => {
1395:                 let key_hex: String = signed_manifest.public_key.iter()
1396:                     .map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b))
1397:                     .collect::<String>();
1398:                 info!("Manifest signature verified. Signing key: {}...", &key_hex[..16]);
1399:                 if !json_output {
1400:                     println!("  {}", format!("Manifest signed by: {}...", &key_hex[..16]).green());
1401:                 }
1402:             }
1403:             Err(e) => {
1404:                 error!("Manifest signature verification FAILED: {}", e);
1405:                 return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Signature verification failed: {}. Use --no-verify-signature to skip.", e));
1406:             }
1407:         }
1408:     } else if !json_output {
1409:         println!("  Note: Signature verification skipped (--no-verify-signature)");
1410:     }
1411:     if !json_output {
1412:         println!("  Manifest: {}", manifest_source);
1413:     }
1414:     let url = format!("{}/attest/{}", daemon_url, vm_id);
1415:     let resp = client.post(&url).send().await
1416:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to contact daemon at {}: {}", daemon_url, e))?;
1417:     let status = resp.status();
1418:     if status != StatusCode::OK {
1419:         let body = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
1420:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Attestation request failed ({}): {}", status, body));
1421:     }
1422:     #[derive(serde::Deserialize, serde::Serialize)]
1423:     struct AttestResponse {
1424:         vm_id: String,
1425:         verified: bool,
1426:         pcr_results: Vec<PcrResult>,
1427:         error: Option<String>,
1428:     }
1429:     #[derive(serde::Deserialize, serde::Serialize)]
1430:     struct PcrResult {
1431:         pcr_index: u32,
1432:         expected: String,
1433:         actual: String,
1434:         verified: bool,
1435:     }
1436:     let attest_resp: AttestResponse = resp.json().await
1437:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse attest response: {}", e))?;
1438:     if json_output {
1439:         #[derive(serde::Serialize)]
1440:         struct JsonOutput<'a> {
1441:             vm_id: &'a str,
1442:             verified: bool,
1443:             pcr_results: &'a Vec<PcrResult>,
1444:             error: &'a Option<String>,
1445:             manifest: String,
1446:         }
1447:         let out = JsonOutput {
1448:             vm_id: &attest_resp.vm_id,
1449:             verified: attest_resp.verified,
1450:             pcr_results: &attest_resp.pcr_results,
1451:             error: &attest_resp.error,
1452:             manifest: manifest_source.clone(),
1453:         };
1454:         println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&out).unwrap());
1455:         return Ok(attest_resp.verified);
1456:     }
1457:     // Human-readable output
1458:     println!("\n{}", "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗".cyan());
1459:     println!("{}", "║              VM Attestation Report                      ║".cyan());
1460:     println!("{}", "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝".cyan());
1461:     println!("VM ID: {}", attest_resp.vm_id);
1462:     println!();
1463:     let pcr_names = [
1464:         (0u32, "PCR-0 (Firmware)"),
1465:         (1, "PCR-1 (Firmware Config)"),
1466:         (4, "PCR-4 (Boot Manager)"),
1467:         (5, "PCR-5 (Boot Manager Config)"),
1468:         (7, "PCR-7 (Secure Boot State)"),
1469:         (9, "PCR-9 (Kernel)"),
1470:         (10, "PCR-10 (Initrd)"),
1471:         (14, "PCR-14 (Rootfs)"),
1472:     ];
1473:     let mut passed = 0;
1474:     let mut failed = 0;
1475:     for result in &attest_resp.pcr_results {
1476:         let pcr_name = if let Some((_, name)) = pcr_names.iter().find(|(idx, _)| *idx == result.pcr_index) {
1477:         *name
1478:     } else {
1479:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Unknown PCR index: {}", result.pcr_index));
1480:     };
1481:         if result.verified {
1482:             println!("  [{}] {}: {}", "OK".green(), pcr_name, result.actual.yellow());
1483:             passed += 1;
1484:         } else {
1485:             println!("  [{}] {}: {}", "FAIL".red(), pcr_name, result.actual.red());
1486:             println!("         Expected: {}", result.expected.yellow());
1487:             failed += 1;
1488:         }
1489:     }
1490:     println!();
1491:     println!("PCRs verified: {}/{}", passed, passed + failed);
1492:     println!();
1493:     if attest_resp.verified {
1494:         println!("{}", "  Attestation: VERIFIED".green().bold());
1495:         println!("  VM {} boot integrity is confirmed.", vm_id);
1496:     } else {
1497:         println!("{}", "  Attestation: FAILED".red().bold());
1498:         if let Some(ref err) = attest_resp.error {
1499:             println!("  Error: {}", err);
1500:         }
1501:         if failed > 0 {
1502:             println!("  {} PCR(s) did not match expected values.", failed);
1503:         }
1504:     }
1505:     println!("  Manifest: {}", manifest_source);
1506:     Ok(attest_resp.verified)
1507: }
1508: fn export_vm(vm_id: &str, output_path: &str) -> Result<()> {
1509:     // 1. Locate VM dir
1510:     // HACK: Accessing .vyoma directly. CLI normally shouldn't allow this if daemon is remote.
1511:     // But for MVP, we assume local access.
1512:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or(anyhow::anyhow!("No home dir"))?;
1513:     let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(vm_id);
1514:     if !vm_dir.exists() {
1515:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("VM directory not found: {:?}", vm_dir));
1516:     }
1517:     // Check files
1518:     let snap_path = vm_dir.join("snapshot.snap");
1519:     let mem_path = vm_dir.join("memory.mem");
1520:     let cow_path = vm_dir.join("diff.cow");
1521:     if !snap_path.exists() || !mem_path.exists() {
1522:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1523:             "Snapshot files missing. Did you run 'vyoma snapshot <id>' first?"
1524:         ));
1525:     }
1526:     // 2. Create Tarball
1527:     let tar_file = File::create(output_path)?;
1528:     let enc = GzEncoder::new(tar_file, Compression::default());
1529:     let mut tar = tar::Builder::new(enc);
1530:     // Add files with specific names for portability
1531:     tar.append_path_with_name(&snap_path, "snapshot.snap")?;
1532:     tar.append_path_with_name(&mem_path, "memory.mem")?;
1533:     tar.append_path_with_name(&cow_path, "diff.cow")?;
1534:     tar.finish()?;
1535:     info!("Export complete: {}", output_path);
1536:     Ok(())
1537: }
1538: async fn import_vm(input_path: &str, daemon_url: &str) -> Result<()> {
1539:     // 1. Unpack to temp dir
1540:     let file = File::open(input_path)?;
1541:     let tar = GzDecoder::new(file);
1542:     let mut archive = Archive::new(tar);
1543:     let temp_dir = tempfile::tempdir()?;
1544:     info!("Unpacking to temporary location: {:?}", temp_dir.path());
1545:     archive.unpack(temp_dir.path())?;
1546:     // 2. Verify files
1547:     let snap_path = temp_dir.path().join("snapshot.snap");
1548:     let mem_path = temp_dir.path().join("memory.mem");
1549:     let cow_path = temp_dir.path().join("diff.cow");
1550:     if !snap_path.exists() || !mem_path.exists() || !cow_path.exists() {
1551:         return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
1552:             "Invalid export archive. Missing core files."
1553:         ));
1554:     }
1555:     // 3. Call Restore API
1556:     // Need absolute paths for Daemon to access them (Daemon must be on same FS)
1557:     let client = Client::new();
1558:     let payload = RestoreRequest {
1559:         snapshot_path: snap_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
1560:         mem_path: mem_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
1561:         cow_path: cow_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
1562:         original_vm_id: "imported".to_string(),
1563:     };
1564:     let resp = client
1565:         .post(format!("{}/restore", daemon_url))
1566:         .json(&payload)
1567:         .send()
1568:         .await;
1569:     handle_response(resp, &daemon_url).await?;
1570:     // NOTE: TempDir will be deleted when it goes out of scope!
1571:     // But Restore API copies the COW file, so that's fine for COW.
1572:     // However, Snapshot and Mem files are LOADED by Firecracker.
1573:     // Firecracker keeps them open? Or does it read them?
1574:     // Firecracker `load_snapshot` usually loads them.
1575:     // If we delete them too early, restoration might fail if it's async?
1576:     // Actually, `restore_vm` awaits `load_snapshot`.
1577:     // But does `load_snapshot` keep file handle or read into RAM?
1578:     // Firecracker loads into RAM.
1579:     // To be safe, we should probably PERSIST these unpacked files into a new VM directory BEFORE calling restore.
1580:     // But the Daemon creates the new VM directory.
1581:     // Ideally, Daemon should handle import upload.
1582:     // For MVP, we will rely on TempDir not being deleted until function returns,
1583:     // and `restore_vm` waits for success.
1584:     // But wait, `Cow` is copied. `Snapshot/Mem` are READ.
1585:     // If `restore_vm` finishes, the VM is running.
1586:     // Does Firecracker need `memory.mem` to stay on disk after load?
1587:     // Docs say: "The file is mmap()-ed". So YES, it must exist while VM is running?
1588:     // Actually, if it's MAP_PRIVATE, maybe not.
1589:     // If it's backing memory, deleting it is BAD.
1590:     // Safer Approach:
1591:     // Move these files to a permanent location?
1592:     // Since Daemon doesn't know about "Import", we are stuck.
1593:     // Let's rely on standard practice: The User (CLI) is responsible for data validity.
1594:     // We should unpack to a PERMANENT "imports" folder in .vyoma/imports/<uuid>/ ?
1595:     Ok(()) // temp_dir drop here.
1596: }

1626: async fn handle_response(resp: Result<reqwest::Response, reqwest::Error>, url: &str) -> Result<()> {
1627:     match resp {
1628:         Ok(response) => {
1629:             if response.status().is_success() {
1630:                 let body: RunResponse = response.json().await?;
1631:                 info!("Success! VM ID: {}, Status: {}", body.vm_id, body.status);
1632:             } else {
1633:                 let status = response.status();
1634:                 let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
1635:                 error!("Daemon returned error: {} - {}", status, text);
1636:             }
1637:         }
1638:         Err(e) => {
1639:             error!("Failed to connect to daemon at {}: {}", url, e);
1640:             info!("Is 'vyomad' running?");
1641:         }
1642:     }
1643:     Ok(())
1644: }
1645: async fn handle_simple_response(
1646:     resp: Result<reqwest::Response, reqwest::Error>,
1647:     url: &str,
1648: ) -> Result<()> {
1649:     match resp {
1650:         Ok(response) => {
1651:             if response.status().is_success() {
1652:                 let text = response.text().await?;
1653:                 info!("Success: {}", text);
1654:             } else {
1655:                 let status = response.status();
1656:                 let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
1657:                 error!("Daemon returned error: {} - {}", status, text);
1658:             }
1659:         }
1660:         Err(e) => {
1661:             error!("Failed to connect to daemon at {}: {}", url, e);
1662:             info!("Is 'vyomad' running?");
1663:         }
1664:     }
1665:     Ok(())
1666: }
1667: async fn start_service_helper(
1668:     client: &Client,
1669:     daemon_url: &str,
1670:     stack_name: &str,
1671:     name: &str,
1672:     service: &vyoma_compose::Service,
1673: ) -> Result<()> {
1674:     let image_target = if let Some(ref build) = service.build {
1675:         let context = match build {
1676:             vyoma_compose::BuildSource::Path(p) => p.clone(),
1677:             vyoma_compose::BuildSource::Config(c) => c.context.clone(),
1678:         };
1679:         info!("Building service '{}' from {}", name, context);
1680:         build_image_with_client(&context, client, daemon_url, false).await?
1681:     } else if let Some(ref img) = service.image {
1682:         img.clone()
1683:     } else {
1684:         error!("Service '{}' has no image or build context", name);
1685:         return Ok(());
1686:     };
1687:     // Prepare Ports
1688:     let mut port_mappings = Vec::new();
1689:     if let Some(ref ports) = service.ports {
1690:         for p in ports {
1691:             let parts: Vec<&str> = p.split(':').collect();
1692:             if parts.len() == 2 {
1693:                 let h = parts[0].parse().unwrap_or(0);
1694:                 let v = parts[1].parse().unwrap_or(0);
1695:                 port_mappings.push(PortMapping {
1696:                     host_port: h,
1697:                     vm_port: v,
1698:                 });
1699:             }
1700:         }
1701:     }
1702:     // Prepare Volumes
1703:     let mut volume_mounts = Vec::new();
1704:     if let Some(ref vols) = service.volumes {
1705:         for v in vols {
1706:             let parts: Vec<&str> = v.split(':').collect();
1707:             if parts.len() == 2 {
1708:                 volume_mounts.push(VolumeMount {
1709:                     host_path: parts[0].to_string(),
1710:                     vm_path: parts[1].to_string(),
1711:                     read_only: false,
1712:                 });
1713:             }
1714:         }
1715:     }
1716:     let payload = RunRequest {
1717:         image: image_target,
1718:         vcpu: service.cpus.unwrap_or(1),
1719:         mem_size_mib: service.memory.unwrap_or(512),
1720:         ports: port_mappings,
1721:         volumes: volume_mounts,
1722:         hostname: Some(name.to_string()),
1723:         labels: {
1724:             let mut l = HashMap::new();
1725:             l.insert("vyoma.stack".to_string(), stack_name.to_string());
1726:             l.insert("vyoma.service".to_string(), name.to_string());
1727:             l
1728:         },
1729:     };
1730:     let resp = client
1731:         .post(format!("{}/run", daemon_url))
1732:         .json(&payload)
1733:         .send()
1734:         .await;
1735:     match resp {
1736:         Ok(r) => {
1737:             if r.status().is_success() {
1738:                 let body: RunResponse = r.json().await?;
1739:                 info!("Service '{}' started as VM {}", name, body.vm_id);
1740:             } else {
1741:                 error!("Failed to start service '{}': {}", name, r.status());
1742:             }
1743:         }
1744:         Err(e) => error!("Failed to request start for '{}': {}", name, e),
1745:     }
1746:     Ok(())
1747: }
1748: async fn show_migration_progress(client: &reqwest::Client, daemon_url: &str, session_id: &str) {
1749:     use std::io::{self, Write};
1750:     let status_url = format!("{}/teleport/status/{}", daemon_url, session_id);
1751:     println!("\nMigration progress:");
1752:     println!("{}", "=".repeat(50));
1753:     loop {
1754:         let resp = client.get(&status_url).send().await;
1755:         match resp {
1756:             Ok(r) if r.status().is_success() => {
1757:                 if let Ok(data) = r.json::<serde_json::Value>().await {
1758:                     let status = data.get("status").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("unknown");
1759:                     let vm_id = data.get("vm_id").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
1760:                     println!("\rVM: {:20} Status: {:20}", vm_id, status);
1761:                     if let Some(progress) = data.get("progress") {
1762:                         let total = progress.get("total_pages").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);
1763:                         let transferred = progress.get("transferred_pages").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);
1764:                         let dirty = progress.get("dirty_pages").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);
1765:                         let round = progress.get("round").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);
1766:                         if total > 0 {
1767:                             let pct = (transferred as f64 / total as f64) * 100.0;
1768:                             let bar_len = 30;
1769:                             let filled = ((pct / 100.0) * bar_len as f64) as usize;
1770:                             let bar: String = format!("{}{}", "█".repeat(filled), "░".repeat(bar_len - filled));
1771:                             io::stdout().write_all(format!("\r[{}] {:5.1}% (round {}, dirty: {} pages)\r", bar, pct, round, dirty).as_bytes()).ok();
1772:                             io::stdout().flush().ok();
1773:                         }
1774:                     }
1775:                     if status == "completed" || status == "source_cleaned" {
1776:                         println!("\n\nMigration completed successfully!");
1777:                         break;
1778:                     }
1779:                     if status == "failed" {
1780:                         println!("\n\nMigration failed!");
1781:                         break;
1782:                     }
1783:                 }
1784:             }
1785:             _ => break,
1786:         }
1787:         tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;
1788:     }
1789: }

File: crates/vyomad/src/vm_service/policy.rs

  1: use std::collections::HashMap;
  2: use std::path::PathBuf;
  3: use std::sync::Arc;
  4: use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration, Instant};
  5: use tracing::{info, warn, error};
  6: use crate::state::{AppState, VmStatus};
  7: use vyoma_core::unified_attest::{UnifiedAttestationManager, UnifiedAttestationRequest};
  8: use vyoma_core::attest::{TpmQuote, AttestationResponse};
