 12: /// PCR comparison result for a single PCR index.
 13: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
 14: pub struct PcrResult {
 15:     pub pcr_index: u32,
 16:     pub expected: String,
 17:     pub actual: String,
 18:     pub verified: bool,
 19: }
 20: /// Result of an attestation verification.
 21: pub struct AttestationResult {
 22:     pub verified: bool,
 23:     pub pcr_results: Vec<PcrResult>,
 24:     pub signed_manifest_verified: bool,
 25:     pub error: Option<String>,
 26: }
 27: /// Configuration for measured boot verification behavior.
 28: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
 29: pub struct MeasuredBootConfig {
 30:     /// PCR indices to verify during attestation.
 31:     pub pcr_selection: Vec<u32>,
 32:     /// Timeout in seconds for the attestation check.
 33:     pub verification_timeout_secs: u64,
 34:     /// If true, block the VM (kill/pause) when attestation fails.
 35:     pub block_on_failure: bool,
 36:     /// If true, require manifest signature verification before trusting PCR values.
 37:     pub require_signed_manifest: bool,
 38:     /// Directory containing trusted public keys for manifest verification.
 39:     pub trusted_keys_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
 40: }
 41: impl Default for MeasuredBootConfig {
 42:     fn default() -> Self {
 43:         Self {
 44:             pcr_selection: vec![0, 1, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 14],
 45:             verification_timeout_secs: 30,
 46:             block_on_failure: true,
 47:             require_signed_manifest: true,
 48:             trusted_keys_dir: None,
 49:         }
 50:     }
 51: }
 52: /// Perform the full attestation check for a VM:
 53: /// 1. Get PCR quote from the vTPM
 54: /// 2. Load and verify the signed manifest
 55: /// 3. Compare PCR values against expected values
 56: /// 4. Update VM status based on result
 57: pub async fn check_policy_and_perform_attestation(
 58:     state: Arc<AppState>,
 59:     vm_id: String,
 60:     vm_dir: PathBuf,
 61:     image_name: String,
 62:     tpm_socket_path: Option<String>,
 63: ) -> Result<(), String> {
 64:     let needs_verification = {
 65:         let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
 66:         policy.must_verify_on_boot()
 67:     };
 68:     if !needs_verification {
 69:         info!("Policy verification not required for VM {}", vm_id);
 70:         update_vm_status(&state, &vm_id, VmStatus::Running).await?;
 71:         return Ok(());
 72:     }
 73:     info!("Measured boot policy requires attestation for VM {}", vm_id);
 74:     // Check if vTPM socket is available
 75:     let tpm_socket = match tpm_socket_path {
 76:         Some(path) => path,
 77:         None => {
 78:             let msg = format!("vTPM socket not available for VM {} - cannot perform attestation", vm_id);
 79:             error!("{}", msg);
 80:             handle_attestation_failure(&state, &vm_id, &msg).await;
 81:             return Err(msg);
 82:         }
 83:     };
 84:     // Build attestation config from policy
 85:     let attestation_config = build_attestation_config(&state);
 86:     let verification_timeout = Duration::from_secs(attestation_config.verification_timeout_secs);
 87:     info!("Starting attestation check for VM {} with timeout {}s", vm_id, verification_timeout.as_secs());
 88:     let start = Instant::now();
 89:     // Perform the attestation check with timeout
 90:     let result = timeout(verification_timeout, verify_attestation_from_manifest(
 91:         &image_name,
 92:         &tpm_socket,
 93:         &attestation_config,
 94:     )).await;
 95:     match result {
 96:         Ok(Ok(attestation_result)) => {
 97:             if attestation_result.verified {
 98:                 let elapsed = start.elapsed();
 99:                 info!("Attestation verification succeeded for VM {} in {:.2?}", vm_id, elapsed);
100:                 update_vm_status(&state, &vm_id, VmStatus::Running).await
101:                     .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to update VM status: {}", e))?;
102:                 // Update attestation_status to verified
103:                 {
104:                     let vm_arc = {
105:                         let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
106:                         vms.get(&vm_id).cloned()
107:                     };
108:                     if let Some(vm_arc) = vm_arc {
109:                         let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
110:                         vm.attestation_status = Some("VERIFIED".to_string());
111:                     }
112:                 }
113:                 // Emit SSE event for attestation success
114:                 let _ = state.events_tx.send(serde_json::json!({
115:                     "type": "vm_attestation",
116:                     "vm_id": vm_id,
117:                     "verified": true,
118:                     "error": null
119:                 }).to_string());
120:                 Ok(())
121:             } else {
122:                 let elapsed = start.elapsed();
123:                 let error_msg = attestation_result.error
124:                     .unwrap_or_else(|| "Attestation failed".to_string());
125:                 error!("Attestation verification failed for VM {} after {:.2?}: {}", vm_id, elapsed, error_msg);
126:                 handle_attestation_failure(&state, &vm_id, &format!("Attestation failed: {}", error_msg)).await;
127:                 Err(error_msg)
128:             }
129:         }
130:         Ok(Err(e)) => {
131:             let elapsed = start.elapsed();
132:             error!("Attestation verification failed for VM {} after {:.2?}: {}", vm_id, elapsed, e);
133:             handle_attestation_failure(&state, &vm_id, &format!("Attestation failed: {}", e)).await;
134:             Err(e)
135:         }
136:         Err(_) => {
137:             // Timeout elapsed
138:             let elapsed = start.elapsed();
139:             let msg = format!("Attestation timed out after {:.2?} for VM {}", elapsed, vm_id);
140:             error!("{}", msg);
141:             handle_attestation_failure(&state, &vm_id, &msg).await;
142:             Err(msg)
143:         }
144:     }
145: }
146: /// The core attestation check logic - gets PCR quote, loads manifest, verifies.
147: /// Returns an AttestationResult with detailed verification info.
148: pub async fn verify_attestation_from_manifest(
149:     image_name: &str,
150:     tpm_socket: &str,
151:     config: &MeasuredBootConfig,
152: ) -> Result<AttestationResult, String> {
153:     // Step 1: Get PCR quote from the vTPM
154:     info!("Getting PCR quote from vTPM for image {}", image_name);
155:     let quote_data = get_pcr_quote("attest", tpm_socket)
156:         .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to get PCR quote: {}", e))?;
157:     info!("Retrieved PCR quote ({} bytes) for image {}", quote_data.len(), image_name);
158:     // Step 2: Parse the PCR quote to extract PCR values
159:     let live_pcrs = vyoma_core::attest::parse_pcr_values(&quote_data)
160:         .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to parse PCR values from quote: {}", e))?;
161:     info!("Parsed {} PCR values from quote for image {}", live_pcrs.len(), image_name);
162:     // Step 3: Load and verify the signed manifest
163:     info!("Loading signed manifest for image {}", image_name);
164:     let signed_manifest = load_and_verify_manifest(image_name, config).await?;
165:     info!("Successfully loaded and verified signed manifest for image {}", image_name);
166:     // Step 4: Extract expected PCR values from the manifest
167:     let expected_pcrs = signed_manifest.manifest.measured_boot.pcr_policy
168:         .ok_or_else(|| format!("No PCR policy found in manifest for image {}", image_name))?;
169:     info!("Found {} expected PCR values in manifest for image {}", expected_pcrs.len(), image_name);
170:     // Step 5: Build an AttestationResponse from the parsed quote
171:     let attestation_response = AttestationResponse {
172:         vm_id: image_name.to_string(),
173:         verified: true,
174:         quote: Some(TpmQuote {
175:             quote: quote_data,
176:             signature: Vec::new(),
177:             pcr_values: live_pcrs.clone(),
178:             timestamp: String::new(),
179:         }),
180:         pcr_results: HashMap::new(),
181:         error: None,
182:     };
183:     // Step 6: Verify PCR values using the unified attestation manager
184:     let verifier = UnifiedAttestationManager::new();
185:     let result = verifier.verify_tpm_attestation(&attestation_response, &expected_pcrs)
186:         .map_err(|e| format!("Attestation verification error: {}", e))?;
187:     // Step 7: Build detailed PCR results
188:     let mut pcr_results = Vec::new();
189:     for (pcr_index, expected_hash) in &expected_pcrs {
190:         let actual_hash = live_pcrs.get(pcr_index).cloned().unwrap_or_default();
191:         let verified = actual_hash == *expected_hash;
192:         pcr_results.push(PcrResult {
193:             pcr_index: *pcr_index,
194:             expected: expected_hash.clone(),
195:             actual: actual_hash,
196:             verified,
197:         });
198:     }
199:     let signed_manifest_verified = result.verified;
200:     if !result.verified {
201:         let error_msg = result.error
202:             .unwrap_or_else(|| "PCR verification failed".to_string());
203:         // Log which PCRs failed
204:         for pcr_result in &pcr_results {
205:             if !pcr_result.verified {
206:                 warn!("PCR {} verification FAILED - expected value did not match live value: {}",
207:                     pcr_result.pcr_index, pcr_result.actual);
208:             }
209:         }
210:         return Ok(AttestationResult {
211:             verified: false,
212:             pcr_results,
213:             signed_manifest_verified,
214:             error: Some(error_msg),
215:         });
216:     }
217:     // Additional direct check: verify all expected PCRs are present and match
218:     for (pcr_index, expected_hash) in &expected_pcrs {
219:         if let Some(actual_hash) = live_pcrs.get(pcr_index) {
220:             if actual_hash != expected_hash {
221:                 return Ok(AttestationResult {
222:                     verified: false,
223:                     pcr_results,
224:                     signed_manifest_verified,
225:                     error: Some(format!(
226:                         "PCR {} mismatch after unified verification: expected {}, got {}",
227:                         pcr_index, expected_hash, actual_hash
228:                     )),
229:                 });
230:             }
231:         } else {
232:             return Ok(AttestationResult {
233:                 verified: false,
234:                 pcr_results,
235:                 signed_manifest_verified,
236:                 error: Some(format!(
237:                     "PCR {} not found in live quote but expected in manifest",
238:                     pcr_index
239:                 )),
240:             });
241:         }
242:     }
243:     info!("All PCR values verified successfully for image {}", image_name);
244:     Ok(AttestationResult {
245:         verified: true,
246:         pcr_results,
247:         signed_manifest_verified,
248:         error: None,
249:     })
250: }
251: /// Get a PCR quote from the vTPM using the tpm2-tools.
252: ///
253: /// This connects to the vTPM socket and retrieves a quote containing
254: /// the specified PCR values.
255: pub fn get_pcr_quote(vm_id: &str, tpm_socket: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<u8>> {
256:     use std::process::Command;
257:     let pcr_list = TPM_QUOTE_PCRS.iter()
258:         .map(|p| p.to_string())
259:         .collect::<Vec<_>>()
260:         .join(",");
261:     let output = Command::new("tpm2_quote")
262:         .args(&[
263:             "-T", &format!("socket:path={}", tpm_socket),
264:             "-c", "/etc/tpm2/tpm2-quote.ak",
265:             "-g", "sha256",
266:             "-p", &pcr_list,
267:             "-o", &format!("/tmp/{}_quote.bin", vm_id),
268:             "-s", &format!("/tmp/{}_sig.bin", vm_id),
269:             "-m", &format!("/tmp/{}_msg.bin", vm_id),
270:             "-f", "sha256",
271:         ])
272:         .output()
273:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to get TPM quote: {}", e))?;
274:     if !output.status.success() {
275:         let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
276:         anyhow::bail!("tpm2_quote failed: {}", stderr);
277:     }
278:     let quote_path = format!("/tmp/{}_quote.bin", vm_id);
279:     std::fs::read(&quote_path)
280:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to read quote: {}", e))
281: }
282: /// Find the image directory on disk, trying multiple known locations.
283: fn find_image_dir(image_name: &str) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf, String> {
284:     let home = dirs::home_dir()
285:         .ok_or_else(|| "No home directory".to_string())?;
286:     // Try ~/.vyoma/images/ first (used by the build pipeline)
287:     let vyoma_images = home.join(".vyoma").join("images");
288:     let image_dir = vyoma_images.join(image_name.replace('/', "_").replace(':', "_"));
289:     if image_dir.exists() {
290:         return Ok(image_dir);
291:     }
292:     // Fallback to ~/.vyoma/images/
293:     let vyoma_images = home.join(".vyoma").join("images");
294:     let image_dir = vyoma_images.join(image_name.replace('/', "_").replace(':', "_"));
295:     if image_dir.exists() {
296:         return Ok(image_dir);
297:     }
298:     Err(format!("Image directory not found for image: {}", image_name))
299: }
300: /// Load and verify the signed manifest from disk.
301: ///
302: /// If `require_signed_manifest` is true in the config, this will:
303: /// 1. Load the signed manifest (.sig file)
304: /// 2. Verify the manifest signature against trusted keys
305: /// 3. Return the verified manifest
306: ///
307: /// If the image is unsigned and `require_signed_manifest` is true, this fails.
308: /// If `require_signed_manifest` is false, unsigned manifests are accepted
309: /// but PCR verification is skipped (returns None for pcr_policy).
310: async fn load_and_verify_manifest(
311:     image_name: &str,
312:     config: &MeasuredBootConfig,
313: ) -> Result<SignedManifest, String> {
314:     // Find the image directory
315:     let image_dir = find_image_dir(image_name)?;
316:     // Try to load signed manifest first (.sig file)
317:     let sig_path = image_dir.join("vyoma.toml.sig");
318:     if sig_path.exists() {
319:         info!("Loading signed manifest from {:?}", sig_path);
320:         let signed_manifest = VmifConverter::load_signed_manifest(&sig_path)
321:             .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to load signed manifest: {}", e))?;
322:         // If signature verification is required, verify against trusted keys
323:         if config.require_signed_manifest {
324:             info!("Verifying manifest signature against trusted keys");
325:             let trusted_keys_dir = config.trusted_keys_dir
326:                 .as_ref()
327:                 .cloned()
328:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| {
329:                     let home = dirs::home_dir()
330:                         .expect("No home directory");
331:                     home.join(".vyoma").join("keys").join("trusted")
332:                 });
333:             let mut trust_policy = vyoma_image::signing::TrustPolicy::new(true);
334:             trust_policy.load_trusted_keys_from_dir(trusted_keys_dir)
335:                 .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to load trusted keys: {}", e))?;
336:             trust_policy.verify(&signed_manifest)
337:                 .map_err(|e| format!("Manifest signature verification failed: {}", e))?;
338:             info!("Manifest signature verified successfully");
339:         } else {
340:             // Even without requiring signed manifests, verify the signature is valid
341:             // if keys are available (best effort)
342:             let trusted_keys_dir = config.trusted_keys_dir
343:                 .as_ref()
344:                 .cloned()
345:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| {
346:                     let home = dirs::home_dir()
347:                         .expect("No home directory");
348:                     home.join(".vyoma").join("keys").join("trusted")
349:                 });
350:             if trusted_keys_dir.exists() {
351:                 let mut trust_policy = vyoma_image::signing::TrustPolicy::new(false);
352:                 if let Ok(()) = trust_policy.load_trusted_keys_from_dir(trusted_keys_dir) {
353:                     if let Err(e) = trust_policy.verify(&signed_manifest) {
354:                         warn!("Manifest signature verification failed (non-fatal): {}", e);
355:                     } else {
356:                         info!("Manifest signature verified successfully (non-fatal)");
357:                     }
358:                 }
359:             }
360:         }
361:         Ok(signed_manifest)
362:     } else {
363:         // No signed manifest - try loading unsigned manifest
364:         let manifest_path = image_dir.join("vyoma.toml");
365:         if !manifest_path.exists() {
366:             return Err(format!(
367:                 "Neither signed manifest (vyoma.toml.sig) nor unsigned manifest (vyoma.toml) found in {:?}",
368:                 image_dir
369:             ));
370:         }
371:         if config.require_signed_manifest {
372:             return Err(format!(
373:                 "Image {} has no signed manifest (vyoma.toml.sig) - signed manifest required by policy. \
374:                  Build the image with --measured flag to generate a signed manifest.",
375:                 image_name
376:             ));
377:         }
378:         // Accept unsigned manifest but skip PCR verification - log warning
379:         warn!(
380:             "Image {} has no signed manifest - PCR verification will be SKIPPED (not recommended for production)",
381:             image_name
382:         );
383:         let manifest = VmifConverter::load_manifest(&manifest_path)
384:             .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to load unsigned manifest: {}", e))?;
385:         // Wrap in a SignedManifest for compatibility with verification code
386:         Ok(SignedManifest {
387:             manifest,
388:             signature: Vec::new(),
389:             public_key: Vec::new(),
390:         })
391:     }
392: }
393: /// Handle attestation failure based on policy configuration.
394: ///
395: /// If `block_on_failure` is true, the VM is killed immediately.
396: /// The VM status is set to AttestationFailed with the failure reason.
397: async fn handle_attestation_failure(state: &Arc<AppState>, vm_id: &str, reason: &str) {
398:     error!("Attestation failure for VM {}: {}", vm_id, reason);
399:     // Set VM status to AttestationFailed
400:     if let Err(e) = update_vm_status(state, vm_id, VmStatus::AttestationFailed {
401:         reason: reason.to_string(),
402:     }).await {
403:         error!("Failed to update VM status after attestation failure: {}", e);
404:     }
405:     // Update attestation_status field
406:     {
407:         let vm_arc = {
408:             let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
409:             vms.get(vm_id).cloned()
410:         };
411:         if let Some(vm_arc) = vm_arc {
412:             let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
413:             vm.attestation_status = Some("FAILED".to_string());
414:         }
415:     }
416:     // Emit SSE event for attestation failure
417:     let _ = state.events_tx.send(serde_json::json!({
418:         "type": "vm_attestation",
419:         "vm_id": vm_id,
420:         "verified": false,
421:         "error": reason
422:     }).to_string());
423:     // Check if we should kill the VM
424:     let should_block = {
425:         let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
426:         policy.get_config().measured_boot.block_on_failure
427:     };
428:     if should_block {
429:         info!("Block-on-failure enabled - killing VM {}", vm_id);
430:         if let Err(e) = kill_vm(state, vm_id).await {
431:             error!("Failed to kill VM {} after attestation failure: {}", vm_id, e);
432:         }
433:     }
434: }
435: /// Force-stop a VM by removing it from the active VM map and cleaning up resources.
436: async fn kill_vm(state: &Arc<AppState>, vm_id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
437:     let vm_arc = {
438:         let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
439:         vms.remove(vm_id)
440:     };
441:     if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
442:         let mut vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
443:         // Kill the VMM process
444:         let _ = vm.vmm.kill();
445:         info!("VM {} killed due to attestation failure", vm_id);
446:     }
447:     Ok(())
448: }
449: /// Build a MeasuredBootConfig from the current policy manager state.
450: pub fn build_attestation_config(state: &Arc<AppState>) -> MeasuredBootConfig {
451:     let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
452:     let config = policy.get_config();
453:     MeasuredBootConfig {
454:         pcr_selection: config.measured_boot.pcr_selection.clone(),
455:         verification_timeout_secs: config.measured_boot.verification_timeout_secs,
456:         block_on_failure: config.measured_boot.block_on_failure,
457:         require_signed_manifest: true,
458:         trusted_keys_dir: config.measured_boot.trusted_keys_dir
459:             .as_ref()
460:             .map(PathBuf::from)
461:             .or_else(|| {
462:                 config.measured_boot.build_signing_key_path
463:                     .as_ref()
464:                     .map(PathBuf::from)
465:             })
466:             .or_else(|| {
467:                 let home = dirs::home_dir()?;
468:                 Some(home.join(".vyoma").join("keys").join("trusted"))
469:             }),
470:     }
471: }
472: /// The standard PCR indices measured during UEFI Secure Boot.
473: ///
474: /// These correspond to:
475: /// - PCR 0:  Firmware (BIOS/UEFI)
476: /// - PCR 1:  Firmware configuration
477: /// - PCR 4:  Boot manager
478: /// - PCR 5:  Boot manager configuration
479: /// - PCR 7:  Secure Boot state
480: /// - PCR 9:  Kernel image
481: /// - PCR 10: Initrd/initramfs
482: /// - PCR 14: Rootfs
483: const TPM_QUOTE_PCRS: &[u32] = &[0, 1, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 14];
484: /// Update the status of a VM in the shared state.
485: pub async fn update_vm_status(state: &AppState, vm_id: &str, new_status: VmStatus) -> Result<(), String> {
486:     let vm_arc = {
487:         let vms = state.vms.lock().await;
488:         vms.get(vm_id).cloned()
489:     };
490:     let vm_arc = match vm_arc {
491:         Some(arc) => arc,
492:         None => return Err(format!("VM {} not found in state", vm_id)),
493:     };
494:     {
495:         let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
496:         vm.status = new_status.clone();
497:     }
498:     // Save updated state to disk
499:     {
500:         let vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
501:         vm.save_state()
502:             .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to save VM state: {}", e))?;
503:     }
504:     info!("Updated VM {} status to {:?}", vm_id, new_status);
505:     Ok(())
506: }
507: #[cfg(test)]
508: mod tests {
509:     use super::*;
510:     use std::collections::HashMap;
511:     #[test]
512:     fn test_pcr_quote_pcr_list_format() {
513:         let pcr_list = TPM_QUOTE_PCRS.iter()
514:             .map(|p| p.to_string())
515:             .collect::<Vec<_>>()
516:             .join(",");
517:         assert_eq!(pcr_list, "0,1,4,5,7,9,10,14");
518:     }
519:     #[test]
520:     fn test_measured_boot_config_default() {
521:         let config = MeasuredBootConfig::default();
522:         assert_eq!(config.pcr_selection, vec![0, 1, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 14]);
523:         assert_eq!(config.verification_timeout_secs, 30);
524:         assert!(config.block_on_failure);
525:         assert!(config.require_signed_manifest);
526:     }
527:     #[test]
528:     fn test_build_attestation_config() {
529:         let state = AppState::new_test();
530:         {
531:             let mut policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
532:             policy.set_require_measured_boot(true);
533:         }
534:         let config = build_attestation_config(&Arc::new(state));
535:         assert_eq!(config.verification_timeout_secs, 30);
536:         assert!(config.block_on_failure);
537:     }
538:     #[test]
539:     fn test_pcr_idx_conversion() {
540:         assert_eq!(pcr_idx_to_usize(&0), 0);
541:         assert_eq!(pcr_idx_to_usize(&14), 14);
542:         assert_eq!(pcr_idx_to_usize(&9), 9);
543:     }
544:     #[test]
545:     fn test_measured_boot_config_custom_timeout() {
546:         let config = MeasuredBootConfig {
547:             verification_timeout_secs: 60,
548:             ..Default::default()
549:         };
550:         assert_eq!(config.verification_timeout_secs, 60);
551:     }
552:     #[test]
553:     fn test_find_image_dir_not_found() {
554:         let result = find_image_dir("nonexistent-image-xyz");
555:         assert!(result.is_err());
556:     }
557: }
558: fn pcr_idx_to_usize(pcr_idx: &u32) -> usize {
559:     *pcr_idx as usize
560: }

================
File: packaging/rpm/build.sh
================
  1: #!/bin/bash
  2: set -e
  3: VERSION="2.7.0"
  4: WORK_DIR="target/rpm"
  5: SOURCES_DIR="${WORK_DIR}/SOURCES/vyoma-${VERSION}"
  6: # Hardcoded checksums for binary verification
  7: # Computed from official release sources
  8: CLOUD_HYPERVISOR_CHECKSUM="8a013003ae29f59da7b2d7ac67f19eea3ea535166dac00ff5e8c2c27ac643f8a"
  9: CNI_PLUGINS_CHECKSUM="77baa2f669980a82255ffa2f2717de823992480271ee778aa51a9c60ae89ff9b"
 10: # Verify checksum of a downloaded file
 11: # Usage: verify_checksum <file> <expected_sha256>
 12: verify_checksum() {
 13:     local file="$1"
 14:     local expected="$2"
 15:     if [ "$VYOMA_SKIP_CHECKSUM" = "1" ]; then
 16:         echo "Skipping checksum verification (VYOMA_SKIP_CHECKSUM=1)"
 17:         return 0
 18:     fi
 19:     if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
 20:         echo "Error: file $file not found for checksum verification"
 21:         return 1
 22:     fi
 23:     local actual
 24:     actual=$(sha256sum "$file" | awk '{print $1}')
 25:     if [ "$actual" != "$expected" ]; then
 26:         echo "CHECKSUM MISMATCH for $file"
 27:         echo "Expected: $expected"
 28:         echo "Actual:   $actual"
 29:         return 1
 30:     fi
 31:     echo "Checksum OK: $file"
 32:     return 0
 33: }
 34: # Clean up any previous build
 35: rm -rf "${WORK_DIR}"
 36: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"/{BUILD,RPMS,SOURCES,SPECS,SRPMS}
 37: mkdir -p "${SOURCES_DIR}"
 38: # 1. Build Binaries
 39: cargo build --release --bin vyomad --bin vyoma
 40: # 2. Copy binaries to source dir
 41: cp target/release/vyomad "${SOURCES_DIR}/"
 42: cp target/release/vyoma "${SOURCES_DIR}/"
 43: # 3. Fetch Dependencies with checksum verification
 44: echo "Fetching dependencies..."
 45: # Fetch Cloud Hypervisor with checksum verification
 46: if [ ! -f "cloud-hypervisor" ]; then
 47:     echo "Downloading cloud-hypervisor v41.0..."
 48:     wget -q -O cloud-hypervisor https://github.com/cloud-hypervisor/cloud-hypervisor/releases/download/v41.0/cloud-hypervisor
 49:     chmod +x cloud-hypervisor
 50: fi
 51: echo "Verifying cloud-hypervisor checksum..."
 52: if ! verify_checksum "cloud-hypervisor" "$CLOUD_HYPERVISOR_CHECKSUM"; then
 53:     echo "ERROR: cloud-hypervisor checksum verification failed!"
 54:     rm -f cloud-hypervisor
 55:     exit 1
 56: fi
 57: cp cloud-hypervisor "${SOURCES_DIR}/"
 58: cp packaging/systemd/vyomad.service "${SOURCES_DIR}/"
 59: cp packaging/systemd/vyoma-net.service "${SOURCES_DIR}/"
 60: # Fetch CNI plugins with checksum verification
 61: CNI_VERSION="v1.5.1"
 62: CNI_DIR="${SOURCES_DIR}/cni/bin"
 63: echo "Fetching CNI plugins..."
 64: mkdir -p "${CNI_DIR}"
 65: if [ ! -f "cni-plugins.tgz" ]; then
 66:     echo "Downloading CNI plugins ${CNI_VERSION}..."
 67:     wget -q -O cni-plugins.tgz "https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/releases/download/${CNI_VERSION}/cni-plugins-linux-amd64-${CNI_VERSION}.tgz"
 68: fi
 69: echo "Verifying CNI plugins checksum..."
 70: if ! verify_checksum "cni-plugins.tgz" "$CNI_PLUGINS_CHECKSUM"; then
 71:     echo "ERROR: CNI plugins checksum verification failed!"
 72:     rm -f cni-plugins.tgz
 73:     exit 1
 74: fi
 75: tar -xzf cni-plugins.tgz -C "${CNI_DIR}/"
 76: # Copy UI (built by workflow step or locally)
 77: echo "Bundling UI..."
 78: UI_AVAILABLE=false
 79: if [ -d "ui/dist" ]; then
 80:     mkdir -p "${SOURCES_DIR}/ui/dist"
 81:     cp -r ui/dist/* "${SOURCES_DIR}/ui/dist/"
 82:     echo "UI bundled successfully"
 83:     UI_AVAILABLE=true
 84: else
 85:     echo "Warning: UI not found - dashboard will not be available"
 86: fi
 87: # Copy kernel binary
 88: if [ -f "bin/vmlinux" ]; then
 89:     mkdir -p "${SOURCES_DIR}/bin"
 90:     cp bin/vmlinux "${SOURCES_DIR}/bin/vmlinux"
 91:     echo "Kernel binary bundled"
 92: else
 93:     echo "Warning: bin/vmlinux not found - VMs will not start"
 94: fi
 95: # Bundle virtiofsd (from system or project bin)
 96: if [ -f "bin/virtiofsd" ] && [ ! -L "bin/virtiofsd" ]; then
 97:     cp bin/virtiofsd "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
 98:     chmod +x "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
 99:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from project bin"
100: elif [ -f "/usr/libexec/virtiofsd" ]; then
101:     cp /usr/libexec/virtiofsd "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
102:     chmod +x "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
103:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from system"
104: elif [ -f "/usr/bin/virtiofsd" ]; then
105:     cp /usr/bin/virtiofsd "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
106:     chmod +x "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd"
107:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from /usr/bin"
108: else
109:     echo "Warning: virtiofsd not found - volume mounts may not work"
110: fi
111: # 4. Create Source Tarball
112: cd "${WORK_DIR}/SOURCES"
113: tar -czf "vyoma-${VERSION}.tar.gz" "vyoma-${VERSION}"
114: cd ../../..
115: # 5. Create SPEC File
116: SPEC_CONTENT="Name:       vyoma
117: Version:    ${VERSION}
118: Release:    1%{?dist}
119: Summary:    MicroVM Ecosystem
120: License:    MIT
121: URL:        https://github.com/Subeshrock/micro-vm-ecosystem
122: Source0:    vyoma-${VERSION}.tar.gz
123: Requires:   iptables
124: Requires:   kmod
125: %description
126: Vyoma is a lightweight MicroVM runtime for running containers
127: as lightweight virtual machines. Combines Cloud Hypervisor speed with Docker UX.
128: Includes CLI, Daemon, Web UI, CNI plugins, and virtiofsd for volume mounts.
129: %prep
130: %setup -q
131: %install
132: mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/bin
133: mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/lib/vyoma
134: mkdir -p %{buildroot}/etc/systemd/system
135: install -m 755 vyomad %{buildroot}/usr/bin/vyomad
136: install -m 755 vyoma %{buildroot}/usr/bin/vyoma
137: install -m 755 cloud-hypervisor %{buildroot}/usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor
138: install -m 644 vyomad.service %{buildroot}/etc/systemd/system/vyomad.service
139: install -m 644 vyoma-net.service %{buildroot}/etc/systemd/system/vyoma-net.service
140: # Install CNI plugins
141: cp -r cni %{buildroot}/usr/lib/vyoma/cni
142: # Install kernel binary
143: mkdir -p %{buildroot}/var/lib/vyoma/bin
144: install -m 755 bin/vmlinux %{buildroot}/var/lib/vyoma/bin/vmlinux"
145: if [ "$UI_AVAILABLE" = true ]; then
146:     SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
147: # Install UI
148: cp -r ui %{buildroot}/usr/lib/vyoma/ui"
149: fi
150: if [ -f "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd" ]; then
151:     SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
152: install -m 755 virtiofsd %{buildroot}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
153: fi
154: SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
155: %files
156: /usr/bin/vyomad
157: /usr/bin/vyoma
158: /usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor
159: /etc/systemd/system/vyomad.service
160: /usr/lib/vyoma/cni
161: /var/lib/vyoma/bin/vmlinux"
162: if [ "$UI_AVAILABLE" = true ]; then
163:     SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
164: /usr/lib/vyoma/ui"
165: fi
166: if [ -f "${SOURCES_DIR}/virtiofsd" ]; then
167:     SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
168: /usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
169: fi
170: SPEC_CONTENT="$SPEC_CONTENT
171: %preun
172: # Stop service before package removal (not on upgrade)
173: # \$1 = 0 means removal, \$1 = 1 means upgrade
174: if [ \$1 -eq 0 ]; then
175:     systemctl stop vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
176:     systemctl disable vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
177: fi
178: %post
179: # $1 = 1: fresh install, $1 = 2: upgrade
180: if ! getent passwd vyoma > /dev/null 2>&1; then
181:     useradd -r -s /sbin/nologin -c 'Vyoma daemon' -d /var/lib/vyoma vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
182: fi
183: # Add vyoma daemon user to kvm group (for /dev/kvm access)
184: if getent group kvm > /dev/null 2>&1; then
185:     usermod -aG kvm vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
186: fi
187: # Fix /dev/kvm permissions
188: chmod 0660 /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null || true
189: chown root:kvm /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null || true
190: # Fix /dev/mapper/control permissions (for device mapper/DM)
191: chmod 0660 /dev/mapper/control 2>/dev/null || true
192: chown root:disk /dev/mapper/control 2>/dev/null || true
193: # Create runtime directory - owned by root:vyoma, group writable (0775)
194: mkdir -p /run/vyoma
195: chown root:vyoma /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
196: chmod 0775 /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
197: # Setup CNI plugins directory (symlink from system data dir to package location)
198: mkdir -p /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni 2>/dev/null || true
199: rm -rf /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni/bin 2>/dev/null || true
200: ln -sf /usr/lib/vyoma/cni/bin /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni/bin 2>/dev/null || true
201: # Setup Vyoma network bridge (for VM networking)
202: if ! ip link show vyoma-br0 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
203:     ip link add vyoma-br0 type bridge
204:     ip addr add 172.16.0.1/24 dev vyoma-br0
205:     ip link set vyoma-br0 up
206: fi
207: # Reload systemd and enable service (idempotent)
208: systemctl daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
209: systemctl enable vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
210: # Only start on fresh install (\$1 = 1); on upgrade (\$1 = 2), try-restart if running
211: if [ \$1 -eq 1 ]; then
212:     systemctl start vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
213: else
214:     systemctl try-restart vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
215: fi
216: %postun
217: # \$1 = 0: package removal, \$1 = 1: upgrade
218: if [ \$1 -eq 0 ]; then
219:     # Package removal - cleanup (not on upgrade)
220:     userdel vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
221:     rm -rf /var/lib/vyoma /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
222:     systemctl daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
223: fi
224: %changelog
225: * Wed Apr 01 2026 Subeshrock <subesh.rock.3@gmail.com> - ${VERSION}-1
226: - Run daemon as root with capabilities (no sudo needed)
227: - Remove sudoers configuration
228: - Fix socket and KVM permissions
229: - Bundle CNI plugins for networking
230: - Bundle UI in package
231: - Fix /run/vyoma directory permissions (0775)
232: "
233: echo "$SPEC_CONTENT" > "${WORK_DIR}/SPECS/vyoma.spec"
234: # 6. Build RPM
235: rpmbuild --define "_topdir $(pwd)/${WORK_DIR}" -bb "${WORK_DIR}/SPECS/vyoma.spec"
236: # 7. Move to dist
237: mkdir -p dist
238: mv ${WORK_DIR}/RPMS/x86_64/*.rpm dist/
239: # 8. Cleanup
240: rm -f cloud-hypervisor cloud-hypervisor.tgz virtiofsd.zip virtiofsd virtiofsd_bin cni-plugins.tgz
241: rm -rf release-v1.7.0-x86_64
242: rm -rf "${WORK_DIR}"
243: echo "RPM created in dist/"

================
File: tests/e2e/03_builder.sh
================
 1: #!/bin/bash
 2: set -e
 3: source tests/e2e/common.sh
 4: echo "=== Test 03: Builder (Vyomafile) ==="
 5: check_root
 6: setup_env
 7: # Start Daemon
 8: echo "Starting Daemon (3003)..."
 9: sudo -E $VYOMAD_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3003 > $TEST_HOME/daemon.log 2>&1 &
10: DAEMON_PID=$!
11: sleep 3
12: VYOMA="$VYOMA_BIN --socket-path /run/vyoma/test.sock --http-port 3003"
13: # 1. Setup Context
14: CTX=$TEST_HOME/build_ctx
15: mkdir -p $CTX
16: cat <<EOF > $CTX/Vyomafile
17: FROM alpine:latest
18: RUN echo "Vyoma Build Test" > /build_test.txt
19: EOF
20: # 2. Build
21: echo "Building Image..."
22: # Output parsing needed? vyoma build currently prints to stdout?
23: OUTPUT=$($VYOMA build $CTX 2>&1)
24: echo "$OUTPUT"
25: assert_success "Build Command"
26: # 3. Verify Build Succeeded
27: # Check output contains "Build complete" or image ID
28: if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Build complete"; then
29:     echo -e "${GREEN}Pass: Build completed successfully${NC}"
30: else
31:     echo -e "${RED}Fail: Build did not complete${NC}"
32:     exit 1
33: fi
34: cleanup_env $DAEMON_PID
35: echo "=== Test 03 Passed ==="

================
File: packaging/deb/build.sh
================
  1: #!/bin/bash
  2: set -e
  3: VERSION="2.7.0"
  4: ARCH="amd64"
  5: PKG_NAME="vyoma"
  6: WORK_DIR="target/debian/${PKG_NAME}_${VERSION}_${ARCH}"
  7: # Hardcoded checksums for binary verification
  8: # Computed from official release sources
  9: CLOUD_HYPERVISOR_CHECKSUM="8a013003ae29f59da7b2d7ac67f19eea3ea535166dac00ff5e8c2c27ac643f8a"
 10: CNI_PLUGINS_CHECKSUM="77baa2f669980a82255ffa2f2717de823992480271ee778aa51a9c60ae89ff9b"
 11: # Verify checksum of a downloaded file
 12: # Usage: verify_checksum <file> <expected_sha256>
 13: verify_checksum() {
 14:     local file="$1"
 15:     local expected="$2"
 16:     if [ "$VYOMA_SKIP_CHECKSUM" = "1" ]; then
 17:         echo "Skipping checksum verification (VYOMA_SKIP_CHECKSUM=1)"
 18:         return 0
 19:     fi
 20:     if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
 21:         echo "Error: file $file not found for checksum verification"
 22:         return 1
 23:     fi
 24:     local actual
 25:     actual=$(sha256sum "$file" | awk '{print $1}')
 26:     if [ "$actual" != "$expected" ]; then
 27:         echo "CHECKSUM MISMATCH for $file"
 28:         echo "Expected: $expected"
 29:         echo "Actual:   $actual"
 30:         return 1
 31:     fi
 32:     echo "Checksum OK: $file"
 33:     return 0
 34: }
 35: echo "Building Vyoma v${VERSION} for Debian..."
 36: # 1. Build Binaries
 37: cargo build --release --bin vyomad --bin vyoma
 38: # 2. Prepare Directory Structure
 39: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/usr/bin"
 40: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma"
 41: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/etc/systemd/system"
 42: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN"
 43: # 3. Fetch & Copy Dependencies (Cloud Hypervisor, Virtiofsd, CNI plugins)
 44: echo "Fetching dependencies..."
 45: # Fetch Cloud Hypervisor with checksum verification
 46: if [ ! -f "cloud-hypervisor" ]; then
 47:     echo "Downloading cloud-hypervisor v41.0..."
 48:     wget -q -O cloud-hypervisor https://github.com/cloud-hypervisor/cloud-hypervisor/releases/download/v41.0/cloud-hypervisor
 49:     chmod +x cloud-hypervisor
 50: fi
 51: echo "Verifying cloud-hypervisor checksum..."
 52: if ! verify_checksum "cloud-hypervisor" "$CLOUD_HYPERVISOR_CHECKSUM"; then
 53:     echo "ERROR: cloud-hypervisor checksum verification failed!"
 54:     rm -f cloud-hypervisor
 55:     exit 1
 56: fi
 57: # Fetch CNI plugins with checksum verification
 58: CNI_VERSION="v1.5.1"
 59: CNI_DIR="${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/cni"
 60: echo "Fetching CNI plugins..."
 61: mkdir -p "${CNI_DIR}/bin"
 62: if [ ! -f "cni-plugins.tgz" ]; then
 63:     echo "Downloading CNI plugins ${CNI_VERSION}..."
 64:     wget -q -O cni-plugins.tgz "https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/releases/download/${CNI_VERSION}/cni-plugins-linux-amd64-${CNI_VERSION}.tgz"
 65: fi
 66: echo "Verifying CNI plugins checksum..."
 67: if ! verify_checksum "cni-plugins.tgz" "$CNI_PLUGINS_CHECKSUM"; then
 68:     echo "ERROR: CNI plugins checksum verification failed!"
 69:     rm -f cni-plugins.tgz
 70:     exit 1
 71: fi
 72: tar -xzf cni-plugins.tgz -C "${CNI_DIR}/bin/"
 73: # Fetch virtiofsd with fallback URLs (no checksum - sources unreliable)
 74: VIRTIOFSD_VERSION="1.11.1"
 75: VIRTIOFSD_DIR="${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma"
 76: echo "Fetching virtiofsd..."
 77: # Build UI (optional - requires Node.js)
 78: echo "Building UI..."
 79: if command -v npm &> /dev/null; then
 80:     cd ui
 81:     npm install
 82:     npm run build
 83:     cd ..
 84:     mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma"
 85:     cp -r ui/dist "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/ui"
 86:     echo "UI bundled successfully"
 87: else
 88:     echo "Skipping UI build (npm not found)"
 89: fi
 90: if [ ! -f "virtiofsd" ]; then
 91:     # Primary: GitLab releases
 92:     PRIMARY_URL="https://gitlab.com/virtio-fs/virtiofsd/-/releases/${VIRTIOFSD_VERSION}/downloads/virtiofsd-v${VIRTIOFSD_VERSION}-x86_64-musl.zip"
 93:     if wget -q -O virtiofsd.zip "$PRIMARY_URL" 2>/dev/null; then
 94:         unzip -q -o virtiofsd.zip
 95:         mv virtiofsd virtiofsd_bin 2>/dev/null || true
 96:         chmod +x virtiofsd_bin 2>/dev/null || true
 97:     fi
 98: fi
 99: # Fallback 1: Try QEMU GitHub raw
100: if [ ! -f "virtiofsd_bin" ]; then
101:     FALLBACK_URL1="https://github.com/qemu/qemu/raw/main/contrib/virtiofsd/virtiofsd-x86_64"
102:     if wget -q -O virtiofsd "$FALLBACK_URL1" 2>/dev/null; then
103:         mv virtiofsd virtiofsd_bin
104:         chmod +x virtiofsd_bin
105:     fi
106: fi
107: # Fallback 2: Try another mirror
108: if [ ! -f "virtiofsd_bin" ]; then
109:     FALLBACK_URL2="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qemu/qemu/master/contrib/virtiofsd/virtiofsd-x86_64"
110:     if wget -q -O virtiofsd "$FALLBACK_URL2" 2>/dev/null; then
111:         mv virtiofsd virtiofsd_bin
112:         chmod +x virtiofsd_bin
113:     fi
114: fi
115: # Check if we got virtiofsd
116: if [ -f "virtiofsd_bin" ]; then
117:     echo "virtiofsd fetched successfully"
118:     cp virtiofsd_bin "${VIRTIOFSD_DIR}/virtiofsd"
119:     chmod +x "${VIRTIOFSD_DIR}/virtiofsd"
120: else
121:     echo "Warning: virtiofsd not available - volume mounts may not work"
122: fi
123: # 4. Copy Assets
124: cp target/release/vyomad "${WORK_DIR}/usr/bin/"
125: cp target/release/vyoma "${WORK_DIR}/usr/bin/"
126: cp cloud-hypervisor "${WORK_DIR}/usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor"
127: cp packaging/systemd/vyomad.service "${WORK_DIR}/etc/systemd/system/"
128: cp packaging/systemd/vyoma-net.service "${WORK_DIR}/etc/systemd/system/"
129: # Copy kernel and cloud-hypervisor to data directory
130: mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}/var/lib/vyoma/bin"
131: if [ -f "bin/vmlinux" ]; then
132:     cp bin/vmlinux "${WORK_DIR}/var/lib/vyoma/bin/vmlinux"
133:     echo "Kernel binary bundled"
134: else
135:     echo "Warning: bin/vmlinux not found - VMs will not start"
136: fi
137: # Bundle virtiofsd (from system or project bin)
138: if [ -f "bin/virtiofsd" ] && [ ! -L "bin/virtiofsd" ]; then
139:     cp bin/virtiofsd "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
140:     chmod +x "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
141:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from project bin"
142: elif [ -f "/usr/libexec/virtiofsd" ]; then
143:     cp /usr/libexec/virtiofsd "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
144:     chmod +x "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
145:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from system"
146: elif [ -f "/usr/bin/virtiofsd" ]; then
147:     cp /usr/bin/virtiofsd "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
148:     chmod +x "${WORK_DIR}/usr/lib/vyoma/virtiofsd"
149:     echo "virtiofsd bundled from /usr/bin"
150: else
151:     echo "Warning: virtiofsd not found - volume mounts may not work"
152: fi
153: # 5. Create Control File
154: cat <<EOF > "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN/control"
155: Package: ${PKG_NAME}
156: Version: ${VERSION}
157: Section: admin
158: Priority: optional
159: Architecture: ${ARCH}
160: Depends: libc6, openssl, ca-certificates, iptables, kmod
161: Maintainer: Subeshrock <subesh.rock.3@gmail.com>
162: Description: Vyoma - MicroVM Ecosystem
163:  Docker-like experience for Cloud Hypervisor MicroVMs.
164:  Includes Daemon, CLI, Web UI, and virtiofsd for volume mounts.
165: EOF
166: # 6. Create Post-Install Script
167: # $2 = previous version (empty if fresh install)
168: cat <<'POSTINST' > "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN/postinst"
169: #!/bin/bash
170: set -e
171: # Create vyoma user (for socket ownership) - idempotent
172: if ! id vyoma >/dev/null 2>&1; then
173:     useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin --comment "Vyoma MicroVM Daemon" vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
174: fi
175: # Add installing user to vyoma, kvm and disk groups
176: if [ -n "$SUDO_USER" ]; then
177:     usermod -aG vyoma "$SUDO_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
178:     usermod -aG kvm "$SUDO_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
179:     usermod -aG disk "$SUDO_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
180:     echo "Added $SUDO_USER to vyoma, kvm and disk groups. Log out and back in to use CLI."
181: fi
182: # Add vyoma daemon user to kvm group (for /dev/kvm access)
183: if getent group kvm > /dev/null 2>&1; then
184:     usermod -aG kvm vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
185: fi
186: # Fix /dev/mapper/control permissions (for device mapper/DM)
187: chmod 0660 /dev/mapper/control 2>/dev/null || true
188: chown root:disk /dev/mapper/control 2>/dev/null || true
189: # Fix /dev/kvm permissions
190: chmod 0660 /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null || true
191: chown root:kvm /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null || true
192: # Create runtime directory - owned by root:vyoma, group writable (0775)
193: mkdir -p /run/vyoma
194: chown root:vyoma /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
195: chmod 0775 /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
196: # Setup CNI plugins directory (symlink from system data dir to package location)
197: mkdir -p /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni
198: rm -rf /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni/bin 2>/dev/null || true
199: ln -sf /usr/lib/vyoma/cni/bin /var/lib/vyoma/.vyoma/cni/bin
200: # Setup Vyoma network bridge (for VM networking)
201: if ! ip link show vyoma-br0 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
202:     ip link add vyoma-br0 type bridge
203:     ip addr add 172.16.0.1/24 dev vyoma-br0
204:     ip link set vyoma-br0 up
205: fi
206: # Enable service (idempotent - won't fail if already enabled)
207: systemctl daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
208: systemctl enable vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
209: # Only start on fresh install ($2 = previous version, empty if first install)
210: # On upgrade, use try-restart to restart only if already running
211: if [ -z "$2" ]; then
212:     # Fresh install - start the service
213:     systemctl start vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
214: else
215:     # Upgrade - restart only if already running (won't disrupt if stopped)
216:     systemctl try-restart vyomad.service 2>/dev/null || true
217: fi
218: echo "Vyoma v${VERSION} installed successfully!"
219: echo "Open http://localhost:8080 for the dashboard"
220: echo "Run 'vyoma run nginx:latest' to start your first VM"
221: POSTINST
222: chmod 755 "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN/postinst"
223: # 7. Create Post-Remove Script
224: cat <<'POSTRM' > "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN/postrm"
225: #!/bin/bash
226: set -e
227: systemctl daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
228: if [ "$1" = "purge" ]; then
229:     userdel vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
230:     rm -rf /var/lib/vyoma /run/vyoma 2>/dev/null || true
231:     ip link del vyoma-br0 2>/dev/null || true
232: fi
233: POSTRM
234: chmod 755 "${WORK_DIR}/DEBIAN/postrm"
235: # 8. Build Package
236: mkdir -p dist
237: dpkg-deb --build "${WORK_DIR}" "dist/${PKG_NAME}_${VERSION}_${ARCH}.deb"
238: # 9. Cleanup
239: rm -f cloud-hypervisor cloud-hypervisor.tgz virtiofsd.zip virtiofsd virtiofsd_bin cni-plugins.tgz
240: rm -rf release-v1.7.0-x86_64
241: rm -rf "${WORK_DIR}"
242: echo "Package created at dist/${PKG_NAME}_${VERSION}_${ARCH}.deb"

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/vm_service/mod.rs
================
  1: mod agent;
  2: pub mod boot;
  3: pub mod build;
  4: pub mod config;
  5: pub mod image;
  6: pub(crate) mod network;
  7: pub mod policy;
  8: pub mod state;
  9: pub mod types;
 10: pub mod storage;
 11: pub mod mocks;
 12: #[cfg(test)]
 13: mod stage_tests;
 14: #[cfg(test)]
 15: mod measured_boot_tests;
 16: #[cfg(test)]
 17: mod error_recovery;
 18: use std::sync::Arc;
 19: use std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe;
 20: use anyhow::{Context, Result};
 21: use futures::FutureExt;
 22: use tracing::{error, info, warn};
 23: use crate::state::{AppState, VmInstance, VmStatus, wal::WalEntry};
 24: use crate::vm_service::policy::update_vm_status;
 25: use types::{VmRunRequest, VmRunResponse, PreparedStorage, VmNetworkConfig};
 26: struct VmCreationContext {
 27:     state: Arc<AppState>,
 28:     vm_id: String,
 29:     vm_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
 30:     storage: Option<PreparedStorage>,
 31:     network_config: Option<VmNetworkConfig>,
 32:     vm_created: bool,
 33: }
 34: impl VmCreationContext {
 35:     fn new(state: Arc<AppState>, vm_id: String) -> Self {
 36:         Self {
 37:             state,
 38:             vm_id,
 39:             vm_dir: None,
 40:             storage: None,
 41:             network_config: None,
 42:             vm_created: false,
 43:         }
 44:     }
 45:     async fn cleanup_on_failure(&mut self) {
 46:         let vm_id = &self.vm_id;
 47:         warn!("Cleaning up resources after failure for VM {}", vm_id);
 48:         if let Some(ref network) = self.network_config {
 49:             info!("Cleaning up network for VM {}", vm_id);
 50:             let networks: Vec<String> = if network.network_infos.is_empty() {
 51:                 vec![]
 52:             } else {
 53:                 network.network_infos.iter()
 54:                     .map(|n| n.network_name.clone())
 55:                     .collect()
 56:             };
 57:             let _ = network::cleanup_network(&self.state, vm_id, &networks, &network.netns_path).await;
 58:         }
 59:         if let Some(ref storage) = self.storage {
 60:             info!("Cleaning up storage for VM {}", vm_id);
 61:             let _ = storage::cleanup_storage(storage).await;
 62:         }
 63:         if let Some(ref vm_dir) = self.vm_dir {
 64:             if vm_dir.exists() {
 65:                 info!("Cleaning up VM directory {:?}", vm_dir);
 66:                 let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(vm_dir);
 67:             }
 68:         }
 69:         info!("Cleanup completed for VM {}", vm_id);
 70:     }
 71: }
 72: pub async fn run_vm(state: Arc<AppState>, request: VmRunRequest) -> Result<VmRunResponse> {
 73:     info!("VmService: Starting VM for image {}", request.image);
 74:     let home = dirs::home_dir().context("No home dir")?;
 75:     let vyoma_root = home.join(".vyoma");
 76:     let images_root = vyoma_root.join("images");
 77:     let vms_root = vyoma_root.join("vms");
 78:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&images_root)?;
 79:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&vms_root)?;
 80:     let vm_uuid = uuid::Uuid::new_v4();
 81:     let vm_id = vm_uuid.to_string();
 82:     let cid = (vm_uuid.as_fields().0 % 1000000) + 3;
 83:     let vm_dir = vms_root.join(&vm_id);
 84:     let mut ctx = VmCreationContext::new(Arc::clone(&state), vm_id.clone());
 85:     ctx.vm_dir = Some(vm_dir.clone());
 86:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&vm_dir).context("Failed to create VM dir")?;
 87:     let prepared_image = match image::prepare_image(&request.image).await {
 88:         Ok(img) => img,
 89:         Err(e) => {
 90:             ctx.cleanup_on_failure().await;
 91:             return Err(e);
 92:         }
 93:     };
 94:     let storage = match storage::prepare_storage(
 95:         &state,
 96:         &prepared_image.rootfs_sqfs_path,
 97:         &vm_dir,
 98:         &vm_id,
 99:     ).await {
100:         Ok(s) => {
101:             ctx.storage = Some(s.clone());
102:             s
103:         }
104:         Err(e) => {
105:             ctx.cleanup_on_failure().await;
106:             return Err(e);
107:         }
108:     };
109:     let network_config = match network::setup_network(
110:         &state,
111:         &vm_id,
112:         &request.networks,
113:     ).await {
114:         Ok(n) => {
115:             ctx.network_config = Some(n.clone());
116:             n
117:         }
118:         Err(e) => {
119:             ctx.cleanup_on_failure().await;
120:             return Err(e);
121:         }
122:     };
123:     let agent_config = agent::prepare_agent(
124:         &state,
125:         &storage.dm_device_path,
126:         &vm_dir,
127:         &prepared_image.config,
128:     ).await?;
129:     let kernel_path = image::resolve_kernel_from_manifest(&prepared_image.manifest, &state.data_dir)
130:         .unwrap_or_else(|| image::get_default_kernel_path(&state.data_dir));
131:     let ch_config = config::build_ch_config(
132:         &state,
133:         &vm_id,
134:         &cid,
135:         &vm_dir,
136:         &storage.dm_device_path,
137:         &network_config,
138:         &agent_config,
139:         &kernel_path,
140:     );
141:     let (vmm, proxy_tasks, fs_managers, mut vtpm_manager) = match boot::start_vm(
142:         &state,
143:         &ch_config,
144:         &network_config,
145:         &request,
146:     ).await {
147:         Ok(result) => result,
148:         Err(e) => {
149:             ctx.cleanup_on_failure().await;
150:             return Err(e);
151:         }
152:     };
153:     // Start attestation check asynchronously
154:     let state_clone = Arc::clone(&state);
155:     let vm_id_clone = vm_id.clone();
156:     let vm_dir_clone = vm_dir.clone();
157:     let image_name = prepared_image.manifest.as_ref()
158:         .and_then(|m| m.labels.get("vyoma.image.name"))
159:         .unwrap_or(&vm_id)
160:         .clone();
161:     let tpm_socket_path = vtpm_manager.as_ref().map(|vtpm| vtpm.socket_path().to_string());
162:     // Check if attestation is required before spawning
163:     let needs_attestation = {
164:         let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
165:         policy.must_verify_on_boot()
166:     };
167:     let attestation_handle = if needs_attestation {
168:         info!("Measured boot attestation required for VM {}, TPM socket: {:?}", vm_id, tpm_socket_path);
169:         let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
170:             let vm_id_for_log = vm_id_clone.clone();
171:             let inner = AssertUnwindSafe(async {
172:                 policy::check_policy_and_perform_attestation(
173:                     state_clone,
174:                     vm_id_clone,
175:                     vm_dir_clone,
176:                     image_name,
177:                     tpm_socket_path,
178:                 ).await
179:             });
180:             let result = inner.catch_unwind().await;
181:             match result {
182:                 Ok(Ok(())) => info!("Attestation completed successfully for VM {}", vm_id_for_log),
183:                 Ok(Err(e)) => error!("Attestation failed for VM {}: {}", vm_id_for_log, e),
184:                 Err(panic_info) => error!("Attestation task panicked for VM {}: {:?}", vm_id_for_log, panic_info),
185:             }
186:         });
187:         Some(handle)
188:     } else {
189:         // No attestation required, mark as running immediately
190:         // But still stop the vTPM if one was started
191:         if let Some(vtpm) = &mut vtpm_manager {
192:             let _ = vtpm.stop();
193:         }
194:         update_vm_status(&state, &vm_id, VmStatus::Running).await
195:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to update VM status: {}", e))?;
196:         None
197:     };
198:     let instance = VmInstance {
199:         vmm,
200:         id: vm_id.clone(),
201:         status: VmStatus::PendingAttestation,
202:         attestation_status: None,
203:         ch_socket_path: ch_config.socket_path.clone(),
204:         tap_name: network_config.primary_tap.clone(),
205:         dm_name: storage.dm_name.clone(),
206:         loop_devices: storage.loop_devices.clone(),
207:         cow_file_path: storage.cow_file_path.clone(),
208:         ip_address: network_config.ip_address.clone(),
209:         proxy_tasks,
210:         fs_managers,
211:         vtpm_manager,
212:         cgroup_path: setup_cgroups(&state, &vm_id, request.vcpu, request.mem_size_mib)?,
213:         netns_path: network_config.netns_path.clone(),
214:         config_ports: request.ports.clone(),
215:         config_volumes: request.volumes.clone(),
216:         hostname: request.hostname.clone(),
217:         labels: request.labels.clone(),
218:         base_image_path: prepared_image.rootfs_sqfs_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
219:         vcpu: request.vcpu,
220:         mem_size_mib: request.mem_size_mib,
221:         networks: request.networks.clone(),
222:         attestation_task: attestation_handle,
223:     };
224:     instance.save_state().context("Failed to save state")?;
225:     let status_string = match &instance.status {
226:         VmStatus::PendingAttestation => "PendingAttestation".to_string(),
227:         VmStatus::Running => "Running".to_string(),
228:         VmStatus::AttestationFailed { reason } => format!("AttestationFailed: {}", reason),
229:         VmStatus::Error { reason } => format!("Error: {}", reason),
230:     };
231:     {
232:         let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
233:         vms.insert(vm_id.clone(), Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(instance)));
234:     }
235:     let _ = state.events_tx.send(serde_json::json!({
236:         "type": "vm_start",
237:         "id": vm_id.clone(),
238:         "name": request.labels.get("vyoma.service").unwrap_or(&vm_id)
239:     }).to_string());
240:     Ok(VmRunResponse {
241:         vm_id,
242:         status: status_string,
243:         ip_address: network_config.ip_address,
244:     })
245: }
246: pub fn setup_cgroups(
247:     state: &AppState,
248:     vm_id: &str,
249:     vcpu: u32,
250:     mem_size_mib: u32,
251: ) -> Result<Option<String>> {
252:     match state.cgroups.create_vm_cgroup(vm_id) {
253:         Ok(path) => {
254:             let quota = vcpu * 100;
255:             if let Err(e) = state.cgroups.set_cpu_limit(vm_id, quota) {
256:                 error!("Failed to set cpu limit: {}", e);
257:             }
258:             let mem_bytes = (mem_size_mib as u64) * 1024 * 1024;
259:             if let Err(e) = state.cgroups.set_memory_limit(vm_id, mem_bytes) {
260:                 error!("Failed to set memory limit: {}", e);
261:             }
262:             Ok(Some(path))
263:         }
264:         Err(e) => {
265:             error!("Failed to create cgroup: {}", e);
266:             Ok(None)
267:         }
268:     }
269: }

================
File: crates/vyomad/src/state.rs
================
  1: use std::collections::HashMap;
  2: use std::sync::Arc;
  3: use tokio::sync::{broadcast, Mutex as TokioMutex, RwLock};
  4: use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
  5: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
  6: use vyoma_core::api::{PortMapping, VolumeMount};
  7: use vyoma_core::cgroups::CgroupManager;
  8: use vyoma_core::fs::VirtioFsManager;
  9: use vyoma_core::vmm::VmmManager;
 10: use vyoma_core::vtpm::VtpmManager;
 11: use vyoma_core::policy::PolicyManager;
 12: use crate::swarm::{SwarmRaft, NetworkIntegration};

 15: #[derive(Clone)]
 16: pub struct AppState {
 17:     pub vms: Arc<TokioMutex<HashMap<String, Arc<TokioMutex<VmInstance>>>>>,

 29: impl AppState {
 30:     pub fn new_test() -> Self {
 31:         let (events_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);
 32:         Self {
 33:             vms: Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(HashMap::new())),
 34:             cgroups: Arc::new(CgroupManager::new()),
 35:             cni_manager: Arc::new(vyoma_core::cni::CniManager::new(
 36:                 std::path::PathBuf::from("/tmp/test-cni-plugins"),
 37:                 std::path::PathBuf::from("/tmp/test-cni-config"),
 38:             )),
 39:             events_tx,
 40:             wal: Arc::new(wal::Wal::new_test()),
 41:             data_dir: "/tmp/test".to_string(),
 42:             swarm_raft: Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(SwarmRaft::new(1))),
 43:             network_integration: Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(None)),
 44:             timemachine: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(crate::timemachine::TimeMachine::new_test())),
 45:             policy_manager: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(PolicyManager::new())),
 46:             api_token: None,

 49:     pub fn with_vm_service(self: Arc<Self>) -> AppState {

 58:             network_integration: self.network_integration.clone(),
 59:             timemachine: self.timemachine.clone(),

 61:             api_token: None,

 65: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
 66: pub enum VmStatus {
 67:     PendingAttestation,
 68:     Running,
 69:     AttestationFailed { reason: String },
 70:     Error { reason: String },
 71: }
 72: impl Default for VmStatus {
 73:     fn default() -> Self {
 74:         VmStatus::PendingAttestation
 75:     }
 76: }
 77: impl std::fmt::Display for VmStatus {
 78:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
 79:         match self {
 80:             VmStatus::PendingAttestation => write!(f, "PendingAttestation"),
 81:             VmStatus::Running => write!(f, "Running"),
 82:             VmStatus::AttestationFailed { reason } => write!(f, "AttestationFailed: {}", reason),
 83:             VmStatus::Error { reason } => write!(f, "Error: {}", reason),
 84:         }
 85:     }
 86: }
 87: pub struct VmInstance {
 88:     pub vmm: VmmManager,
 89:     pub id: String,
 90:     pub status: VmStatus,
 91:     pub attestation_status: Option<String>,
 92:     pub ch_socket_path: String,
 93:     pub tap_name: String,
 94:     pub dm_name: String,
 95:     pub loop_devices: Vec<String>,
 96:     pub cow_file_path: String,
 97:     pub ip_address: String,
 98:     pub proxy_tasks: Vec<JoinHandle<()>>,
 99:     pub fs_managers: Vec<VirtioFsManager>,
100:     pub vtpm_manager: Option<VtpmManager>,
101:     pub cgroup_path: Option<String>,
102:     pub netns_path: Option<String>,
103:     pub config_ports: Vec<PortMapping>,
104:     pub config_volumes: Vec<VolumeMount>,
105:     pub hostname: Option<String>,
106:     pub labels: HashMap<String, String>,
107:     pub networks: Vec<String>,
108:     pub base_image_path: String,
109:     pub vcpu: u32,
110:     pub mem_size_mib: u32,
111:     pub attestation_task: Option<tokio::task::JoinHandle<()>>,
112: }
113: #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
114: pub struct VmState {
115:     pub id: String,
116:     pub tap_name: String,
117:     pub dm_name: String,
118:     pub loop_devices: Vec<String>,
119:     pub cow_file_path: String,
120:     pub ip_address: String,
121:     pub cgroup_path: Option<String>,
122:     pub netns_path: Option<String>,
123:     pub ports: Vec<PortMapping>,
124:     pub volumes: Vec<VolumeMount>,
125:     pub hostname: Option<String>,
126:     #[serde(default)]
127:     pub labels: HashMap<String, String>,
128:     #[serde(default)]
129:     pub base_image_path: String,
130:     #[serde(default)]
131:     pub vcpu: u32,
132:     #[serde(default)]
133:     pub mem_size_mib: u32,
134:     #[serde(default)]
135:     pub networks: Vec<String>,
136:     #[serde(default)]
137:     pub status: VmStatus,
138: }
139: use tracing::{info, error};
140: use std::process::Command;
141: use vyoma_core::network::NetworkManager;
142: use vyoma_core::storage::StorageManager;
143: impl VmInstance {
144:     pub fn save_state(&self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
145:         let state = VmState {
146:             id: self.id.clone(),
147:             tap_name: self.tap_name.clone(),
148:             dm_name: self.dm_name.clone(),
149:             loop_devices: self.loop_devices.clone(),
150:             cow_file_path: self.cow_file_path.clone(),
151:             ip_address: self.ip_address.clone(),
152:             cgroup_path: self.cgroup_path.clone(),
153:             netns_path: self.netns_path.clone(),
154:             ports: self.config_ports.clone(),
155:             volumes: self.config_volumes.clone(),
156:             hostname: self.hostname.clone(),
157:             labels: self.labels.clone(),
158:             base_image_path: self.base_image_path.clone(),
159:             vcpu: self.vcpu,
160:             mem_size_mib: self.mem_size_mib,
161:             networks: self.networks.clone(),
162:             status: self.status.clone(),
163:         };
164:         let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No home dir"))?;
165:         let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(&self.id);
166:         if !vm_dir.exists() {
167:             std::fs::create_dir_all(&vm_dir)?;
168:         }
169:         let state_path = vm_dir.join("state.json");
170:         let f = std::fs::File::create(state_path)?;
171:         serde_json::to_writer_pretty(f, &state)?;
172:         Ok(())
173:     }
174:     pub async fn cleanup(&mut self, cni_manager: &vyoma_core::cni::CniManager) {
175:         info!("Cleaning up VM resources for {}", self.id);
176:         // 1. Kill VMM
177:         if let Err(e) = self.vmm.kill() {
178:             error!("Failed to kill VMM: {}", e);
179:         }
180:         // 2. Remove Network Interface / CNI (handle multiple networks)
181:         if let Some(netns) = &self.netns_path {
182:             // Use del_multiple for cleanup with all attached networks
183:             if !self.networks.is_empty() {
184:                 if let Err(e) = cni_manager.del_multiple(&self.networks, &self.id, netns) {
185:                     error!("CNI DEL failed for multiple networks: {}", e);
186:                 }
187:             } else {
188:                 // Fallback to single interface cleanup
189:                 if let Err(e) = cni_manager.del(None, &self.id, netns, "eth0") {
190:                     error!("CNI DEL failed: {}", e);
191:                 }
192:             }
193:             let netns_name = format!("vm-{}", self.id);
194:             let _ = Command::new("ip")
195:                 .args(&["netns", "delete", &netns_name])
196:                 .output();
197:         }
198:         if !self.tap_name.is_empty() {
199:             if let Err(e) = NetworkManager::remove_interface(&self.tap_name) {
200:                 error!("Failed to remove TAP {}: {}", self.tap_name, e);
201:             }
202:         }
203:         // 3. Remove DM Device
204:         if let Err(e) = (|| -> Result<(), vyoma_storage::StorageError> {
205:             let dm_manager = vyoma_storage::DmManager::new()?;
206:             dm_manager.remove_snapshot(&self.dm_name)
207:         })() {
208:             error!("Failed to remove DM {}: {}", self.dm_name, e);
209:         }
210:         // 4. Detach Loop Devices
211:         for dev in &self.loop_devices {
212:             if let Err(e) = StorageManager::detach_loop_device(dev) {
213:                 error!("Failed to detach loop {}: {}", dev, e);
214:             }
215:         }
216:         // 5. Remove COW file
217:         if std::path::Path::new(&self.cow_file_path).exists() {
218:             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&self.cow_file_path);
219:         }
220:         // 6. Abort Proxy Tasks
221:         for task in &self.proxy_tasks {
222:             task.abort();
223:         }
224:         // 7. Remove Cgroup
225:         if let Some(_path) = &self.cgroup_path {
226:             let cm = CgroupManager::new();
227:             if let Err(e) = cm.remove_vm_cgroup(&self.id) {
228:                 error!("Failed to remove cgroup for {}: {}", self.id, e);
229:             }
230:         }
231:         // 8. Kill VirtioFs Managers (ADR-025)
232:         for fs_mgr in &mut self.fs_managers {
233:             if let Err(e) = fs_mgr.kill() {
234:                 error!("Failed to kill virtiofsd: {}", e);
235:             }
236:         }
237:         // 8b. Abort attestation task before stopping vTPM to prevent use-after-free
238:         if let Some(ref handle) = self.attestation_task {
239:             handle.abort();
240:             info!("Aborted attestation task for VM {}", self.id);
241:         }
242:         // 9. Stop vTPM if present
243:         if let Some(ref mut vtpm) = self.vtpm_manager {
244:             if let Err(e) = vtpm.stop() {
245:                 error!("Failed to stop vTPM: {}", e);
246:             }
247:         }
248:         // 9. Remove VM Directory
249:         if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {
250:             let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(&self.id);
251:             if vm_dir.exists() {
252:                 if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&vm_dir) {
253:                     error!("Failed to remove VM directory {}: {}", vm_dir.display(), e);
254:                 } else {
255:                     info!("Removed VM directory: {}", vm_dir.display());
256:                 }
257:             }
258:         }
259:     }
260: }

  48: static MIGRATION_SESSIONS: OnceLock<StdMutex<HashMap<String, MigrationSession>>> = OnceLock::new();
  49: fn get_migration_sessions() -> &'static StdMutex<HashMap<String, MigrationSession>> {
  50:     MIGRATION_SESSIONS.get_or_init(|| StdMutex::new(HashMap::new()))
  51: }
  52: #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
  53: pub struct MigrationSession {
  54:     pub session_id: String,
  55:     pub vm_id: String,
  56:     pub source_node: String,
  57:     pub target_node: String,

  63: pub async fn shutdown_signal(state: AppState) {
  64:     let ctrl_c = async {
  65:         let _ = tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await;
  66:     };
  67:     #[cfg(unix)]
  68:     let terminate = async {
  69:         let _ = tokio::signal::unix::signal(tokio::signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())
  70:             .expect("failed to install signal handler")
  71:             .recv()
  72:             .await;
  73:     };
  74:     #[cfg(not(unix))]
  75:     let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>();
  76:     tokio::select! {
  77:         _ = ctrl_c => {},
  78:         _ = terminate => {},
  79:     }
  80:     info!("Signal received, starting graceful shutdown...");
  81:     let ids: Vec<String> = {
  82:         let map = state.vms.lock().await;
  83:         map.keys().cloned().collect()
  84:     };
  85:     if !ids.is_empty() {
  86:         info!("Cleaning up {} active VMs...", ids.len());
  87:         for id in ids {
  88:             let vm_arc = {
  89:                 let mut map = state.vms.lock().await;
  90:                 map.remove(&id)
  91:             };
  92:             if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
  93:                 let mut vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
  94:                 vm.cleanup(&state.cni_manager).await;
  95:                 // WAL: Log VM stop
  96:         if let Err(e) = state.wal.append(&WalEntry::vm_stop(id.clone())) {
  97:             error!("Failed to write WAL entry: {}", e);
  98:         }
  99:             }
 100:         }
 101:     }
 102:     info!("Graceful shutdown complete.");
 103: }
 104: pub async fn health_check() -> &'static str {
 105:     "OK"
 106: }

 136:     status: String,

 139: use vyoma_core::layers::LayerManager;

 143: pub async fn run_vm(
 144:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 145:     Json(payload): Json<RunRequest>,
 146: ) -> Result<Json<RunResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 147:     info!("Received request to run image: {}", payload.image);
 148:     let request = crate::vm_service::types::VmRunRequest::from(payload);
 149:     let result = crate::vm_service::run_vm(Arc::new(state), request).await;
 150:     result.map(|r| Json(crate::api::handlers::RunResponse {
 151:         vm_id: r.vm_id,
 152:         status: r.status,
 153:         ip_address: r.ip_address,
 154:     })).map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))
 155: }
 156: pub async fn stop_vm(
 157:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 158:     Path(id): Path<String>,
 159: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
 160:     vm_service_state::stop_vm(&state, &id)
 161:         .await
 162:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))
 163: }
