 204: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 205: pub struct RestoreRequest {
 206:     pub snapshot_path: String,
 207:     pub mem_path: String,
 208:     pub cow_path: String,
 209:     pub original_vm_id: String,
 210: }
 211: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 212: pub struct TimeTravelRequest {
 213:     pub vm_id: String,
 214:     pub snapshot_id: String,
 215: }
 216: pub async fn restore_vm(
 217:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 218:     Json(payload): Json<RestoreRequest>,
 219: ) -> Result<Json<RunResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 220:     info!(
 221:         "Request to restore VM from snapshot: {}",
 222:         payload.snapshot_path
 223:     );
 224:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
 225:     let vyoma_root = home.join(".vyoma");
 226:     let images_root = vyoma_root.join("images");
 227:     let vms_root = vyoma_root.join("vms");
 228:     // We still assume alpine:latest base for MVP clone
 229:     let safe_image_name = "alpine_latest";
 230:     let image_store_path = images_root.join(&safe_image_name);
 231:     let base_image_file = image_store_path.join("base.ext4");
 232:     // 2. New VM ID & Dir (Clone from Snapshot)
 233:     let vm_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
 234:     let vm_dir = vms_root.join(&vm_id);
 235:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&vm_dir)
 236:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 237:     // 3. New COW File
 238:     let cow_file = vm_dir.join("diff.cow");
 239:     info!("Restoring disk state from {:?}", payload.cow_path);
 240:     std::fs::copy(&payload.cow_path, &cow_file).map_err(|e| {
 241:         (
 242:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 243:             format!("Failed to copy COW file: {}", e),
 244:         )
 245:     })?;
 246:     // We don't create empty cow file anymore
 247:     // StorageManager::create_cow_file(&cow_file, size_mb)...
 248:     // 4. Setup Storage Stack
 249:     let base_loop = StorageManager::setup_loop_device(&base_image_file).map_err(|e| {
 250:         (
 251:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 252:             format!("Loop base: {}", e),
 253:         )
 254:     })?;
 255:     let cow_loop = StorageManager::setup_loop_device(&cow_file).map_err(|e| {
 256:         (
 257:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 258:             format!("Loop cow: {}", e),
 259:         )
 260:     })?;
 261:         use vyoma_storage::DmManager;
 262:         let dm_name = format!("vyoma-{}", vm_id);
 263:         let dm_manager = DmManager::new().map_err(|e| {
 264:             (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("DM init: {}", e))
 265:         })?;
 266:         let base_path = std::path::Path::new(&base_loop);
 267:         let cow_path = std::path::Path::new(&cow_loop);
 268:         let dm_device = dm_manager.create_snapshot(&dm_name, base_path, cow_path)
 269:             .map_err(|e| {
 270:                 (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("DM create: {}", e))
 271:             })?;
 272:         let dm_path = dm_device.path().to_string_lossy().to_string();
 273:     // 5. Setup Network
 274:     // Ensure bridge exists (idempotent-ish)
 275:     let bridge_name = "ign0";
 276:     let bridge_cidr = "172.16.0.1/24";
 277:     // We skip bridge setup here assuming it's up from previous run, or we should just call it safe.
 278:     // NetworkManager::setup_bridge(bridge_name, bridge_cidr)...
 279:     let tap_name = format!("tap{}", &vm_id[0..8]);
 280:     NetworkManager::setup_tap(&tap_name, bridge_name).map_err(|e| {
 281:         (
 282:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 283:             format!("TAP setup: {}", e),
 284:         )
 285:     })?;
 286:     let random_octet = rand::random::<u8>();
 287:     let safe_octet = std::cmp::max(2, std::cmp::min(254, random_octet));
 288:     let vm_ip = format!("172.16.0.{}", safe_octet);
 289:     // 6. Cloud Hypervisor VMM
 290:     let socket_path = vm_dir.join("ch.sock").to_string_lossy().to_string();
 291:     let mut vmm = VmmManager::new(&socket_path);
 292:     if let Err(e) = vmm.start_daemon(&format!("{}/bin/cloud-hypervisor", state.data_dir), None, false) {
 293:         return Err((
 294:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 295:             format!("Failed to start Cloud Hypervisor: {}", e),
 296:         ));
 297:     }
 298:     // 7. Load Snapshot INSTEAD of Boot Source
 299:     if let Err(e) = vmm
 300:         .load_snapshot(&payload.snapshot_path, &payload.mem_path)
 301:         .await
 302:     {
 303:         let _ = vmm.kill();
 304:         return Err((
 305:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 306:             format!("Load snapshot: {}", e),
 307:         ));
 308:     }
 309:     // 8. Add Drives (Must match configuration of snapped VM usually, but we are attaching NEW cow)
 310:     // Firecracker snapshot restoration often involves re-attaching block devices.
 311:     // The device ID "rootfs" must match.
 312:     if let Err(e) = vmm.add_drive("rootfs", &dm_path, true).await {
 313:         let _ = vmm.kill();
 314:         return Err((
 315:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 316:             format!("Add drive: {}", e),
 317:         ));
 318:     }
 319:     // 9. Add Network
 320:     if let Err(e) = vmm.add_network_interface("eth0", &tap_name, None).await {
 321:         let _ = vmm.kill();
 322:         return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Add net: {}", e)));
 323:     }
 324:     // 10. Resume
 325:     if let Err(e) = vmm.resume_instance().await {
 326:         let _ = vmm.kill();
 327:         return Err((
 328:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 329:             format!("Resume instance: {}", e),
 330:         ));
 331:     }
 332:     // Success - Store State
 333:     let proxy_tasks = Vec::new(); // No ports restored for now
 334:     let instance = VmInstance {
 335:         vmm,
 336:         id: vm_id.clone(),
 337:         status: VmStatus::Running, // Restored VMs are considered running
 338:         ch_socket_path: socket_path.clone(),
 339:         tap_name: tap_name.clone(),
 340:         dm_name: dm_name.clone(),
 341:         loop_devices: vec![base_loop, cow_loop],
 342:         cow_file_path: cow_file.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 343:         ip_address: vm_ip.clone(),
 344:         proxy_tasks,
 345:         fs_managers: Vec::new(),
 346:         cgroup_path: {
 347:             let default_vcpu = 2u32;
 348:             let default_mem_mib = 1024u32;
 349:             match crate::vm_service::setup_cgroups(&state, &vm_id, default_vcpu, default_mem_mib) {
 350:                 Ok(path) => path,
 351:                 Err(e) => {
 352:                     warn!("Failed to setup cgroups for restored VM: {}", e);
 353:                     None
 354:                 }
 355:             }
 356:         },
 357:         netns_path: None, // Simplified restore lacks CNI for now
 358:         config_ports: vec![],
 359:         config_volumes: vec![],
 360:         hostname: None,
 361:         labels: HashMap::new(),
 362:         base_image_path: String::new(),
 363:         vcpu: 1,
 364:         mem_size_mib: 512,
 365:         networks: vec![],
 366:         vtpm_manager: None,
 367:         attestation_status: None,
 368:         attestation_task: None,
 369:     };
 370:     {
 371:         let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
 372:         vms.insert(vm_id.clone(), Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(instance)));
 373:     // WAL: Log VM creation and start
 374:     if let Err(e) = state.wal.append(&WalEntry::vm_create(vm_id.clone())) {
 375:         error!("Failed to write WAL entry: {}", e);
 376:     }
 377:     if let Err(e) = state.wal.append(&WalEntry::vm_start(vm_id.clone())) {
 378:         error!("Failed to write WAL entry: {}", e);
 379:     }
 380:     }
 381:     Ok(Json(RunResponse {
 382:         vm_id,
 383:         status: "Restored".to_string(),
 384:         ip_address: vm_ip,
 385:     }))
 386: }
 387: pub async fn time_travel_vm(
 388:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 389:     Json(payload): Json<TimeTravelRequest>,
 390: ) -> Result<Json<RunResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 391:     info!(
 392:         "Request to time-travel VM {} to snapshot: {}",
 393:         payload.vm_id, payload.snapshot_id
 394:     );
 395:     let tm = state.timemachine.read().await;
 396:     let _snapshot = tm.get_snapshot(&payload.vm_id, &payload.snapshot_id)
 397:         .ok_or((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Snapshot not found".to_string()))?;
 398:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
 399:     let vms_root = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms");
 400:     let source_vm_dir = vms_root.join(&payload.vm_id);
 401:     let snaps_dir = source_vm_dir.join("snapshots").join(&payload.snapshot_id);
 402:     let snapshot_path = snaps_dir.join("snapshot.snap");
 403:     let mem_path = snaps_dir.join("memory.mem");
 404:     let cow_path = snaps_dir.join("diff.cow");
 405:     let restore_req = RestoreRequest {
 406:         snapshot_path: snapshot_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 407:         mem_path: mem_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 408:         cow_path: cow_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 409:         original_vm_id: payload.vm_id.clone(),
 410:     };
 411:     // Forward to existing restore logic
 412:     restore_vm(State(state.clone()), Json(restore_req)).await
 413: }
 414: #[derive(Serialize)]
 415: pub struct HistoryResponse {
 416:     pub snapshots: Vec<crate::timemachine::SnapshotEntry>,
 417: }
 418: pub async fn history_vm(
 419:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 420:     Path(id): Path<String>,
 421: ) -> Result<Json<HistoryResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 422:     let tm = state.timemachine.read().await;
 423:     let history = tm.get_snapshot_history(&id).unwrap_or_default();
 424:     Ok(Json(HistoryResponse { snapshots: history }))
 425: }
 426: #[derive(Serialize)]
 427: struct VmSummary {
 428:     id: String,
 429:     status: String,
 430:     ip_address: String,
 431:     hostname: Option<String>,
 432:     labels: HashMap<String, String>,
 433:     attestation_status: Option<String>,
 434:     // For restart
 435:     base_image_path: String,
 436:     vcpu: u32,
 437:     mem_size_mib: u32,
 438: }
 439: #[derive(Serialize)]
 440: pub struct ListResponse {
 441:     vms: Vec<VmSummary>,
 442: }
 443: #[derive(Serialize)]
 444: pub struct PullResponse {
 445:     status: String,
 446:     path: String,
 447: }
 448: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 449: pub struct PullRequest {
 450:     image: String,
 451: }
 452: pub async fn pull_image_handler(
 453:     Json(payload): Json<PullRequest>,
 454: ) -> Result<Json<PullResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 455:     info!("Handling Pull request for {}", payload.image);
 456:     let path = ensure_image_locally_handler(&payload.image).await?;
 457:     Ok(Json(PullResponse {
 458:         status: "Image pulled successfully".to_string(),
 459:         path: path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
 460:     }))
 461: }
 462: pub async fn list_vms(State(state): State<AppState>) -> Json<ListResponse> {
 463:     let instances: Vec<Arc<TokioMutex<VmInstance>>> = {
 464:         let vms_map = state.vms.lock().await;
 465:         vms_map.values().cloned().collect()
 466:     };
 467:     let mut summaries = Vec::new();
 468:     for arc_inst in instances {
 469:         let inst = arc_inst.lock().await;
 470:         summaries.push(VmSummary {
 471:             id: inst.id.clone(),
 472:             status: inst.status.to_string(),
 473:             ip_address: inst.ip_address.clone(),
 474:             hostname: inst.hostname.clone(),
 475:             labels: inst.labels.clone(),
 476:             attestation_status: inst.attestation_status.clone(),
 477:             base_image_path: inst.base_image_path.clone(),
 478:             vcpu: inst.vcpu,
 479:             mem_size_mib: inst.mem_size_mib,
 480:         });
 481:     }
 482:     Json(ListResponse { vms: summaries })
 483: }
 484: pub async fn stream_logs(
 485:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 486:     Path(id): Path<String>,
 487: ) -> Result<Sse<impl Stream<Item = Result<Event, Infallible>>>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 488:     info!("Request to stream logs for VM: {}", id);
 489:     let vm_arc = {
 490:         let vms = state.vms.lock().await;
 491:         vms.get(&id).cloned()
 492:     };
 493:     if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
 494:         let vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
 495:         let rx = vm.vmm.subscribe_logs();
 496:         let stream = tokio_stream::StreamExt::filter_map(
 497:             BroadcastStream::new(rx),
 498:             |try_msg| {
 499:                 match try_msg {
 500:                     Ok(msg) => Some(Ok(Event::default().data(msg))),
 501:                     Err(_) => None,
 502:                 }
 503:             },
 504:         );
 505:         Ok(Sse::new(stream).keep_alive(axum::response::sse::KeepAlive::default()))
 506:     } else {
 507:         Err((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "VM not found".to_string()))
 508:     }
 509: }
 510: pub async fn inspect_vm_handler(
 511:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 512:     Path(id): Path<String>,
 513: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
 514:     // 1. Check running
 515:     let vm_arc = {
 516:         let vms = state.vms.lock().await;
 517:         vms.get(&id).cloned()
 518:     };
 519:     if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
 520:         let vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
 521:         // ... build json ...
 522:         let (status_str, error_reason) = match &vm.status {
 523:             VmStatus::PendingAttestation => ("pending_attestation", None),
 524:             VmStatus::Running => ("running", None),
 525:             VmStatus::AttestationFailed { reason } => ("attestation_failed", Some(reason.clone())),
 526:             VmStatus::Error { reason } => ("error", Some(reason.clone())),
 527:         };
 528:         let val = serde_json::json!({
 529:             "id": vm.id,
 530:             "status": status_str,
 531:             "error_reason": error_reason,
 532:             "tap_name": vm.tap_name,
 533:             "dm_name": vm.dm_name,
 534:             "loop_devices": vm.loop_devices,
 535:             "cow_file_path": vm.cow_file_path,
 536:             "ip_address": vm.ip_address,
 537:             "cgroup_path": vm.cgroup_path,
 538:             "netns_path": vm.netns_path,
 539:             "ports": vm.config_ports,
 540:             "volumes": vm.config_volumes,
 541:             "hostname": vm.hostname,
 542:             "labels": vm.labels,
 543:             "base_image_path": vm.base_image_path,
 544:             "vcpu": vm.vcpu,
 545:             "mem_size_mib": vm.mem_size_mib,
 546:         });
 547:         return Ok(val.to_string());
 548:     }
 549:     // 2. Check disk (stopped)
 550:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
 551:     let state_file = home
 552:         .join(".vyoma")
 553:         .join("vms")
 554:         .join(&id)
 555:         .join("state.json");
 556:     if state_file.exists() {
 557:         let f = std::fs::File::open(state_file)
 558:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 559:         let s: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_reader(f)
 560:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 561:         return Ok(s.to_string());
 562:     }
 563:     Err((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "VM not found".to_string()))
 564: }
 565: pub async fn build_image(
 566:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 567:     Query(params): Query<HashMap<String, String>>,
 568:     body: Body,
 569: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
 570:     info!("Received build request using VM-isolated build system");
 571:     let measured = params.get("measured").map(|v| v == "true").unwrap_or(false);
 572:     if measured {
 573:         info!("Measured build requested via query parameter");
 574:     }
 575:     // 1. Stream body to a temp file (tar.gz)
 576:     let temp_dir =
 577:         tempfile::tempdir().map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 578:     let tar_path = temp_dir.path().join("context.tar.gz");
 579:     {
 580:         // Convert Body stream to AsyncRead
 581:         use futures::StreamExt;
 582:         let stream = body
 583:             .into_data_stream()
 584:             .map(|b| b.map_err(|e| std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)));
 585:         let mut reader = StreamReader::new(stream);
 586:         let mut file = tokio::fs::File::create(&tar_path)
 587:             .await
 588:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 589:         tokio::io::copy(&mut reader, &mut file)
 590:             .await
 591:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 592:     }
 593:     // 2. Unpack context
 594:     let tar_file = std::fs::File::open(&tar_path)
 595:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 596:     let decoder = GzDecoder::new(tar_file);
 597:     let mut archive = Archive::new(decoder);
 598:     let context_dir = temp_dir.path().join("context");
 599:     std::fs::create_dir(&context_dir)
 600:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 601:     archive.unpack(&context_dir).map_err(|e| {
 602:         (
 603:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 604:             format!("Unpack failed: {}", e),
 605:         )
 606:     })?;
 607:     // 3. Parse Vyomafile
 608:     let vyomafile_path = context_dir.join("Vyomafile");
 609:     if !vyomafile_path.exists() {
 610:         return Err((
 611:             StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
 612:             "Vyomafile not found in build context".to_string(),
 613:         ));
 614:     }
 615:     let vyomafile = vyoma_build::Vyomafile::parse(&vyomafile_path)
 616:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, format!("Failed to parse Vyomafile: {}", e)))?;
 617:     // Check if Vyomafile requests measured boot
 618:     let vyomafile_measured = vyomafile.has_measured_boot();
 619:     if vyomafile_measured {
 620:         info!("Measured build requested via VM_MEASURED_BOOT directive in Vyomafile");
 621:     }
 622:     // Measured build is enabled if either query parameter or Vyomafile directive specifies it
 623:     let measured = measured || vyomafile_measured;
 624:     // 4. Generate build ID and prepare work directory
 625:     let build_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
 626:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
 627:     let work_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("builds");
 628:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&work_dir)
 629:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
 630:     // 5. Determine signing key path from policy config
 631:     let signing_key_path = {
 632:         let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
 633:         policy.get_config().measured_boot.build_signing_key_path.clone()
 634:     };
 635:     // 6. Execute build using VM-isolated BuildRunner
 636:     let mut build_runner = vyoma_build::BuildRunner::new(work_dir.clone())
 637:         .with_measured(measured, signing_key_path)
 638:         .with_cgroups(Arc::clone(&state.cgroups));
 639:     let build_result = build_runner
 640:         .build(&vyomafile_path, &context_dir, &build_id)
 641:         .await
 642:         .map_err(|e| {
 643:             (
 644:                 StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
 645:                 format!("Build failed: {:?}", e),
 646:             )
 647:         })?;
 648:     info!(
 649:         "Build completed successfully: {} -> {}",
 650:         build_id,
 651:         build_result.rootfs_path.display()
 652:     );
 653:     // 7. Return the build ID
 654:     Ok(build_id)
 655: }
 656: pub async fn start_process_monitor(state: AppState) {
 657:     tokio::spawn(async move {
 658:         // Check every 5 seconds
 659:         let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5));
 660:         loop {
 661:             interval.tick().await;
 662:             let ids: Vec<String> = {
 663:                 let map = state.vms.lock().await;
 664:                 map.keys().cloned().collect()
 665:             };
 666:             for id in ids {
 667:                 // Get VM Arc
 668:                 let vm_arc = {
 669:                     let map = state.vms.lock().await;
 670:                     map.get(&id).cloned()
 671:                 };
 672:                 if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
 673:                     let mut vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
 674:                     // 1. Check Cloud Hypervisor
 675:                     match vm.vmm.try_wait() {
 676:                         Ok(Some(status)) => {
 677:                             let mut msg = format!(
 678:                                 "Monitor: VM {} Cloud Hypervisor process EXITED (Reaped): {}",
 679:                                 id, status
 680:                             );
 681:                             // Check for OOM
 682:                             if let Ok(count) = state.cgroups.get_oom_kill_count(&id) {
 683:                                 if count > 0 {
 684:                                     msg.push_str(&format!(
 685:                                         " [WARNING: OOM Kill Detected: {}]",
 686:                                         count
 687:                                     ));
 688:                                 }
 689:                             }
 690:                             error!("{}", msg);
 691:                         }
 692:                         Ok(None) => {
 693:                             // Still running
 694:                         }
 695:                         Err(e) => {
 696:                             error!("Monitor: VM {} wait error: {}", id, e);
 697:                         }
 698:                     }
 699:                     // 2. Check VirtioFS
 700:                     let fs_count = vm.fs_managers.len();
 701:                     for idx in 0..fs_count {
 702:                         // Check status with scoped mutable borrow
 703:                         let exit_status = {
 704:                             let fs = &mut vm.fs_managers[idx];
 705:                             match fs.try_wait() {
 706:                                 Ok(Some(s)) => Some(s),
 707:                                 Err(e) => {
 708:                                     error!("Monitor Check Error for FS {}: {}", id, e);
 709:                                     None
 710:                                 }
 711:                                 Ok(None) => None,
 712:                             }
 713:                         };
 714:                         if let Some(status) = exit_status {
 715:                             warn!(
 716:                                 "Monitor: VM {} VirtioFS (vol{}) EXITED with {}. Restarting...",
 717:                                 id, idx, status
 718:                             );
 719:                             // Get Config (Immutable borrow OK now)
 720:                             let host_path = vm.config_volumes.get(idx).map(|v| v.host_path.clone());
 721:                             if let Some(path) = host_path {
 722:                                 let fs = &mut vm.fs_managers[idx]; // Re-borrow mutably
 723:                                 if let Err(e) = fs.start(&path) {
 724:                                     error!(
 725:                                         "Monitor: FAILED to auto-restart VirtioFS for VM {}: {}",
 726:                                         id, e
 727:                                     );
 728:                                 } else {
 729:                                     info!("Monitor: RESTARTED VirtioFS for VM {} (vol{})", id, idx);
 730:                                 }
 731:                             } else {
 732:                                 error!(
 733:                                     "Monitor: Cannot restart VirtioFS (vol{}), config not found.",
 734:                                     idx
 735:                                 );
 736:                             }
 737:                         }
 738:                     }
 739:                 }
 740:             }
 741:         }
 742:     });
 743: }
 744: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 745: pub struct CreateNetworkRequest {
 746:     name: String,
 747:     subnet: String,
 748:     #[serde(default = "default_driver")]
 749:     driver: String,
 750: }
 751: fn default_driver() -> String {
 752:     "bridge".to_string()
 753: }
 754: pub async fn list_networks_handler(
 755:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 756: ) -> Result<Json<Vec<String>>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 757:     state
 758:         .cni_manager
 759:         .list_networks()
 760:         .map(Json)
 761:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))
 762: }
 763: pub async fn create_network_handler(
 764:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 765:     Json(payload): Json<CreateNetworkRequest>,
 766: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
 767:     match payload.driver.as_str() {
 768:         "bridge" => state
 769:             .cni_manager
 770:             .create_network(&payload.name, &payload.subnet)
 771:             .map(|_| format!("Network {} created", payload.name))
 772:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string())),
 773:         "overlay" => state
 774:             .cni_manager
 775:             .create_overlay_network(&payload.name, &payload.subnet)
 776:             .map(|_| format!("Overlay Network {} created", payload.name))
 777:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string())),
 778:         _ => Err((
 779:             StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
 780:             format!("Unknown driver: {}", payload.driver),
 781:         )),
 782:     }
 783: }
 784: pub async fn delete_network_handler(
 785:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 786:     Path(name): Path<String>,
 787: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
 788:     state
 789:         .cni_manager
 790:         .delete_network(&name)
 791:         .map(|_| format!("Network {} deleted", name))
 792:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))
 793: }
 794: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 795: pub struct JoinRequest {
 796:     pub node_id: u64,
 797:     pub addr: String,
 798:     pub public_key: String,
 799:     pub wireguard_key: Option<String>,
 800:     pub wireguard_port: Option<u16>,
 801: }
 802: #[derive(Serialize)]
 803: pub struct InitResponse {
 804:     pub node_id: u64,
 805:     pub subnet_id: u8,
 806:     pub wireguard_port: Option<u16>,
 807:     pub wireguard_key: Option<String>,
 808: }
 809: #[derive(Serialize)]
 810: pub struct JoinResponse {
 811:     pub node_id: u64,
 812:     pub subnet_id: u8,
 813:     pub peers: Vec<swarm::NodeInfo>,
 814: }
 815: fn get_outbound_ip() -> String {
 816:     match std::net::UdpSocket::bind("0.0.0.0:0") {
 817:         Ok(s) => match s.connect("8.8.8.8:80") {
 818:             Ok(_) => s.local_addr().map(|a| a.ip().to_string()).unwrap_or("127.0.0.1".to_string()),
 819:             Err(_) => "127.0.0.1".to_string(),
 820:         },
 821:         Err(_) => "127.0.0.1".to_string(),
 822:     }
 823: }
 824: pub async fn swarm_init_handler(State(state): State<AppState>) -> Result<Json<InitResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 825:     let node_id = 1u64;
 826:     let addr = format!("{}:7946", get_outbound_ip());
 827:     let mut swarm_raft = state.swarm_raft.lock().await;
 828:     swarm_raft.bootstrap(addr, format!("init_key_{}", node_id), None, None)
 829:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("SwarmRaft Error: {}", e)))?;
 830:     let subnet_id = ((node_id % 254) + 1) as u8;
 831:     info!("Swarm initialized - Node ID: {}, Subnet: 10.42.{}.0/24", node_id, subnet_id);
 832:     Ok(Json(InitResponse {
 833:         node_id,
 834:         subnet_id,
 835:         wireguard_port: None,
 836:         wireguard_key: None,
 837:     }))
 838: }
 839: pub async fn swarm_join_handler(
 840:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 841:     Json(payload): Json<JoinRequest>,
 842: ) -> Result<Json<JoinResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 843:     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
 844:     let leader_addr = payload.addr.clone();
 845:     let join_url = format!("http://{}/swarm/join-propose", leader_addr);
 846:     let req = serde_json::json!({
 847:         "node_id": payload.node_id,
 848:         "addr": payload.addr,
 849:         "public_key": payload.public_key,
 850:         "wireguard_key": payload.wireguard_key,
 851:         "wireguard_port": payload.wireguard_port,
 852:     });
 853:     let response = client.post(&join_url)
 854:         .json(&req)
 855:         .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(10))
 856:         .send()
 857:         .await
 858:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to contact leader: {}", e)))?;
 859:     if !response.status().is_success() {
 860:         return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, format!("Join rejected: {}", response.text().await.unwrap_or_default())));
 861:     }
 862:     let subnet_id = ((payload.node_id % 254) + 1) as u8;
 863:     {
 864:         let mut swarm_raft = state.swarm_raft.lock().await;
 865:         if !swarm_raft.is_initialized() {
 866:             let addr = format!("{}:7946", get_outbound_ip());
 867:             swarm_raft.bootstrap(addr, format!("init_key_{}", payload.node_id), None, None)
 868:                 .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))?;
 869:         }
 870:         swarm_raft.add_node(
 871:             payload.node_id,
 872:             payload.addr,
 873:             payload.public_key,
 874:             payload.wireguard_key.clone(),
 875:             payload.wireguard_port,
 876:         ).map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to add node locally: {}", e)))?;
 877:     }
 878:     let peers: Vec<swarm::NodeInfo> = {
 879:         let swarm_raft = state.swarm_raft.lock().await;
 880:         swarm_raft.get_nodes().iter().map(|n| (*n).clone()).collect()
 881:     };
 882:     info!("Node {} joined swarm - Subnet: 10.42.{}.0/24, Peers: {}", payload.node_id, subnet_id, peers.len());
 883:     Ok(Json(JoinResponse {
 884:         node_id: payload.node_id,
 885:         subnet_id,
 886:         peers,
 887:     }))
 888: }
 889: #[derive(Serialize)]
 890: pub struct SwarmNodeInfo {
 891:     pub id: u64,
 892:     pub addr: String,
 893:     pub public_key: String,
 894:     pub wireguard_key: Option<String>,
 895:     pub wireguard_port: Option<u16>,
 896:     pub subnet_id: Option<u8>,
 897:     pub is_leader: bool,
 898: }
 899: pub async fn swarm_nodes_handler(State(state): State<AppState>) -> Json<Vec<SwarmNodeInfo>> {
 900:     let swarm_raft = state.swarm_raft.lock().await;
 901:     let nodes: Vec<SwarmNodeInfo> = swarm_raft.get_nodes().iter().map(|n| {
 902:         SwarmNodeInfo {
 903:             id: n.id,
 904:             addr: n.addr.clone(),
 905:             public_key: n.public_key.clone(),
 906:             wireguard_key: n.wireguard_key.clone(),
 907:             wireguard_port: n.wireguard_port,
 908:             subnet_id: n.subnet_id,
 909:             is_leader: n.is_leader,
 910:         }
 911:     }).collect();
 912:     Json(nodes)
 913: }
 914: #[derive(Serialize)]
 915: pub struct RegisterResponse {
 916:     pub node_id: u64,
 917:     pub subnet_id: u8,
 918:     pub peers: Vec<SwarmNodeInfo>,
 919: }
 920: pub async fn events_handler(
 921:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 922: ) -> Sse<impl Stream<Item = Result<Event, Infallible>>> {
 923:     let rx = state.events_tx.subscribe();
 924:     let stream = BroadcastStream::new(rx);
 925:     Sse::new(tokio_stream::StreamExt::filter_map(stream, |msg| {
 926:             match msg {
 927:                 Ok(data) => Some(Ok(Event::default().data(data))),
 928:                 Err(_) => Some(Ok(Event::default().comment("missed message"))),
 929:             }
 930:         })
 931:     )
 932:     .keep_alive(axum::response::sse::KeepAlive::default())
 933: }
 934: // --- Extended API Handlers for Web UI ---
 935: pub async fn list_images_handler() -> Json<Vec<String>> {
 936:     let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("/tmp"));
 937:     let images_root = home.join(".vyoma").join("images");
 938:     let mut images = Vec::new();
 939:     if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(images_root) {
 940:         for entry in entries.flatten() {
 941:             if entry.path().is_dir() {
 942:                  if let Ok(name) = entry.file_name().into_string() {
 943:                      images.push(name);
 944:                  }
 945:             }
 946:         }
 947:     }
 948:     Json(images)
 949: }
 950: #[derive(Serialize)]
 951: pub struct VolumeInfo {
 952:     name: String,
 953:     path: String,
 954: }
 955: pub async fn list_volumes_handler() -> Json<Vec<VolumeInfo>> {
 956:     let home = dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("/tmp"));
 957:     let vol_root = home.join(".vyoma").join("volumes");
 958:     let mut vols = Vec::new();
 959:     if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(vol_root) {
 960:         for entry in entries.flatten() {
 961:              if let Ok(name) = entry.file_name().into_string() {
 962:                   vols.push(VolumeInfo { name: name, path: entry.path().to_string_lossy().to_string() });
 963:              }
 964:         }
 965:     }
 966:     Json(vols)
 967: }
 968: #[derive(Deserialize)]
 969: pub struct TeleportRequest {
 970:     pub vm_id: String,
 971:     pub target_node_ip: String,
 972:     pub bandwidth_mbps: Option<u32>,
 973: }
 974: #[derive(Serialize)]
 975: pub struct TeleportResponse {
 976:     pub session_id: String,
 977:     pub status: String,
 978:     pub message: String,
 979: }
 980: #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
 981: pub struct AdoptTeleportRequest {
 982:     pub vm_id: String,
 983: }
 984: pub async fn adopt_teleported_vm(
 985:     State(state): State<AppState>,
 986:     Json(payload): Json<AdoptTeleportRequest>,
 987: ) -> Result<Json<TeleportResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
 988:     info!("Handling POST /adopt-teleported-vm for VM {}", payload.vm_id);
 989:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
 990:     let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(&payload.vm_id);
 991:     if !vm_dir.exists() {
 992:         return Err((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, format!("VM directory not found for {}", payload.vm_id)));
 993:     }
 994:     let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock").to_string_lossy().to_string();
 995:     // Query the VM info from the now-running Cloud Hypervisor
 996:     let client = Client::builder()
 997:         .timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))
 998:         .unix_socket(ch_socket.as_str())
 999:         .build()
1000:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to build socket client: {}", e)))?;
1001:     let response = client
1002:         .request(Method::GET, "http://localhost/api/v1/vm.info")
1003:         .send()
1004:         .await
1005:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to query vm.info: {}", e)))?;
1006:     let vm_info: VmInfo = response
1007:         .json()
1008:         .await
1009:         .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to parse vm.info: {}", e)))?;
1010:     // Ensure VM is running
1011:     if vm_info.state != "Running" {
1012:         // Try to resume it
1013:         client
1014:             .request(Method::PUT, "http://localhost/api/v1/vm.resume")
1015:             .send()
1016:             .await
1017:             .map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to resume VM: {}", e)))?;
1018:     }
1019:     // Register the adopted VM in the daemon state
1020:     let vmm = VmmManager::new(&ch_socket);
1021:     let instance = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(VmInstance {
1022:         vmm,
1023:         id: payload.vm_id.clone(),
1024:         status: VmStatus::Running, // Adopted VMs are considered running
1025:         attestation_status: None,
1026:         ch_socket_path: ch_socket,
1027:         tap_name: String::new(),
1028:         dm_name: String::new(),
1029:         loop_devices: Vec::new(),
1030:         cow_file_path: String::new(),
1031:         ip_address: String::new(),
1032:         proxy_tasks: Vec::new(),
1033:         fs_managers: Vec::new(),
1034:         cgroup_path: None,
1035:         netns_path: None,
1036:         config_ports: Vec::new(),
1037:         config_volumes: Vec::new(),
1038:         hostname: None,
1039:         labels: HashMap::new(),
1040:         networks: Vec::new(),
1041:         base_image_path: String::new(),
1042:         vcpu: 1,
1043:         mem_size_mib: 512,
1044:         vtpm_manager: None,
1045:         attestation_task: None,
1046:     }));
1047:     {
1048:         let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
1049:         vms.insert(payload.vm_id.clone(), instance);
1050:     }
1051:     info!("Adopted VM {} into local state", payload.vm_id);
1052:     Ok(Json(TeleportResponse {
1053:         session_id: payload.vm_id.clone(),
1054:         status: "adopted".to_string(),
1055:         message: format!("VM {} adopted successfully", payload.vm_id),
1056:     }))
1057: }
1058: pub async fn teleport_handler(
1059:     State(state): State<AppState>,
1060:     Json(payload): Json<TeleportRequest>,
1061: ) -> Result<Json<TeleportResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
1062:     info!("Handling POST /teleport for VM {} to {}", payload.vm_id, payload.target_node_ip);
1063:     let session_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
1064:     let started_at = SystemTime::now()
1065:         .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
1066:         .map(|d| d.as_secs())
1067:         .unwrap_or(0);
1068:     let migration_session = MigrationSession {
1069:         session_id: session_id.clone(),
1070:         vm_id: payload.vm_id.clone(),
1071:         source_node: "local".to_string(),
1072:         target_node: payload.target_node_ip.clone(),
1073:         status: "initiating".to_string(),
1074:         progress: None,
1075:         started_at,
1076:         completed_at: None,
1077:     };
1078:     {
1079:         let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1080:         let mut sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1081:         sessions.insert(session_id.clone(), migration_session.clone());
1082:     }
1083:     let vm_arc = {
1084:         let vms = state.vms.lock().await;
1085:         vms.get(&payload.vm_id).cloned()
1086:     };
1087:     if let Some(vm_mutex) = vm_arc {
1088:         let (ch_socket, mem_size_mib_for_spawn) = {
1089:             let vm = vm_mutex.lock().await;
1090:             (vm.ch_socket_path.clone(), vm.mem_size_mib)
1091:         };
1092:         let target_url = payload.target_node_ip.clone();
1093:         let vms_for_cleanup = Arc::clone(&state.vms);
1094:         // Get local WireGuard IP for trusted source restriction
1095:         let local_wg_ip = state.network_integration
1096:             .lock()
1097:             .await
1098:             .as_ref()
1099:             .and_then(|ni| ni.get_local_wireguard_ip());
1100:         let trusted_ips = local_wg_ip.map(|ip| vec![ip.to_string()]);
1101:         let http_client = reqwest::Client::new();
1102:         let target_api = format!("http://{}:3000/receive-teleport", target_url);
1103:         info!("Telling target node at {} to prepare for reception (trusted IPs: {:?})...", target_api, trusted_ips);
1104:         let res = http_client.post(&target_api)
1105:             .json(&ReceiveTeleportRequest {
1106:                 vm_id: payload.vm_id.clone(),
1107:                 trusted_source_ips: trusted_ips,
1108:             })
1109:             .send()
1110:             .await;
1111:         if let Err(e) = res {
1112:             {
1113:                 let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1114:                 let mut sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1115:                 if let Some(s) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id) {
1116:                     s.status = "failed".to_string();
1117:                 }
1118:             }
1119:             return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Target node unreachable: {}", e)));
1120:         } else if let Ok(resp) = res {
1121:             if !resp.status().is_success() {
1122:                 {
1123:                     let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1124:                     let mut sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1125:                     if let Some(s) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id) {
1126:                         s.status = "failed".to_string();
1127:                     }
1128:                 }
1129:                 return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Target node refused: {:?}", resp.text().await)));
1130:             }
1131:         }
1132:         let bandwidth = payload.bandwidth_mbps;
1133:         let session_id_clone = session_id.clone();
1134:         let progress_callback = Box::new(move |progress: TeleportProgress| {
1135:             let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1136:             if let Ok(mut sessions) = sessions.lock() {
1137:                 if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id_clone) {
1138:                     session.status = progress.status.clone();
1139:                     session.progress = Some(progress);
1140:                 }
1141:             }
1142:         });
1143:         let ch_socket_for_spawn = ch_socket.clone();
1144:         let session_id_for_spawn = session_id.clone();
1145:         let target_url_for_spawn = target_url.clone();
1146:         let adopt_url_for_spawn = format!("http://{}:3000/adopt-teleported-vm", target_url_for_spawn);
1147:         let vm_id_for_cleanup = payload.vm_id.clone();
1148:         let vms_for_cleanup = vms_for_cleanup.clone();
1149:         tokio::spawn(async move {
1150:             let teleporter = vyoma_teleport::sender::Teleporter::new(
1151:                 payload.vm_id.clone(),
1152:                 target_url_for_spawn,
1153:                 mem_size_mib_for_spawn as u64,
1154:             );
1155:             let result = teleporter.teleport_vm_with_config(
1156:                 &ch_socket_for_spawn,
1157:                 bandwidth,
1158:                 Some(progress_callback),
1159:             ).await;
1160:             if result.is_ok() {
1161:                  // Stop the source VM after successful migration
1162:                  {
1163:                      let vm_arc_opt = {
1164:                          let vms = vms_for_cleanup.lock().await;
1165:                          vms.get(&vm_id_for_cleanup).cloned()
1166:                      };
1167:                     if let Some(vm_arc) = vm_arc_opt {
1168:                         let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
1169:                         if let Err(e) = vm.vmm.pause_instance().await {
1170:                             error!("Failed to pause source VM {}: {}", vm_id_for_cleanup, e);
1171:                         }
1172:                         info!("Source VM {} paused after successful live migration", vm_id_for_cleanup);
1173:                     }
1174:                 }
1175:                  // Remove from registry
1176:                  if let Some(_vm_arc) = vms_for_cleanup.lock().await.remove(&vm_id_for_cleanup) {
1177:                      info!("Source VM {} removed from registry after migration", vm_id_for_cleanup);
1178:                  }
1179:                 // Notify target node to adopt the VM
1180:                 let adopt_resp = reqwest::Client::new()
1181:                     .post(&adopt_url_for_spawn)
1182:                     .json(&AdoptTeleportRequest { vm_id: vm_id_for_cleanup.clone() })
1183:                     .send()
1184:                     .await;
1185:                 match adopt_resp {
1186:                     Ok(r) if r.status().is_success() => {
1187:                         info!("Target node adopted VM {}", vm_id_for_cleanup);
1188:                     }
1189:                     Ok(r) => {
1190:                         warn!("Target adoption returned non-success: {}", r.status());
1191:                     }
1192:                     Err(e) => {
1193:                         warn!("Failed to notify target to adopt VM {}: {}", vm_id_for_cleanup, e);
1194:                     }
1195:                 }
1196:                 let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1197:                 if let Ok(mut sessions) = sessions.lock() {
1198:                     if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id_for_spawn) {
1199:                         session.status = "completed".to_string();
1200:                         session.completed_at = Some(
1201:                             SystemTime::now()
1202:                                 .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
1203:                                 .map(|d| d.as_secs())
1204:                                 .unwrap_or(0)
1205:                         );
1206:                     }
1207:                 }
1208:             } else {
1209:                  // Resume source VM on migration failure
1210:                  {
1211:                      let vm_arc_opt = {
1212:                          let vms = vms_for_cleanup.lock().await;
1213:                          vms.get(&vm_id_for_cleanup).cloned()
1214:                      };
1215:                     if let Some(vm_arc) = vm_arc_opt {
1216:                         let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
1217:                         if let Err(e) = vm.vmm.resume_instance().await {
1218:                             error!("Failed to resume source VM {}: {}", vm_id_for_cleanup, e);
1219:                         } else {
1220:                             info!("Source VM {} resumed after migration failure", vm_id_for_cleanup);
1221:                         }
1222:                     }
1223:                 }
1224:                 let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1225:                 if let Ok(mut sessions) = sessions.lock() {
1226:                     if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id_for_spawn) {
1227:                         session.status = "failed".to_string();
1228:                     }
1229:                 }
1230:             }
1231:             match result {
1232:                 Ok(_) => info!("Teleportation of VM {} succeeded!", payload.vm_id),
1233:                 Err(e) => error!("Teleportation of VM {} failed: {}", payload.vm_id, e),
1234:             }
1235:         });
1236:         {
1237:             let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1238:             let mut sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1239:             if let Some(s) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id) {
1240:                 s.status = "in_progress".to_string();
1241:             }
1242:         }
1243:         Ok(Json(TeleportResponse {
1244:             session_id,
1245:             status: "in_progress".to_string(),
1246:             message: "Live migration initiated".to_string(),
1247:         }))
1248:     } else {
1249:         {
1250:             let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1251:             let mut sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1252:             if let Some(s) = sessions.get_mut(&session_id) {
1253:                 s.status = "failed".to_string();
1254:             }
1255:         }
1256:         Err((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "VM not found".to_string()))
1257:     }
1258: }
1259: #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
1260: pub struct ReceiveTeleportRequest {
1261:     pub vm_id: String,
1262:     #[serde(default)]
1263:     pub trusted_source_ips: Option<Vec<String>>,
1264: }
1265: pub async fn receive_teleport_handler(
1266:     State(state): State<AppState>,
1267:     Json(payload): Json<ReceiveTeleportRequest>,
1268: ) -> Result<String, (StatusCode, String)> {
1269:     info!("Handling POST /receive-teleport for VM {}", payload.vm_id);
1270:     // Warn if no trusted source IPs provided (insecure mode)
1271:     if payload.trusted_source_ips.is_none() {
1272:         warn!("Receiving teleport for VM {} WITHOUT trusted source IPs; migration port is open to any host!", payload.vm_id);
1273:     } else {
1274:         info!("Receiving teleport with trusted source IPs: {:?}", payload.trusted_source_ips);
1275:     }
1276:     // Create directories
1277:     let home = dirs::home_dir().ok_or((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "No home dir".into()))?;
1278:     let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(&payload.vm_id);
1279:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&vm_dir).map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))?;
1280:     let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock").to_string_lossy().to_string();
1281:     let mut vmm = VmmManager::new(&ch_socket);
1282:     // Start Cloud Hypervisor
1283:     if let Err(e) = vmm.start_daemon(&format!("{}/bin/cloud-hypervisor", state.data_dir), None, false) {
1284:         return Err((
1285:             StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
1286:             format!("Failed to start Cloud Hypervisor: {}", e),
1287:         ));
1288:     }
1289:     // Call Receiver with trusted source IPs
1290:     let receiver = vyoma_teleport::receiver::TeleportReceiver::new(PathBuf::new(), PathBuf::new(), "0.0.0.0".to_string());
1291:     if let Err(e) = receiver.start_receiving_with_config(&ch_socket, payload.trusted_source_ips.clone()).await {
1292:         let _ = vmm.kill();
1293:         return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Failed to set receive-migration mode: {}", e)));
1294:     }
1295:     // We leave it running. Sender will connect to port 9000 and it will resume automatically.
1296:     // The VM state should probably be adopted by the daemon after migration completes.
1297:     // In a complete implementation, we would wait for it and insert into `state.vms`.
1298:     Ok(format!("Cloud Hypervisor ready to receive VM {} on TCP port 9000 (trusted sources: {:?})", payload.vm_id, payload.trusted_source_ips))
1299: }
1300: #[derive(Serialize)]
1301: pub struct TeleportStatusResponse {
1302:     pub session_id: String,
1303:     pub vm_id: String,
1304:     pub source_node: String,
1305:     pub target_node: String,
1306:     pub status: String,
1307:     pub progress: Option<TeleportProgress>,
1308:     pub started_at: u64,
1309:     pub completed_at: Option<u64>,
1310: }
1311: pub async fn teleport_status_handler(
1312:     State(_state): State<AppState>,
1313:     Path(session_id): Path<String>,
1314: ) -> Result<Json<TeleportStatusResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
1315:     let sessions = get_migration_sessions();
1316:     let sessions = sessions.lock().unwrap();
1317:     if let Some(session) = sessions.get(&session_id) {
1318:         Ok(Json(TeleportStatusResponse {
1319:             session_id: session.session_id.clone(),
1320:             vm_id: session.vm_id.clone(),
1321:             source_node: session.source_node.clone(),
1322:             target_node: session.target_node.clone(),
1323:             status: session.status.clone(),
1324:             progress: session.progress.clone(),
1325:             started_at: session.started_at,
1326:             completed_at: session.completed_at,
1327:         }))
1328:     } else {
1329:         Err((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Session not found".to_string()))
1330:     }
1331: }
1332: #[derive(Deserialize)]
1333: pub struct PolicyRequest {
1334:     pub policy: String,
1335:     pub enabled: bool,
1336: }
1337: #[derive(Serialize)]
1338: pub struct PolicyResponse {
1339:     pub policy: String,
1340:     pub enabled: bool,
1341:     pub message: String,
1342: }
1343: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
1344: pub struct AttestResponse {
1345:     pub vm_id: String,
1346:     pub verified: bool,
1347:     pub pcr_results: Vec<PcrResult>,
1348:     pub error: Option<String>,
1349: }
1350: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
1351: pub struct PcrResult {
1352:     pub pcr_index: u32,
1353:     pub expected: String,
1354:     pub actual: String,
1355:     pub verified: bool,
1356: }
1357: pub async fn attest_vm_handler(
1358:     State(state): State<AppState>,
1359:     Path(id): Path<String>,
1360: ) -> Result<Json<AttestResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
1361:     info!("Attestation request for VM {}", id);
1362:     let vm_arc = {
1363:         let vms = state.vms.lock().await;
1364:         vms.get(&id).cloned()
1365:     };
1366:     let vm_arc = match vm_arc {
1367:         Some(arc) => arc,
1368:         None => {
1369:             return Err((
1370:                 StatusCode::NOT_FOUND,
1371:                 format!("VM {} not found", id),
1372:             ));
1373:         }
1374:     };
1375:     let tpm_socket = {
1376:         let vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
1377:         vm.vtpm_manager.as_ref().map(|vtpm| vtpm.socket_path().to_string())
1378:     };
1379:     let tpm_socket = match tpm_socket {
1380:         Some(s) => s,
1381:         None => {
1382:             return Err((
1383:                 StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
1384:                 format!("VM {} has no vTPM - measured boot not enabled", id),
1385:             ));
1386:         }
1387:     };
1388:     // Build attestation config from policy (same as boot-time does)
1389:     let config = crate::vm_service::policy::build_attestation_config(&Arc::new(state.clone()));
1390:     let image_name = resolve_image_name_from_path(&vm_arc.lock().await.base_image_path);
1391:     // Call the shared verification function
1392:     let result = crate::vm_service::policy::verify_attestation_from_manifest(
1393:         &image_name,
1394:         &tpm_socket,
1395:         &config,
1396:     ).await.map_err(|e| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))?;
1397:     // Map policy PcrResult to handler PcrResult
1398:     let pcr_results = result.pcr_results.into_iter().map(|pcr| PcrResult {
1399:         pcr_index: pcr.pcr_index,
1400:         expected: pcr.expected,
1401:         actual: pcr.actual,
1402:         verified: pcr.verified,
1403:     }).collect();
1404:     let verified = result.verified;
1405:     info!(
1406:         "Attestation for VM {}: {}",
1407:         id,
1408:         if verified { "VERIFIED" } else { "FAILED" }
1409:     );
1410:     // Update the VM instance's attestation status
1411:     {
1412:         let mut vm = vm_arc.lock().await;
1413:         vm.attestation_status = Some(if verified {
1414:             "VERIFIED".to_string()
1415:         } else {
1416:             "FAILED".to_string()
1417:         });
1418:     }
1419:     Ok(Json(AttestResponse {
1420:         vm_id: id,
1421:         verified,
1422:         pcr_results,
1423:         error: result.error,
1424:     }))
1425: }
1426: fn resolve_image_name_from_path(base_image_path: &str) -> String {
1427:     use std::path::Path;
1428:     if let Some(path) = Path::new(base_image_path).parent() {
1429:         if let Some(name) = path.file_name() {
1430:             return name.to_string_lossy().to_string();
1431:         }
1432:     }
1433:     base_image_path.to_string()
1434: }
1435: pub async fn set_policy_handler(
1436:     State(state): State<AppState>,
1437:     Json(payload): Json<PolicyRequest>,
1438: ) -> Result<Json<PolicyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
1439:     info!("Setting policy: {} to {}", payload.policy, payload.enabled);
1440:     match payload.policy.as_str() {
1441:         "require-measured-boot" => {
1442:             let mut policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
1443:             policy.set_require_measured_boot(payload.enabled);
1444:             let message = if payload.enabled {
1445:                 "Measured boot policy enabled. VMs will require TPM attestation."
1446:             } else {
1447:                 "Measured boot policy disabled."
1448:             };
1449:             Ok(Json(PolicyResponse {
1450:                 policy: payload.policy,
1451:                 enabled: payload.enabled,
1452:                 message: message.to_string(),
1453:             }))
1454:         }
1455:         _ => Err((
1456:             StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
1457:             format!("Unknown policy: {}", payload.policy),
1458:         )),
1459:     }
1460: }
1461: pub async fn get_policy_handler(
1462:     State(state): State<AppState>,
1463: ) -> Result<Json<Vec<PolicyResponse>>, (StatusCode, String)> {
1464:     let policy = state.policy_manager.lock().unwrap();
1465:     let config = policy.get_config();
1466:     let status = PolicyStatus::from_config(config);
1467:     let responses: Vec<PolicyResponse> = status.into_iter().map(|s| PolicyResponse {
1468:         policy: s.policy_name,
1469:         enabled: s.enabled,
1470:         message: if s.enforced { "enforced" } else { "optional" }.to_string(),
1471:     }).collect();
1472:     Ok(Json(responses))
1473: }

 21: fn check_dependencies(data_dir: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
 22:     let mut missing = Vec::new();
 23:     // Check for iptables
 24:     if std::process::Command::new("which")
 25:         .arg("iptables")
 26:         .output()
 27:         .map(|o| !o.status.success())
 28:         .unwrap_or(true)
 29:     {
 30:         // Try alternative paths
 31:         if !std::path::Path::new("/sbin/iptables").exists()
 32:             && !std::path::Path::new("/usr/sbin/iptables").exists()
 33:         {
 34:             missing.push("iptables (required for NAT networking)");
 35:         }
 36:     }
 37:     // Check for KVM device
 38:     if !std::path::Path::new("/dev/kvm").exists() {
 39:         missing.push("/dev/kvm (KVM kernel module required for virtualization)");
 40:     }
 41:     // Check for bundled cloud-hypervisor
 42:     let ch_path = std::path::Path::new(data_dir).join("bin/cloud-hypervisor");
 43:     if !ch_path.exists() {
 44:         // Check in common fallback locations
 45:         let fallback_paths = vec![
 46:             "/usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor",
 47:             "/usr/local/bin/cloud-hypervisor",
 48:         ];
 49:         let found = fallback_paths.iter().any(|p| std::path::Path::new(p).exists());
 50:         if !found {
 51:             missing.push("cloud-hypervisor (not found in data_dir/bin or /usr/bin)");
 52:         }
 53:     }
 54:     if missing.is_empty() {
 55:         Ok(())
 56:     } else {
 57:         Err(missing.join("\n  - "))
 58:     }
 59: }

 81: #[derive(Parser, Debug)]
 82: #[command(name = "vyomad", about = "Vyoma MicroVM Daemon", version)]
 83: struct Args {
 84:     /// Path to listen on (Unix Socket)
 85:     #[arg(short, long, default_value = "/run/vyoma/vyoma.sock")]
 86:     socket_path: String,
 87:     /// HTTP port for dashboard (default: 8080, use 0 to disable)
 88:     #[arg(short = 'p', long, default_value_t = 8080)]
 89:     http_port: u16,
 90:     /// HTTP bind IP address (defaults to 127.0.0.1 for security)
 91:     /// Use 0.0.0.0 for remote access (requires --api-token for authentication)
 92:     #[arg(long, default_value = "127.0.0.1")]
 93:     http_bind_ip: String,
 94:     /// API authentication token (optional)
 95:     /// When set, all API endpoints require Authorization: Bearer <token>
 96:     /// Can also be set via VYOMA_API_TOKEN environment variable
 97:     #[arg(long)]
 98:     api_token: Option<String>,
 99:     /// Data directory containing kernel and cloud-hypervisor binaries
100:     #[arg(short, long, default_value = "/var/lib/vyoma")]
101:     data_dir: String,
102:     /// Enable chaos mode for crash injection testing
103:     #[arg(long)]
104:     chaos_mode: bool,
105:     /// Do not drop privileges (keep running as root). For development only.
106:     #[arg(long)]
107:     keep_root: bool,
108:     /// Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins (defaults to the daemon's own HTTP URL).
109:     /// Use "*" to allow all origins (not recommended for production).
110:     #[arg(long)]
111:     cors_origins: Option<String>,
112: }
113: #[tokio::main]
114: async fn main() {
115:     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();
116:     // Parse Args (Handles --help / --version)
117:     let args = Args::parse();
118:     // Check critical dependencies before starting
119:     if let Err(e) = check_dependencies(&args.data_dir) {
120:         error!("Missing dependencies: {}", e);
121:         error!("Please install the required dependencies and try again.");
122:         std::process::exit(1);
123:     }
124:     // Root requirement stripped in favor of AmbientCapabilities (ADR-022)
125:     info!("vyomad (Vyoma Daemon) starting up (Unix Socket: {})...", args.socket_path);
126:     #[cfg(feature = "chaos")]
127:     {
128:         if args.chaos_mode {
129:             chaos::enable_chaos_on_startup(std::path::Path::new(&args.data_dir));
130:             info!("Chaos mode enabled!");
131:         }
132:     }
133:     let cgroups = CgroupManager::new();
134:     if let Err(e) = cgroups.init() {
135:         error!("Failed to init cgroups: {}", e);
136:     }
137:     let cgroups = Arc::new(cgroups);
138:     // CNI Initialization
139:     let home = dirs::home_dir().expect("No home dir");
140:     let cni_config_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("cni").join("net.d");
141:     let cni_plugin_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("cni").join("bin");
142:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&cni_config_dir).unwrap();
143:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&cni_plugin_dir).unwrap();
144:     // Create default bridge
145:     let bridge_conf = cni_config_dir.join("10-vyoma-bridge.conf");
146:     if !bridge_conf.exists() {
147:         let conf = r#"{
148:     "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
149:     "name": "vyoma-net",
150:     "type": "bridge",
151:     "bridge": "vyoma0",
152:     "isGateway": true,
153:     "ipMasq": true,
154:     "ipam": {
155:         "type": "host-local",
156:         "subnet": "172.16.0.0/24",
157:         "routes": [ { "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" } ]
158:     }
159: }"#;
160:         std::fs::write(&bridge_conf, conf).unwrap();
161:     }
162:     let cni_manager = Arc::new(vyoma_core::cni::CniManager::new(
163:         cni_plugin_dir,
164:         cni_config_dir,
165:     ));
166:     // Initialize WAL (ADR-023)
167:     let state_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("state");
168:     let (db, wal) = match Wal::open_or_create(&state_dir) {
169:         Ok((db, wal)) => {
170:             info!("WAL initialized successfully");
171:             (db, Arc::new(wal))
172:         }
173:         Err(e) => {
174:             error!("Failed to initialize WAL: {}", e);
175:             panic!("Cannot start without WAL");
176:         }
177:     };
178:     let (events_tx, _rx) = broadcast::channel(100);
179:     let timemachine = Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(timemachine::TimeMachine::new(&db)));
180:     let data_dir_path = std::path::PathBuf::from(&args.data_dir);
181:     let node_id = 1u64;
182:     let network_integration = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(Some(
183:         crate::swarm::NetworkIntegration::new(data_dir_path.clone())
184:     )));
185:     let mut swarm_raft = crate::swarm::SwarmRaft::new(node_id);
186:     let net_integration = network_integration.lock().await.as_ref().unwrap().clone();
187:     let callback = crate::swarm::create_network_callback(net_integration);
188:     swarm_raft.set_side_effect_callback(callback);
189:     let swarm_raft = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(swarm_raft));
190:     // Get API token from CLI arg or environment variable
191:     let api_token = args.api_token.clone().or_else(|| std::env::var("VYOMA_API_TOKEN").ok());
192:      let state = AppState {
193:          vms: Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(HashMap::new())),
194:          cgroups,
195:          cni_manager,
196:          events_tx,
197:          wal,
198:          data_dir: args.data_dir.clone(),
199:          swarm_raft,
200:          network_integration,
201:          timemachine,
202:          policy_manager: Arc::new(StdMutex::new(PolicyManager::new())),
203:          api_token,
204:      };
205:     // Run WAL-based crash recovery and adopt surviving VMs
206:     let recovered_vms = match Recovery::recover_on_startup(&home, &state.wal, &state).await {
207:         Ok(vms) => vms,
208:         Err(e) => {
209:             warn!("Recovery scan failed: {}", e);
210:             vec![]
211:         }
212:     };
213:     // Adopt recovered VMs into state
214:     for rvm in recovered_vms {
215:         if matches!(rvm.status, crate::state::recovery::VmRecoveryStatus::Running) {
216:             info!("Adopting recovered VM: {}", rvm.vm_id);
217:             let vm_dir = home.join(".vyoma").join("vms").join(&rvm.vm_id);
218:             let ch_socket = vm_dir.join("ch.sock").to_string_lossy().to_string();
219:             let vmm = vyoma_core::vmm::VmmManager::new(&ch_socket);
220:             let mut proxy_tasks = Vec::new();
221:             for port in &rvm.state.ports {
222:                 let task = ProxyManager::start_proxy(
223:                     port.host_port,
224:                     rvm.state.ip_address.clone(),
225:                     port.vm_port,
226:                 );
227:                 proxy_tasks.push(task);
228:             }
229:             if !rvm.state.volumes.is_empty() {
230:                 warn!("VM {} has volumes but virtiofsd cannot be restarted after daemon restart - manual remount may be required", rvm.vm_id);
231:             }
232:             let vm_instance = crate::state::VmInstance {
233:                 vmm,
234:                 id: rvm.vm_id.clone(),
235:                 status: crate::state::VmStatus::Running,
236:                 attestation_status: None,
237:                 ch_socket_path: ch_socket,
238:                 tap_name: rvm.state.tap_name.clone(),
239:                 dm_name: rvm.state.dm_name.clone(),
240:                 loop_devices: rvm.state.loop_devices.clone(),
241:                 cow_file_path: rvm.state.cow_file_path.clone(),
242:                 ip_address: rvm.state.ip_address.clone(),
243:                 proxy_tasks,
244:                 fs_managers: vec![],
245:                 vtpm_manager: None,
246:                 cgroup_path: rvm.state.cgroup_path.clone(),
247:                 netns_path: rvm.state.netns_path.clone(),
248:                 config_ports: rvm.state.ports.clone(),
249:                 config_volumes: rvm.state.volumes.clone(),
250:                 hostname: rvm.state.hostname.clone(),
251:                 labels: rvm.state.labels.clone(),
252:                 networks: rvm.state.networks.clone(),
253:                 base_image_path: rvm.state.base_image_path.clone(),
254:                 vcpu: rvm.state.vcpu,
255:                 mem_size_mib: rvm.state.mem_size_mib,
256:                 attestation_task: None,
257:             };
258:             let mut vms = state.vms.lock().await;
259:             vms.insert(rvm.vm_id, Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(vm_instance)));
260:         }
261:     }
262:     api::handlers::start_process_monitor(state.clone()).await;
263:     // Start DNS
264:     dns::start_dns_server(state.clone()).await;
265:     let shutdown_state = state.clone();
266:     // Create API routes with auth middleware
267:     let api_routes = Router::new()
268:         .route("/run", post(handlers::run_vm))
269:         .route("/pull", post(handlers::pull_image_handler))
270:         .route("/stop/:id", post(handlers::stop_vm))
271:         .route("/pause/:id", post(handlers::pause_vm))
272:         .route("/resume/:id", post(handlers::resume_vm))
273:         .route("/ps", get(handlers::list_vms))
274:         .route("/commit/:id", post(handlers::commit_vm))
275:         .route("/snapshot/:id", post(handlers::snapshot_vm))
276:         .route("/history/:id", get(handlers::history_vm))
277:         .route("/time-travel", post(handlers::time_travel_vm))
278:         .route("/restore", post(handlers::restore_vm))
279:         .route("/logs/:id", get(handlers::stream_logs))
280:         .route("/build", post(handlers::build_image))
281:         .route("/images", get(handlers::list_images_handler))
282:         .route("/volumes", get(handlers::list_volumes_handler))
283:         .route(
284:             "/networks",
285:             get(handlers::list_networks_handler).post(handlers::create_network_handler),
286:         )
287:         .route(
288:             "/networks/:name",
289:             axum::routing::delete(handlers::delete_network_handler),
290:         )
291:         .route("/events", get(handlers::events_handler))
292:         .route("/vms/:id", get(handlers::inspect_vm_handler))
293:         .route("/swarm/init", post(handlers::swarm_init_handler))
294:         .route("/swarm/join", post(handlers::swarm_join_handler))
295:         .route("/swarm/nodes", get(handlers::swarm_nodes_handler))
296:         .route("/teleport", post(handlers::teleport_handler))
297:         .route("/teleport/status/:session_id", get(handlers::teleport_status_handler))
298:         .route("/receive-teleport", post(handlers::receive_teleport_handler))
299:         .route("/adopt-teleported-vm", post(handlers::adopt_teleported_vm))
300:         .route("/policy", get(handlers::get_policy_handler).post(handlers::set_policy_handler))
301:         .route("/attest/:id", post(handlers::attest_vm_handler))
302:         .route_layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), auth::auth_middleware));
303:     // Health check and UI are public (no auth required)
304:     let public_routes = Router::new()
305:         .route("/health", get(handlers::health_check));
306:     // Build allowed CORS origins from configuration
307:     let mut allowed_origins: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
308:     let http_port = args.http_port;
309:     // Default origin from the daemon's HTTP address
310:     let default_origin = format!("http://{}:{}", args.http_bind_ip, http_port);
311:     allowed_origins.push(default_origin);
312:     // Always add localhost origins for convenience
313:     if http_port > 0 {
314:         allowed_origins.push(format!("http://localhost:{}", http_port));
315:         allowed_origins.push(format!("http://127.0.0.1:{}", http_port));
316:     }
317:     // Add user-specified origins
318:     if let Some(ref custom_origins) = args.cors_origins {
319:         for origin in custom_origins.split(',') {
320:             let origin = origin.trim().to_string();
321:             if !origin.is_empty() {
322:                 allowed_origins.push(origin);
323:             }
324:         }
325:     }
326:     // Build CORS layer
327:     let cors_layer = if allowed_origins.iter().any(|o| o == "*") {
328:         warn!("CORS is configured to allow any origin. This is insecure and should only be used in development.");
329:         CorsLayer::permissive()
330:     } else {
331:         let header_values: Vec<http::HeaderValue> = allowed_origins
332:             .iter()
333:             .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
334:             .collect();
335:         info!("CORS allowed origins: {:?}", allowed_origins);
336:         CorsLayer::new()
337:             .allow_origin(AllowOrigin::list(header_values))
338:             .allow_methods([Method::GET, Method::POST, Method::DELETE])
339:             .allow_headers(vec![
340:                 http::header::AUTHORIZATION,
341:                 http::header::CONTENT_TYPE,
342:             ])
343:     };
344:     // Combine: public routes + API routes (with auth) + UI fallback (public)
345:     let app = public_routes
346:         .merge(api_routes)
347:         .fallback(ui::ui_handler)
348:         .layer(cors_layer)
349:         .with_state(state.clone());
350:     let socket_path = args.socket_path;
351:     let path = std::path::Path::new(&socket_path);
352:     if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
353:         // Try to create directory
354:         match std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {
355:             Ok(_) => {}
356:             Err(e) => {
357:                 warn!("Cannot create directory {}: {}", parent.display(), e);
358:             }
359:         }
360:     }
361:     // Try to bind, if fails try alternative locations
362:     let actual_socket_path: String;
363:     let listener = match UnixListener::bind(&socket_path) {
364:         Ok(l) => {
365:             actual_socket_path = socket_path.clone();
366:             l
367:         }
368:         Err(_) => {
369:             // Try alternative: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR or /tmp
370:             let alt_dir = std::env::var("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR")
371:                 .map(|d| std::path::PathBuf::from(d))
372:                 .unwrap_or_else(|_| std::env::temp_dir());
373:             let alt_socket_path = alt_dir.join("vyoma.sock");
374:             warn!("Cannot bind to {}. Trying alternative: {}", socket_path, alt_socket_path.display());
375:             let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&alt_socket_path);
376:             match UnixListener::bind(&alt_socket_path) {
377:                 Ok(l) => {
378:                     actual_socket_path = alt_socket_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
379:                     warn!("Using alternative socket at {}", actual_socket_path);
380:                     l
381:                 }
382:                 Err(e) => {
383:                     error!("Failed to bind socket at any location: {}", e);
384:                     std::process::exit(1);
385:                 }
386:             }
387:         }
388:     };
389:     // Set socket permissions: root:vyoma (0660) - users in vyoma group can access
390:     let permissions = std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o660);
391:     if let Err(e) = std::fs::set_permissions(&actual_socket_path, permissions) {
392:         warn!("Could not set 0660 permissions on socket: {}", e);
393:     }
394:     info!("Daemon listening on Unix Socket {}", actual_socket_path);
395:     // Drop privileges to vyoma user (unless --keep-root is specified)
396:     if !args.keep_root {
397:         match privdrop::drop_privileges() {
398:             Ok(()) => info!("Privileges dropped successfully, running as user 'vyoma'"),
399:             Err(e) => {
400:                 error!("Failed to drop privileges: {}", e);
401:                 std::process::exit(1);
402:             }
403:         }
404:     } else {
405:         info!("Running as root (--keep-root specified)");
406:     }
407:     // Start HTTP server for dashboard if port is not 0
408:     if args.http_port > 0 {
409:         let http_app = app.clone();
410:         let api_token = args.api_token.clone();
411:         tokio::spawn(async move {
412:             let addr = format!("{}:{}", args.http_bind_ip, args.http_port);
413:             // Warn if binding to non-localhost address
414:             if !args.http_bind_ip.starts_with("127.") && args.http_bind_ip != "::1" && args.http_bind_ip != "localhost" {
415:                 if api_token.is_none() {
416:                     warn!("HTTP server bound on non-localhost address {} WITHOUT authentication! Use --api-token for security", addr);
417:                 } else {
418:                     warn!("HTTP server bound on non-localhost address {} with authentication enabled", addr);
419:                 }
420:             }
421:             info!("Dashboard available at http://{}", addr);
422:             let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(&addr).await.unwrap();
423:             loop {
424:                 match listener.accept().await {
425:                     Ok((stream, _)) => {
426:                         let io = TokioIo::new(stream);
427:                         let tower_service = http_app.clone();
428:                         tokio::spawn(async move {
429:                             let hyper_service = hyper::service::service_fn(move |request| {
430:                                 tower_service.clone().call(request)
431:                             });
432:                             let _ = auto::Builder::new(TokioExecutor::new())
433:                                 .serve_connection(io, hyper_service)
434:                                 .await;
435:                         });
436:                     }
437:                     Err(e) => error!("HTTP accept error: {}", e),
438:                 }
439:             }
440:         });
441:     }
442:     let grpc_state = Arc::new(state);
443:     tokio::spawn(async move {
444:         let addr = "[::1]:7071".parse().unwrap();
445:         info!("gRPC interface available at {}", addr);
446:         use vyoma_proto::v1::vm_service_server::VmServiceServer;
447:         let svc = VmServiceServer::new(grpc::GrpcVmService::new(grpc_state.clone()));
448:         if let Err(e) = tonic::transport::Server::builder()
449:             .add_service(svc)
450:             .serve(addr)
451:             .await
452:         {
453:             error!("gRPC server error: {}", e);
454:         }
455:     });
456:     let shutdown_rx = handlers::shutdown_signal(shutdown_state);
457:     let mut shutdown_flag = Box::pin(shutdown_rx);
458:     loop {
459:         tokio::select! {
460:             result = listener.accept() => {
461:                 match result {
462:                     Ok((stream, _)) => {
463:                         let io = TokioIo::new(stream);
464:                         let tower_service = app.clone();
465:                         tokio::spawn(async move {
466:                             let hyper_service = hyper::service::service_fn(move |request: axum::extract::Request<hyper::body::Incoming>| {
467:                                 tower_service.clone().call(request)
468:                             });
469:                             if let Err(err) = auto::Builder::new(TokioExecutor::new())
470:                                 .serve_connection(io, hyper_service)
471:                                 .await
472:                             {
473:                                 error!("Error parsing connection: {:?}", err);
474:                             }
475:                         });
476:                     }
477:                     Err(e) => error!("Failed to accept connection: {}", e),
478:                 }
479:             }
480:             _ = &mut shutdown_flag => {
481:                 info!("Daemon shutting down gracefully...");
482:                 break;
483:             }
484:         }
485:     }
486:     let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&socket_path);
487: }





================================================================
End of Codebase
================================================================
